nodes.label: A priori bounds
nodes.summary: Uniform lower bounds on the moduli of fundamental annuli of all renormalizations of an infinitely renormalizable map.

evidence.quote: moduli are bounded away from zero

nodes.label: Abel-Jacobi map for marked curves
nodes.summary: The morphism that associates to a marked curve and weights the corresponding degree-zero line bundle, such as O_C(sum_i a_i p_i), viewed in a Picard stack or relative Picard space. The double ramification condition is the triviality condition on this Abel-Jacobi line bundle.

evidence.quote: For a classical perspective from the point of view of Abel-Jacobi theory
The image of the Abel-Jacobi map
evidence.section: 3.5. The image of the Abel-Jacobi map

nodes.label: Abelian surface
nodes.summary: A two-dimensional abelian variety. The paper uses moduli spaces of sheaves on abelian surfaces to study the topology of Hitchin fibrations.

evidence.quote: We study the topology of Hitchin fibrations via abelian surfaces.

nodes.label: Absence of infinitesimal automorphisms of a pointed curve
nodes.summary: The vanishing H^0(C,T_C(-D))=0 for a pointed curve (C,D), equivalent in the excerpt to hyperbolicity of the pointed curve.

evidence.quote: it has no infinitesimal automorphisms, i.e., H^0 (C,T_C(-D)) = 0

nodes.label: Absolute cohomology of a translation surface
nodes.summary: Absolute cohomology H^1(X,C) is the ordinary first cohomology of the underlying Riemann surface X. The rank of an invariant subvariety is defined using the image of its tangent space in this cohomology.

evidence.quote: $H^1 (X,\mathbb{C})$

nodes.label: Adjoint trace field of a lattice
nodes.summary: The field over Q generated by traces of Ad(γ) for γ in Γ. For complex hyperbolic lattices in PU(1,n), n>1, the paper proves this field is totally real and uses it to define a K-form of G.

evidence.quote: field generated over Q

nodes.label: Admissible eigenvalue cone
nodes.summary: An open cone \Gamma of allowable eigenvalue vectors, lying in the positive-trace half-space, having vertex at the origin, and containing the positive orthant.

evidence.quote: an open cone \Gamma \subset \{\lambda : \lambda_1 + \ldots + \lambda_n > 0\} with vertex at the origin and containing the first octant

nodes.label: Admissible Hermitian-Yang-Mills connection
nodes.summary: A connection on the locally free locus of a reflexive sheaf that is the Chern connection of a smooth HYM metric and whose curvature has finite L2 norm on compact subsets.

evidence.quote: This means that A is the Chern connection
curvature  $F_A$  has finite  $L^2$  norm

nodes.label: Admissible residue set of an Apollonian packing
nodes.summary: The set R(A) of residue classes modulo 24 represented by curvatures in a primitive integral Apollonian packing. It is called the admissible set of the packing.

evidence.quote: called the admissible set

nodes.label: Affine log Calabi-Yau variety containing an open torus
aliases: smooth affine log Calabi-Yau with open torus
nodes.summary: A connected smooth affine log Calabi-Yau variety U equipped with a nowhere-vanishing volume form and containing a Zariski open split algebraic torus T_M. In this paper this is the input class for the Frobenius structure theorem.

evidence.quote: affine log Calabi-Yau variety containing an open algebraic torus

nodes.label: ALE gravitational instanton end
nodes.summary: The asymptotically locally Euclidean standard end family for gravitational instantons, characterized in the introduction by volume growth of order 4.

evidence.quote: ALE, ALF, ALG, and ALH, where the volume growth is of order 4, 3, 2, and 1

nodes.label: Alexandrov maximum principle
nodes.summary: A maximum principle bounding the interior excess of a function by an Ld norm of the negative part of an elliptic inequality. The paper applies it to Delta_phi after rewriting the determinant factor in terms of F.

evidence.quote: Alexandroff maximum principle
evidence.section: 5. Entropy bound of the volume ratio and C0 bound of Kähler potential

nodes.label: ALF curvature decay of order three
nodes.summary: A sharpened curvature decay property for ALF gravitational instantons, improving the initial faster-than-quadratic assumption to order r^{-3}.

evidence.quote: improve the curvature decay rate of an ALF instanton to O(r^{-3})

nodes.label: ALF-D_k gravitational instanton
nodes.summary: A D_k-type ALF gravitational instanton. The paper discusses its twistor space and a map to the total space of the O(4) line bundle over CP^1.

evidence.quote: Twistor space of ALF-D_k instantons

nodes.label: ALF gravitational instanton end
nodes.summary: The asymptotically locally flat standard end family for gravitational instantons, characterized in the introduction by volume growth of order 3.

evidence.quote: ALE, ALF, ALG, and ALH, where the volume growth is of order 4, 3, 2, and 1

nodes.label: Complex analytic compactification of ALG and ALH non-splitting instantons
aliases: compactification of ALG and ALH non-splitting instantons
nodes.summary: The paper's compactification result for gravitational instantons in the ALG and ALH non-splitting cases, verifying the complex analytic compactifiability predicted by Yau's conjecture in this setting.

evidence.quote: in both the ALG and ALH non-splitting cases, we can prove Yau's conjecture
Compactification of ALG and ALH non-splitting instantons

nodes.label: ALG gravitational instanton end
nodes.summary: The ALG standard end family for gravitational instantons, characterized in the introduction by volume growth of order 2 and treated as one of the paper's main non-ALE cases.

evidence.quote: Our new contribution lies in the ALG and ALH cases
where the volume growth is of order 4, 3, 2, and 1

nodes.label: Algebra of reflexive forms
nodes.summary: The graded algebra of global reflexive differential forms on a normal variety or its quasi-étale cover. Generation properties of this algebra define the irreducible Calabi-Yau and holomorphic symplectic factors.

evidence.quote: Omega^{[bullet]}

nodes.label: Algebraic solution of an isomonodromy equation
nodes.summary: A solution of an isomonodromic deformation equation that is algebraic in the relevant deformation parameters. The paper relates these solutions to semisimple MCG-finite representations.

evidence.quote: algebraic solutions to a certain isomonodromy differential equation

nodes.label: Algebraic monodromy group
nodes.summary: For an irreducible subvariety Y, the algebraic monodromy group is the identity component of the Zariski closure in GL(V_Q) of the monodromy of the pulled-back local system on the normalization of Y.

evidence.quote: The algebraic monodromy group HY of Y for VZ is the ... identity component of the Zariski-closure in GL(VQ) of the monodromy of the restriction to Ynor

nodes.label: Algebraic solution to the Painleve VI equation
nodes.summary: An algebraic solution of the Painleve VI equation. In appropriate coordinates, these correspond to MCG-finite rank-two trivial-determinant representations for the four-punctured sphere.

evidence.quote: Algebraic solutions to the Painlevé VI equation in an appropriate choice of coordinates correspond to MCG-finite representations with trivial determinant

nodes.label: Algebraic tangent cone of a reflexive sheaf
nodes.summary: A torsion-free sheaf on the exceptional divisor obtained by restricting a reflexive extension of the pullback of the original reflexive sheaf over the punctured ball to the blowup divisor.

evidence.quote: notion of an algebraic tangent cone
defined to be a torsion-free sheaf

nodes.label: Algebraicity of dynamical degrees for affine 3-space automorphisms
aliases: algebraic dynamical degrees of affine 3-space automorphisms
nodes.summary: The property that all dynamical degrees of a polynomial automorphism of affine 3-space are algebraic numbers, obtained as an application of the paper's spectral interpretation and b-divisor techniques.

evidence.quote: dynamical degrees of an automorphism of the affine 3-space are all algebraic numbers

nodes.label: Algebraicity of the dimension-bounded F^i-locus
nodes.summary: For a polarized ZVHS and d>0, the dimension-bounded non-trivial F^i-class locus V^i_{>=d} and its projection to the base are closed algebraic subvarieties.

evidence.quote: For any i ∈ Z and any d ∈ N*, the subset Vi≥d ⊂ FiV is a closed algebraic subvariety of V. Its projection Si(V)≥d is a closed algebraic subvariety of S.
evidence.section: 1.4.2 A global algebraicity result for the locus of classes of F^i-type

nodes.label: Algebro-geometric two-step degeneration at infinity
nodes.summary: The degeneration procedure that relates the complete Calabi-Yau manifold to its asymptotic cone through the weighted asymptotic cone W. It compares metric scaling on X with algebraic scaling on the affine model X'.

evidence.quote: algebro-geometric 2-step degeneration from X to C

nodes.label: ALH exponential convergence to the flat model
aliases: exponential decay of curvature of ALH instantons
exponential convergence to the flat model
nodes.summary: For ALH non-splitting instantons, the metric converges exponentially to the flat model; equivalently, the curvature decay improves to exponential behavior in the paper's setting.

evidence.quote: the metric must converge to the flat model exponentially
Exponential decay of curvature of ALH instantons

nodes.label: ALH gravitational instanton end
nodes.summary: The ALH standard end family for gravitational instantons, characterized in the introduction by volume growth of order 1 and treated as one of the paper's main non-ALE cases.

evidence.quote: Our new contribution lies in the ALG and ALH cases
where the volume growth is of order 4, 3, 2, and 1

nodes.label: ALH non-splitting instanton
nodes.summary: An ALH gravitational instanton whose end is not in the splitting/product case. The paper singles these out for exponential convergence and compactification results.

evidence.quote: For ALH non-splitting instantons
ALG and ALH non-splitting instantons

nodes.label: Almost complex manifold
nodes.summary: A smooth even-dimensional manifold equipped with an almost complex structure J, serving as the target of J-holomorphic curves.

evidence.quote: perturbed almost complex structures

nodes.label: Almost parabolic renormalization
nodes.summary: A perturbative parabolic renormalization obtained from a return germ built by composing a cylinder horn map with a transit map; it approximates true parabolic renormalization.

evidence.quote: almost parabolic renormalization

nodes.label: Ample Griffiths bundle on a period-map image
aliases: ampleness of the restricted Griffiths bundle
nodes.summary: For the algebraic image of a period map, the restricted Griffiths rational line bundle is the analytification of an ample algebraic rational line bundle.

evidence.quote: the Griffiths  $\mathbb{Q}$ -bundle  $L$  restricted to  $Y$  is the analytification of an ample algebraic  $\mathbb{Q}$ -bundle

nodes.label: Ample numerical class
nodes.summary: The numerical class of an ample line bundle or divisor, used as a polarization to measure degrees of rational maps.

evidence.quote: with respect to a given ample class  $\omega$

nodes.label: Ample Q-line bundle
nodes.summary: A rational line bundle L whose positive multiple is an ample line bundle. It determines the Kähler cohomology class c1(L) containing the current in the twisted Kähler-Einstein equation.

evidence.quote: L an ample Q-line bundle on X

nodes.label: Analytic blow-up cycle
nodes.summary: A codimension-two algebraic cycle on CP^{n-1} whose support cones to the pure codimension-two part of the bubbling and sheaf-singularity tangent set, with coefficients given by analytic Yang-Mills energy multiplicities.

evidence.quote: called the analytic blow-up cycle
affine cone over the support

nodes.label: Analytic moduli stack of pointed curves
aliases: analytic moduli stack of n-pointed curves
nodes.summary: The analytic moduli stack parameterizing smooth n-pointed curves of genus g, used as the space on which analytic very generality is evaluated.

evidence.quote: M_{g,n} is the analytic moduli stack of n-pointed curves of genus g

nodes.label: Analytic multiplicity of Yang-Mills energy
nodes.summary: A positive integer attached to a generic codimension-two bubbling component, measuring transverse blow-up of Yang-Mills energy. These multiplicities are the coefficients of the analytic blow-up cycle.

evidence.quote: analytic multiplicity
blow-up of Yang-Mills energy

nodes.label: Analytic tangent cone of an admissible HYM connection
nodes.summary: A subsequential gauge limit of dilated admissible HYM connections at a point, together with its analytic blow-up cycle. It is represented by the pair consisting of the limiting HYM cone connection and the codimension-two energy cycle.

evidence.quote: We call the pair
an analytic tangent cone

nodes.label: Analytically very general point
nodes.summary: A property holds at an analytically very general point of a complex orbifold if, locally, it holds outside a countable union of nowhere dense closed analytic subsets.

evidence.quote: there exists a countable collection of nowhere dense closed analytic subsets such that the property holds on the complement of their union

nodes.label: Analytically very general pointed curve
nodes.summary: An n-pointed curve corresponding to an analytically very general point of the analytic moduli stack M_{g,n}.

evidence.quote: a property holds for an analytically (very) general n-pointed curve if it holds for an analytically (very) general point of M_{g,n}

nodes.label: Analytification of a complex algebraic space
nodes.summary: The complex analytic space obtained from a finite-type algebraic space over the complex numbers by taking its complex points with the natural analytic structure.

evidence.quote: $X^{\mathrm{an}}$  is the analytification of  $X$ , that is,  $X(\mathbb{C})$  endowed with its natural structure as a complex analytic manifold

nodes.label: Anosov representation
nodes.summary: A representation with Anosov dynamical boundary behavior; in this paper, equivariant quasisymmetric maps to the Einstein Universe are linked to Anosov representations and rigidity applications.

evidence.quote: equivariant ones are linked to Anosov representations
applications of these results to asymptotically hyperbolic surfaces, rigidity of Anosov representations

nodes.label: Apollonian circle packing
nodes.summary: A fractal circle packing obtained by repeatedly adjoining the two Apollonius solutions tangent to triples of mutually tangent circles. In the arithmetic formulation it is generated by a Descartes quadruple.

evidence.quote: one obtains a fractal called an Apollonian circle packing

nodes.label: Apollonian group
nodes.summary: The thin group acting on Descartes quadruples whose orbit consists of all Descartes quadruples in a fixed Apollonian packing. The paper's obstructions are properties of this orbit rather than of the larger Zariski closure.

evidence.quote: orbit of the Apollonian group

nodes.label: Apollonian local-global principle
nodes.summary: The expected property that congruence-admissible curvatures in a primitive integral Apollonian packing should all occur up to finitely many exceptions. The paper proves this property fails for many packings.

evidence.quote: number of missing curvatures in A is finite
evidence.section: 1.2. The set of curvatures of an Apollonian circle packing

nodes.label: Apollonian packing type
nodes.summary: The pair (x,k) attached to the admissible residue set R(A), where x is the cardinality of R(A) and k is the smallest positive residue in R(A) coprime to 24.

evidence.quote: type (x, k) denotes

nodes.label: Approximately hyperkähler triple
nodes.summary: A glued triple of closed 2-forms on the assembled 4-manifold whose normalized matrix Q is uniformly close to the identity, intended to be perturbed to an exact hyperkähler triple.

evidence.quote: glued triple of closed 2-forms ω
very close to being a hyperkähler triple

nodes.label: Arbarello-Cornalba inductive method
aliases: inductive method for cohomology of stable-curve moduli
nodes.summary: An inductive method for computing rational singular cohomology of moduli spaces of stable curves using boundary stratification and Deligne's weight filtration.

evidence.quote: introduced an inductive method for computing the rational singular cohomology groups
using the combinatorial stratification of the boundary and basic properties of Deligne's weight filtration

nodes.label: Area Siegel-Veech constant
aliases: area Siegel-Veech constant of a stratum
nodes.summary: An invariant of a stratum governing quadratic counting asymptotics for flat-surface configurations, with cylinder contributions weighted by area and averaged over the Masur-Veech measure.

evidence.quote: area Siegel-Veech constants associated with principal strata

nodes.label: Arithmetic lattice
nodes.summary: A subgroup Γ of a semisimple real group G that is commensurable with the image of integral points of a semisimple algebraic Q-group under a real morphism with compact kernel.

evidence.quote: Γ lies in the commensurability class

nodes.label: Arithmetic lattice in a Mumford-Tate group
nodes.summary: An arithmetic subgroup of the rational points of the generic Mumford-Tate group, chosen to contain the monodromy representation of the variation of Hodge structure.

evidence.quote: $\Gamma \subset \mathbf{G}(\mathbb{Q})$  an arithmetic lattice containing the image of the monodromy representation

nodes.label: Arithmetic linear representation
nodes.summary: A linear representation considered with arithmetic structure, such as a number-field or p-adic realization and associated integrality or geometric constraints. The paper has consequences for such representations.

evidence.quote: Consequences for arithmetic representations

nodes.label: Arithmetic quotient of a period domain
nodes.summary: The complex analytic quotient of a polarized period domain by an arithmetic lattice; it parametrizes pure Hodge structures up to integral framing in that lattice.

evidence.quote: the quotient  $\Gamma \backslash \Omega$  parameterizes pure Hodge structures up to integral framing in  $\Gamma$

nodes.label: Arithmeticity via special correspondences
nodes.summary: For a complex hyperbolic lattice in PU(1,n), arithmeticity is equivalent to the ball quotient admitting infinitely many special correspondences. This is the paper's Hodge-theoretic version of the commensurability criterion.

evidence.quote: admits infinitely many Γ-special correspondences

nodes.label: Artin algebraization
nodes.summary: A family of algebraization results used to pass from formal or analytic data satisfying suitable conditions to algebraic spaces or morphisms.

evidence.quote: combine this with algebraization theorems of Artin

nodes.label: Asymmetric simple random walk
nodes.summary: A nearest-neighbor random walk with unequal jump probabilities in the two directions. The paper compares coefficients in the Virasoro recursions with such jump probabilities.

evidence.quote: the jump probabilities of a certain asymmetric simple random walk

nodes.label: Asymptotic Calabi-Yau cone at infinity
nodes.summary: The unique Calabi-Yau cone obtained as the pointed Gromov-Hausdorff limit of rescalings of the complete Calabi-Yau manifold at infinity. It is the final cone C in the algebraic degeneration.

evidence.quote: asymptotic to a unique Calabi-Yau cone C at infinity

nodes.label: Asymptotic contradiction framework
nodes.summary: A proof framework that assumes a sequence of normalized counterexamples with degrees n tending to infinity and derives a contradiction from limiting structure.

evidence.quote: it is convenient to rephrase the above theorem in the following equivalent asymptotic contradiction form

nodes.label: Ambient-sequence asymptotic notation
nodes.summary: The paper lets n tend to infinity along an ambient sequence, allows subsequences, and interprets o(1), O(1), and fixed quantities relative to that sequence.

evidence.quote: Throughout this paper we assume that is a parameter ranging in some sequence of natural numbers tending to infinity, which we will call the 'ambient sequence'.

nodes.label: Asymptotic period expansion from a resolution
nodes.summary: A method expressing period integrals as sums of powers t^{sigma_{i,nu}(omega)-1} times integrals of forms R_{i,nu}(omega) on pieces of exceptional divisors. It connects resolution data with candidate b-exponents.

evidence.quote: construct the asymptotic expansion ... from a resolution
evidence.section: The asymptotic expansion of the periods

nodes.label: Asymptotic Riemannian fibration
nodes.summary: A Riemannian fibration structure on the end of a gravitational instanton, produced from the analysis of short geodesic loops, local covers, holonomy, and tangent cones.

evidence.quote: Asymptotic fibration
From geodesic loops to Riemannian fibration

nodes.label: Asymptotic to a standard metric of order epsilon
aliases: standard metric asymptotic of order epsilon
nodes.summary: The property that the metric on a gravitational instanton's end converges to the appropriate standard model with asymptotic control of order epsilon.

evidence.quote: must be asymptotic to the standard metric of order epsilon

nodes.label: Asymptotic volume growth order
nodes.summary: The power-law order governing growth of volumes of large metric balls. The paper uses orders 4, 3, 2, and 1 to distinguish ALE, ALF, ALG, and ALH families.

evidence.quote: According to the volume growth rate, they can be divided into four categories
where the volume growth is of order 4, 3, 2, and 1

nodes.label: Asymptotically Calabi manifold
nodes.summary: A complete Riemannian 4-manifold that is identified outside a compact set with a Calabi model space, with metric derivatives decaying at an exponential rate specified by delta.

evidence.quote: is said to be δ-asymptotically Calabi
there exist a compact subset K ... and a Calabi model space

nodes.label: Asymptotically hyperbolic surface
nodes.summary: A surface whose geometry approaches hyperbolic behavior near infinity. The paper applies its quasisymmetric-boundary results to smooth spacelike curves and asymptotic hyperbolicity.

evidence.quote: applications of these results to asymptotically hyperbolic surfaces
8. Smooth spacelike curves and asymptotic hyperbolicity

nodes.label: Atypical Hodge locus
nodes.summary: The union inside the Hodge locus of all atypical special subvarieties of the base for the variation.

evidence.quote: The atypical Hodge locus ... is the union of the atypical ... special subvarieties of $S$ for ${\mathbb{V}}$
evidence.section: 1.3 Special subvarieties as (a)typical intersection loci and conjectures

nodes.label: Atypical special subvariety for a variation
nodes.summary: A special subvariety that is either contained in the singular locus of the period image or whose period image intersects its Hodge subvariety with excess dimension, equivalently with smaller-than-expected Hodge codimension.

evidence.quote: It is said to be atypical if either $Z$ is singular for ${\mathbb{V}}$, or if $\Phi(S^{\textnormal{an}})$ and $\Gamma^{\prime}\backslash D^{\prime}$ do not intersect generically along $\Phi(Z)$
evidence.section: 1.3 Special subvarieties as (a)typical intersection loci and conjectures

nodes.label: Aubin I functional
nodes.summary: A scalar functional on Kähler potentials used here to fix the additive constant by imposing I(ϕ)=0. Its continuous extension defines the normalized finite-energy space E^1_0.

evidence.quote: I(ϕ)
I(u)
evidence.section: 6. Existence of cscK and geodesic stability

nodes.label: Augmented irregularity
nodes.summary: The supremal irregularity obtained after passing to finite quasi-étale covers. Vanishing augmented irregularity is used to isolate the non-torus part of the decomposition.

evidence.quote: augmented irregularity

nodes.label: Auxiliary complex Monge-Ampère equation
nodes.summary: A normalized complex Monge-Ampère equation for an auxiliary potential \psi, with right hand side built from the original potential and density; it supplies the comparison potential in the PDE proof.

evidence.quote: Let us solve the following auxiliary Monge-Ampère equation with \sup \psi = 0
(\omega_ {X} + i \partial \bar {\partial} \psi) ^ {n} = \frac {(- \varphi) ^ {p} e ^ {n F}}{E _ {p} (\varphi)} \omega_ {X} ^ {n}

nodes.label: Auxiliary entropy Monge-Ampere potential
nodes.summary: A normalized Kähler potential psi solving a Monge-Ampere equation with density proportional to e^F Phi(F). It is introduced to compare the entropy density with Tian alpha-invariant estimates.

evidence.quote: \det(g_{i\bar{j}}+\psi_{i\bar{j}})=\frac{e^{F}\Phi(F)\det(g_{i\bar{j}})}{\int_{M}e^{F}\Phi(F)dvol_{g}}
evidence.section: 5. Entropy bound of the volume ratio and C0 bound of Kähler potential

nodes.label: Auxiliary Monge-Ampère comparison method
nodes.summary: A PDE method that compares the given fully nonlinear equation with a carefully chosen auxiliary complex Monge-Ampère equation and applies a maximum-principle test function to derive L^\infty or integrability bounds.

evidence.quote: to compare the given equation with an auxiliary complex Monge-Ampère equation
A key novelty in our paper resides in the choice of auxiliary complex Monge-Ampère equation, as well as of the test function \Phi for the comparison.
by the same maximum argument as in Lemma 1, we obtain the inequality

nodes.label: Ax-Lindemann functional transcendence for Hodge variations
nodes.summary: A functional transcendence principle: the Zariski closure of the projection of an algebraic subvariety of the universal cover, for the bi-algebraic structure of a Hodge variation, is bi-algebraic and hence weakly special.

evidence.quote: Let Y ⊂ S~ be a closed algebraic subvariety for the bi-algebraic structure defined by V. Then π(Y)Zar is a bi-algebraic subvariety of S, i.e. a weakly special subvariety of S for V.

nodes.label: Ax-Schanuel for variations of Hodge structure
nodes.summary: A functional transcendence theorem for period maps: atypically large algebraic intersections between a subvariety of S times a period domain and the graph of the lifted period map force containment in a strict weakly special subvariety.

evidence.quote: Then the projection of $U$ to $S$ is contained in a strict weakly special subvariety of $S$ for ${\mathbb{V}}$

nodes.label: Double ramification class in b-Chow
nodes.summary: A refinement of the double ramification cycle considered in b-Chow, allowing the class to be tracked through birational or logarithmic modifications of the moduli space.

evidence.quote: The double ramification cycle in b-Chow
evidence.section: 3.8. The double ramification cycle in b-Chow

nodes.label: b-divisor class associated with a fractional ideal
nodes.summary: The construction assigning to a fractional ideal sheaf a numerical b-divisor class, with coefficients measured by valuations on birational models.

evidence.quote: Fractional ideal sheaves and  $b$ -divisor classes

nodes.label: b-exponents of an isolated singularity
nodes.summary: The roots of the characteristic polynomial of the endomorphism partial_t t on the quotient of the saturated Brieskorn lattice by t. They differ by sign convention from roots of the Bernstein-Sato polynomial when the minimal and characteristic polynomials coincide.

evidence.quote: roots of the characteristic polynomial ... are called the b-exponents

nodes.label: Background Kahler forms asymptotic to a cone
aliases: background Kahler metrics on approximating varieties
nodes.summary: The construction of controlled background Kahler forms on approximating crepant resolutions, asymptotic to the target cone and approximately solving the Calabi-Yau Monge-Ampere equation at infinity. These forms supply the analytic input for the adapted Tian-Yau construction.

evidence.quote: construct a family of background Kähler metrics ... asymptotic to the cone

nodes.label: Balayage to a circle
nodes.summary: Balayage maps a measure or random variable inside a disk to its harmonic-measure distribution on the boundary circle, computed via the Poisson kernel.

evidence.quote: define the balayage by the formula ... probability density of the first location where a Brownian motion originating at exits the disk

nodes.label: Ball quotient
nodes.summary: A quotient Γ\B^n of the complex unit ball by a torsion-free discrete subgroup of its automorphism group. In the paper ball quotients are the locally symmetric spaces for which precise boundary asymptotics of cone metrics are obtained.

evidence.quote: toroidal compactifications of ball quotients X° = Γ \ B^n
where Γ ⊂ Aut(Bn) is a torsion-free, discrete subgroup
ball quotient S_Γ=Γ\X

nodes.label: Barbot crown
nodes.summary: A geometric configuration associated with semi-positive loops in the Einstein Universe and used in the paper's discussion of Barbot surfaces and boundary geometry.

evidence.quote: 2.6. Semi-positive loops and Barbot crowns
4.6.Barbot surfaces

nodes.label: Barbot surface
nodes.summary: A homogeneous complete maximal surface in pseudo-hyperbolic space that is flat and arises as an orbit of a diagonal subgroup of the isometry group. Barbot surfaces are characterized by curvature rigidity in the paper.

evidence.quote: Barbot surfaces which are flat and orbits of diagonal subgroup of  $\mathsf{G}$
Barbot surfaces are characterised by a rigidity theorem

nodes.label: Base-point free cycle class
nodes.summary: A positivity notion for higher-codimension numerical cycle classes, used as the higher-codimension substitute for nef classes and as the positive cone behind the BPF norm.

evidence.quote: base-point free (BPF) classes
substitute for nef classes in higher codimensions

nodes.label: Beauville-Bogomolov form
nodes.summary: The canonical quadratic form on the second cohomology of a hyper-Kähler manifold, used to express isotropy and top intersection relations.

evidence.quote: isotropic with respect to the Beauville-Bogomolov form q_X

nodes.label: Behrend-Grothendieck-Lefschetz trace formula for algebraic stacks
aliases: Lefschetz trace formula for algebraic stacks
nodes.summary: For a Deligne-Mumford stack over a finite field, this formula expresses the stacky finite-field point count as the graded trace of geometric Frobenius on compactly supported ell-adic etale cohomology.

evidence.quote: $\# \mathfrak {X} (\mathbb {F} _ {q}) = \operatorname {T r} (F _ {q} \mid H _ {c, \acute {e} t} ^ {\bullet}

nodes.label: Berkovich analytification
nodes.summary: For an algebraic variety over a non-archimedean field, the Berkovich analytification is the associated analytic space whose points encode scheme-theoretic points together with compatible absolute values. The paper uses it as the ambient space for analytic disks, curves, skeletons, and retractions.

evidence.quote: Xan consists of pairs (ξ, ν)

nodes.label: Bernstein-Sato quotient module
nodes.summary: The cyclic left D_X[s]-module quotient generated by the class of f^s modulo the shifted submodule generated by f^{s+a}. Its parameter annihilator is the Bernstein-Sato type ideal, making it the D-module model for the zero locus.

evidence.quote: M is a cyclic D_X[s]-module generated by the class of f^s
D_X[s]f^s / D_X[s]f^{s+a}

nodes.label: Bernstein-Sato slope set
nodes.summary: The set of primitive nonnegative integer slope vectors L occurring in hyperplanes L.s+b=0 that arise from supports of the relevant quotient modules. These slopes control codimension-one components of Bernstein-Sato zero loci.

evidence.quote: sets of primitive slopes L=(l_1,...,l_r)
hyperplanes of the form L.s + b = 0

nodes.label: Bernstein-Sato ideal of type a
nodes.summary: For a tuple F=(f_1,...,f_r) and a multi-index a in N^r, B_F^a is the ideal of polynomials b(s) such that b(s) f^s equals P f^{s+a} for some differential operator P in D_X[s]. It includes the usual Bernstein-Sato ideal as the case a=(1,...,1).

evidence.quote: One defines an ideal of Bernstein-Sato type
b prod f_i^{s_i} = P prod f_i^{s_i+a_i}

nodes.label: Beta invariant of a valuation
nodes.summary: The valuation invariant beta(v)=A_(Z,Q)(v)-S_L(v). The paper records its transformation under torus twists and uses it to express semistability consequences.

evidence.quote: beta(v):=A(v)-S_L(v)

nodes.label: Betti numbers
nodes.summary: The ranks of the cohomology groups of a topological space. The paper uses them among the topological invariants forced to match K3[2] fourfolds.

evidence.quote: forces the Betti numbers, the Fujiki constant, and the Huybrechts-Riemann-Roch polynomial
such isotropic classes exist when b_2(X) >= 5

nodes.label: Bi-algebraic structure defined by a variation of Hodge structure
aliases: bi-algebraic structure on S defined by V
nodes.summary: The bi-algebraic structure attached to a polarized ZVHS is the developing map from the universal cover to the compact dual of the Mumford-Tate domain together with the holonomy representation into Gamma.

evidence.quote: The bi-algebraic structure on S defined by V is the pair (ΦˆS : S~ → Dˆ, ρS := (ΦS)∗ : π1(S) → Γ ⊂ G(C)).

nodes.label: Bi-algebraic subvariety
nodes.summary: In a bi-algebraic structure, a closed irreducible algebraic subvariety of S is bi-algebraic when analytic irreducible components of its inverse image in the universal cover are algebraic for the developing map.

evidence.quote: W ⊂ S, is said to be bi-algebraic if ... any (equivalently one) analytic irreducible component of π−1(W) is a closed irreducible algebraic subvariety of S~.

nodes.label: Biholomorphic local pi-spray
nodes.summary: A local pi-spray that is biholomorphic from a neighborhood of the zero section onto its image. The proof uses such sprays to pass from local models to dominating global sprays.

evidence.quote: biholomorphic local pi-spray
evidence.section: 2. Dominating sprays and the Oka property

nodes.label: Birational model over a variety
nodes.summary: A projective birational morphism from a normal variety to the fixed base variety. Numerical classes across all such models are organized into Weil and Cartier b-classes.

evidence.quote: intersection theory on birational models over  $X$

nodes.label: Bishop-Gromov volume monotonicity
nodes.summary: The monotonicity of volume ratios under suitable Ricci curvature bounds. The paper applies it to the constructed metric on W to force equality of volume densities and hence a metric cone structure.

evidence.quote: Bishop-Gromov inequality ... volume ratio is a non-increasing function

nodes.label: Black Hole criterion
nodes.summary: A finite-time probabilistic criterion: for primitive stationary Feigenbaum maps with a priori bounds, sufficiently large landing probability relative to escaping probability forces positive-area Julia set.

evidence.quote: If η ≥ Cξ then area J(f) > 0

nodes.label: Black hole Feigenbaum map
nodes.summary: A Feigenbaum map whose Julia set has positive Lebesgue measure; it is one branch of the Avila-Lyubich lean/balanced/black-hole trichotomy.

evidence.quote: Black hole case: area J(f) > 0

nodes.label: Black-hole parameter Cantor set
nodes.summary: A Cantor set of quadratic parameters obtained from black-hole renormalization combinatorics whose Hausdorff dimension is positive, and in the construction at least 1/2.

evidence.quote: Cantor set has bounded geometry

nodes.label: Blowup at a point
nodes.summary: The birational modification replacing a point by the projectivized set of tangent directions. It is used to define algebraic tangent cones of sheaves at the origin.

evidence.quote: blowup at 0

nodes.label: Boundary of an invariant subvariety
aliases: boundary of an affine invariant submanifold
nodes.summary: The boundary of an invariant subvariety consists of limiting translation surfaces obtained by degenerations. Components of this boundary are used inductively in the classification.

evidence.quote: a component $\mathcal{M}'$ in the boundary of $\mathcal{M}$
The boundary of an invariant subvariety

nodes.label: Boundary stratification of stable-curve moduli
nodes.summary: The decomposition of the boundary of the compactified moduli space of stable curves into combinatorially described strata corresponding to degenerate stable curves.

evidence.quote: combinatorial stratification of the boundary

nodes.label: Bounded combinatorics
nodes.summary: For an infinitely renormalizable map, the property that the periods of the successive renormalizations remain uniformly bounded.

evidence.quote: remain bounded

nodes.label: Bounded right inverse for the linearized hyperkähler operator
nodes.summary: Uniform invertibility estimate for the linearized hyperkähler triple operator in the weighted spaces; it is the analytic input for applying the implicit function theorem.

evidence.quote: finding a bounded right inverse to the linearized operator L
Existence of a bounded right inverse

nodes.label: Bounded symmetric domain quotient
aliases: quotient of a bounded symmetric domain
nodes.summary: A quasi-projective variety X=Γ\Ω obtained as the quotient of a bounded symmetric domain Ω by a torsion-free lattice Γ in Aut(Ω). Such quotients carry descended locally symmetric Kähler-Einstein metrics.

evidence.quote: Let X = Γ\Ω be an n-dimensional quotient of a bounded symmetric domain Ω by a torsion-free lattice Γ ⊂ Aut(Ω).
evidence.section: 1. Closing the cusps of locally symmetric spaces

nodes.label: BPF norm on numerical b-classes
nodes.summary: A Banach norm on b-classes built from base-point-free positivity and comparison with positive cycle classes. It is used to complete spaces of higher-codimension numerical b-classes.

evidence.quote: The norm  $\| \cdot \|_{\mathrm{BPF}}$
endow this space with various Banach norms

nodes.label: Branch point count
nodes.summary: The algebraic count of branch points of a branched cover, denoted in the excerpt by Z(d phi).

evidence.quote: algebraic count of branch points

nodes.label: Branched cover of Riemann surfaces
nodes.summary: A holomorphic cover of Riemann surfaces allowed to have ramification points, detected by zeros of the derivative of the covering map.

evidence.quote: branched cover of closed connected Riemann surfaces

nodes.label: Transversality obstruction for branched multiple covers
nodes.summary: In dimensions at least six, a branched cover of a simple index-zero curve can have negative expected dimension while the family of branched covers has positive dimension, obstructing ordinary transversality.

evidence.quote: cannot achieve transversality if phi has branch points

nodes.label: Brieskorn lattice
nodes.summary: The O_{T,0}-module Omega^{n+1}_{X,0}/(df wedge d Omega^{n-1}_{X,0}) associated with an isolated hypersurface singularity. It carries a meromorphic connection identified with the Gauss-Manin connection.

evidence.quote: which is called the Brieskorn lattice

nodes.label: Bubble limit
nodes.summary: A pointed Gromov-Hausdorff limit obtained after rescaling a degenerating metric sequence by factors tending to infinity near curvature blow-up points.

evidence.quote: we will call any such limit a bubble limit
rescalings of the metrics near xβ,j converge

nodes.label: Bubbling set
nodes.summary: The subset away from the initial sheaf singular tangent cone where rescaled HYM connections fail to converge smoothly. Its codimension-two part contributes to the analytic blow-up cycle.

evidence.quote: Sigma is the bubbling set
where the convergence is not smooth

nodes.label: Butterfly complex bounds
aliases: complex bounds for quasicritical circle maps
nodes.summary: Uniform complex a priori bounds representing renormalizations of quasicritical circle pairs as butterfly maps with bounded shape and definite modulus.

evidence.quote: represented as a butterfly

nodes.label: C1,1 estimate from W2,p for cscK
nodes.summary: An iteration argument that upgrades sufficiently high Lp control of n+Delta phi, together with bounded F, to a uniform bound for n+Delta phi and |grad_phi F|. This supplies uniform metric equivalence for the cscK system.

evidence.quote: n+\Delta\varphi\leq C
evidence.section: 4. C1,1 bound of the Kähler potential in terms of its W2,p bound

nodes.label: C-complete holomorphic vector field
nodes.summary: A holomorphic vector field whose complex-time flow exists for all complex times. These vector fields generate the Lie algebra used in the density property.

evidence.quote: C-complete holomorphic vector fields

nodes.label: Calabi ansatz
nodes.summary: A construction of explicit Kähler metrics on the total space of a Hermitian holomorphic line bundle using a radial Kähler potential φ(t). In this paper the ansatz reduces the Kähler-Einstein equation to an ODE for φ and supplies model cone metrics near D.

evidence.quote: construct some explicit model Kähler metrics in the total space of a holomorphic line bundle L over D
Kähler potential φ = φ(t) only depends on t
(−σφ′)n−1φ′′ = ce−σφ
first use the Calabi ansatz to write down a background complete Kähler metric

nodes.label: Calabi dream manifold
nodes.summary: A Kähler manifold such that every Kähler class on it admits an extremal Kähler metric. The paper introduces this name while discussing surfaces whose every Kähler class admits a canonical representative.

evidence.quote: Calabi dream manifold
every Kähler class on it admits an extremal Kähler metric

nodes.label: Calabi model cone metric
aliases: model metric from the Calabi ansatz
nodes.summary: The one-parameter family of Kähler-Einstein cone metrics ωβ=i∂∂φβ on L\D produced by the Calabi ansatz, with cone angle 2πβ degenerating to zero. In the negative case it models a hyperbolic cusp, while in the positive case its rescaling has the Tian-Yau asymptotic form.

evidence.quote: produce our model families (φβ(t)) with angle 2πβ degenerating to zero
ωβ = i∂∂φβ
rescaling β−1−1/n φβ converges

nodes.label: Calabi model space
aliases: Calabi model metric of degree b
nodes.summary: A Ricci-flat Kähler or hyperkähler model on a punctured degree-b line bundle over an elliptic curve, equivalently a Gibbons-Hawking metric over T2 x R with linear potential; its level sets are Heisenberg nilmanifolds.

evidence.quote: we call the corresponding metric a Calabi model metric of degree b
model space at infinity of the Tian-Yau metrics
evidence.section: 2. The Gibbons-Hawking ansatz and the model space

nodes.label: Calabi-Tian-Yau gluing construction
nodes.summary: The construction of an approximate conic Kähler-Einstein metric by gluing the Calabi cone model near D to a scaled Tian-Yau metric away from D, followed by an implicit-function or fixed-point argument to solve the exact equation.

evidence.quote: construct a model cone metric ωβ by gluing the Calabi metric ωβ,L near D and the Tian-Yau metric ωTY away from D
the implicit function theorem allows us to find the Kähler-Einstein metric

nodes.label: Calabi-Yau cone
nodes.summary: A Riemannian cone whose smooth locus carries a Calabi-Yau metric, complex structure, Kahler form, and holomorphic volume form. In this paper it is also regarded as a normal affine algebraic cone.

evidence.quote: is a Calabi-Yau metric on C \ {O}

nodes.label: Calabi-Yau hypersurface
nodes.summary: A projective hypersurface that is also a Calabi-Yau manifold; the paper considers such hypersurfaces in the Fermat family.

evidence.quote: Calabi-Yau hypersurfaces in the Fermat family
Fermat family of projective hypersurfaces

nodes.label: Calabi-Yau manifold
nodes.summary: A Kähler complex manifold with trivial canonical data, represented here by a holomorphic volume form, and with the relevant smooth metrics having holonomy SU(n).

evidence.quote: Calabi-Yau (CY) manifolds
of holonomy SU(n)

nodes.label: Calabi-Yau metric
nodes.summary: A Ricci-flat Kähler metric in a specified Kähler class on a Calabi-Yau manifold; the paper studies such metrics on the Fermat hypersurfaces as the complex structure degenerates.

evidence.quote: consider the Calabi-Yau metrics on X_s in the polarisation class s^{-1}[Delta]
understanding how CY metrics degenerate as the complex and Kahler structures vary

nodes.label: Calabi-Yau metric on the intermediate cone
nodes.summary: A complete Calabi-Yau metric constructed on the smooth part of the intermediate cone W so that its asymptotic cone is C and its metric completion is homeomorphic to W. This constructed metric is the key bridge from the algebraic degeneration to the Bishop-Gromov volume argument.

evidence.quote: construct a Calabi-Yau metric ω_W on W\{0\}

nodes.label: Calabi-Yau Monge-Ampere equation
nodes.summary: The equation equating the top exterior power of the Kahler form with the holomorphic volume form volume density, written in this paper as omega^n = i^{n^2} Omega wedge conjugate(Omega). It is part of the definition of the complete Calabi-Yau metrics considered here.

evidence.quote: the following complex Monge-Ampère equation is satisfied

nodes.label: Calabi-Yau rescaling at infinity
nodes.summary: The construction that rescales the pointed Calabi-Yau manifold by powers of a factor lambda, with g_j = lambda^{2j} g and Omega_j = lambda^{nj} Omega. It produces subsequential pointed Gromov-Hausdorff limits at infinity.

evidence.quote: g_j = λ^{2j}g, Ω_j = λ^{nj}Ω

nodes.label: Calabi-Yau 3-fold
nodes.summary: A complex or symplectic six-dimensional Calabi-Yau target for which the paper's super-rigidity result has consequences for Gromov-Witten invariants.

evidence.quote: Calabi-Yau 3-folds

nodes.label: Canonical real form of a magical triple
nodes.summary: For a magical sl2-triple, choose a real form tau_e such that sigma_e is a Cartan involution; the fixed real Lie algebra is the canonical real form. For principal triples this is the split real form.

evidence.quote: canonical real form
evidence.section: 2.3. The canonical real form associated to a magical nilpotent

nodes.label: Canonical sheaf
nodes.summary: The sheaf of holomorphic top-degree forms on a smooth complex manifold, and its appropriate dualizing analogue on singular spaces. It is used to define the Grauert-Riemenschneider sheaf.

evidence.quote: omega_X^GR = r_* omega_X̃
omega_X^{[m]}

nodes.label: Canonical singularity condition
nodes.summary: A mild birational singularity condition stronger than rationality in the settings used here. The paper reduces to K-trivial Kähler varieties with canonical singularities when applying singular Ricci-flat metrics.

evidence.quote: canonical singularities

nodes.label: Cartier numerical b-class
nodes.summary: A numerical b-class determined on one birational model and pulled back to all higher models. Cartier b-classes form the model-finite part of the b-class formalism.

evidence.quote: Weil and Cartier  $b$  -classes

nodes.label: Cauchy-Riemann operator with symmetries
nodes.summary: A Cauchy-Riemann type operator equipped with symmetries, such as those induced by covering transformations, so that representation theory can split it into components.

evidence.quote: Cauchy-Riemann operators with symmetries

nodes.label: Cayley character variety locus
nodes.summary: The open-and-closed subset of X(G^R) obtained from Cayley components by nonabelian Hodge. It contains the Fuchsian locus and has representations with compact centralizer and no compact-subgroup factorization.

evidence.quote: Pe(GR)

nodes.label: Cayley component
nodes.summary: A connected component in the image of the Cayley map inside M(G^R). Such components are open and closed and consist of Higgs bundles with nowhere-vanishing Higgs field.

evidence.quote: Cayley components
evidence.section: 7.1. Generalized Cayley correspondence and direct consequences

nodes.label: Cayley group of a magical triple
nodes.summary: The real Lie group associated to the Cayley real form, written as (R_+)^{r(e)} times the semisimple real group with Lie algebra tilde g^R. It is the group whose twisted Higgs-bundle moduli form the domain of the Cayley map.

evidence.quote: Cayley group

nodes.label: Cayley map for a magical triple
nodes.summary: A map from a product of twisted Cayley-group Higgs moduli and holomorphic differentials to G^R-Higgs bundles, constructed from the magical decomposition and the uniformizing Higgs bundle. It descends to an injective open-and-closed map on moduli spaces.

evidence.quote: Cayley map

nodes.label: Cayley real form of a magical triple
nodes.summary: The real form of the centralizer g_0 of h obtained from the involution theta_e associated to a magical triple. It decomposes as a Euclidean factor plus a semisimple real Lie algebra.

evidence.quote: Cayley real form
evidence.section: 2.3. The canonical real form associated to a magical nilpotent

nodes.label: Cayley transform of sl2-triples
nodes.summary: A bijective transform between Cayley triples in a real form and normal sl2-triples in the complex Lie algebra. It realizes the Sekiguchi correspondence used to relate magical triples to real nilpotents and positive parabolics.

evidence.quote: Cayley transform

nodes.label: Characteristic polynomial of a Higgs field
nodes.summary: The spectral invariant of a Higgs field theta, regarded as a point of the Hitchin base. The Hitchin map sends a Higgs bundle to this invariant.

evidence.quote: sending (\mathcal{E},\theta)\in \mathcal{M}_{\mathrm{Dol}} to the characteristic polynomial \mathrm{char}(\theta)\in \Lambda

nodes.label: Characteristic sequence of a plane branch
nodes.summary: The sequence (n, beta_1, ..., beta_g), where n is the multiplicity and g is the number of characteristic pairs of an irreducible plane curve singularity. Yano's generating function is built from this topological data.

evidence.quote: characteristic sequence (n, beta_1, ..., beta_g)

nodes.label: Chern class
nodes.summary: The characteristic classes c_i(E) of a complex vector bundle. In this paper they enter through the tautological Chern classes whose perversity is controlled.

evidence.quote: Strong perversity for Chern classes

nodes.label: Chern connection
nodes.summary: The canonical connection on a Hermitian holomorphic vector bundle compatible with both the Hermitian metric and holomorphic structure. In the paper an admissible HYM connection is the Chern connection of an HYM metric on the locally free locus.

evidence.quote: Chern connection

nodes.label: chi_2 invariant of an Apollonian packing
nodes.summary: An invariant assigning a sign to each circle using the Kronecker symbol and the associated form f_C; it is constant across a primitive packing, giving chi_2(A). The value of chi_2 determines the quadratic obstruction families.

evidence.quote: χ2 : {circles in A} → {±1}

nodes.label: chi_4 invariant of an Apollonian packing
nodes.summary: An invariant for packings of type (6,1) or (6,17), taking values in {1,i,-1,-i}, defined from a Gaussian-integer lattice associated to a circle and the quartic residue symbol. It is constant across the packing, squares to chi_2, and determines quartic obstructions.

nodes.label: Chow group of an algebraic stack
nodes.summary: The group of algebraic cycle classes on an algebraic stack, modulo rational equivalence. The classical double ramification cycle is an element of the Chow group of the stable-curve compactification.

evidence.quote: DR_{g,A} in CH_{2g-3+n}(\overline{\mathcal{M}}_{g,n})

nodes.label: Circle curvature
nodes.summary: The signed curvature attached to a circle in an Apollonian packing. Straight lines are allowed as circles of curvature zero, while negative curvature records an orientation convention.

evidence.quote: circles of curvature zero

nodes.label: Quadratic form associated to a circle
nodes.summary: For a circle C of curvature n in a primitive Apollonian packing, f_C is a quadratic form of discriminant -4n^2 corresponding to C through the Descartes-quadruple/quadratic-form bijection. Curvatures of circles tangent to C are properly represented by a shift of f_C.

evidence.quote: define fC to be a quadratic form

nodes.label: Closed complex disk
nodes.summary: The closed disk with center z0 and radius r is the set of complex z with |z-z0| <= r.

evidence.quote: the closed disk ... {z in C: | z - z0 | <= r}

nodes.label: Exterior of a closed disk
nodes.summary: The exterior region of a closed disk is the set of complex z with |z-z0| > r.

evidence.quote: the exterior region ... {z in C: | z - z0 | > r}

nodes.label: Closed positive (1,1)-current
nodes.summary: A weak positive (1,1)-form closed in the sense of currents, locally expressible as i∂∂bar of a potential. The proof uses such currents as weak limits.

evidence.quote: closed positive (1,1) current ω

nodes.label: Closed quasi-positive (1,1)-current

nodes.label: Closed self-dual 2-form

evidence.quote: there is a triple of closed self-dual 2-forms

nodes.label: CM weight of a test configuration
nodes.summary: An intersection-theoretic invariant of a normal test configuration used as the K-stability weight. On special test configurations it coincides with the non-Archimedean Ding invariant and the generalized Futaki invariant.

nodes.label: Coarse-moduli point count equivalence for Deligne-Mumford stacks

nodes.label: Coercivity of the twisted Ding functional
nodes.summary: A lower growth condition for the twisted Ding functional on the finite-energy potential space. It is part of the variational bridge between existence of twisted Kähler-Einstein currents and uniform Ding stability.

nodes.label: Cohen-Macaulay D-module
nodes.summary: A nonzero finitely generated A_R-module N is j-Cohen-Macaulay when Ext^k_{A_R}(N,A_R) vanishes for all k different from j. The paper uses this property for relative holonomic modules over parameter rings.

nodes.label: Coherent analytic sheaf
nodes.summary: A sheaf of modules over the holomorphic functions that is locally finitely generated with finitely generated relation sheaves. It is the ambient sheaf class in which reflexive and torsion-free sheaves are considered.

nodes.label: Cohomological J-constant

nodes.label: Cohomological rigidity of complex hyperbolic lattices
aliases: vanishing of H1 with adjoint coefficients
nodes.summary: For lattices in the relevant rank-one groups other than low-rank exceptions, first Eilenberg-MacLane cohomology with coefficients in the adjoint representation vanishes. The paper uses this rigidity, together with Esnault-Groechenig integrality, to obtain integral traces.

nodes.label: Cohomologically rigid local system
nodes.summary: A complex local system whose infinitesimal deformation cohomology vanishes, so it has no nontrivial first-order deformations with the prescribed local data. Simpson integrality is invoked for such local systems.

nodes.label: Collapse map to an interval
nodes.summary: A continuous surjective map from the K3 surface to the interval that realizes the metric collapse and whose regular fibers are circle bundles over a 2-torus.

nodes.label: Schauder estimate for collapsed cone metrics

nodes.label: Compact convex set in complex Euclidean space
nodes.summary: A compact convex subset of some complex Euclidean space C^n. Such sets are the test domains in the convex approximation definition of Oka manifolds and Oka submersions.

nodes.label: Compact holomorphically convex set
nodes.summary: A compact subset K of a complex manifold or Stein space whose holomorphic hull with respect to the ambient holomorphic functions is K itself. In the paper, complements of such sets are the primary objects shown to be Oka.

nodes.label: Compact Kähler manifold
nodes.summary: A compact complex manifold equipped with a Kähler metric. The paper fixes an n-dimensional Kähler manifold M as the ambient space for the J-equation and dHYM equation.

nodes.label: Compact Kähler variety
nodes.summary: A compact complex analytic variety equipped with a Kähler structure in the singular analytic sense. It is the ambient category for the paper's decomposition and approximation results.

evidence.quote: compact Kähler variety

nodes.label: Compactified projectivized Hodge bundle
aliases: compactified projectivization of a Hodge bundle
nodes.summary: A compactification of the projectivization of a bundle of differentials over moduli of curves. Intersection formulas for strata of differentials use classes on such compactified projectivizations.

evidence.quote: compactified projectivizations of Hodge bundles

nodes.label: Compactly supported ell-adic etale cohomology
nodes.summary: The compactly supported etale cohomology groups of a stack or variety after base change to an algebraic closure, with coefficients in $\mathbb{Q}_\ell$.

evidence.quote: $H _ {c, \acute {e} t} ^ {\bullet} (\mathfrak {X} _ {\overline {{\mathbb {F}}} _ {q}}, \mathbb {Q} _ {\ell})$

nodes.label: Compactness property in the Picard-Manin Banach space
nodes.summary: A compactness property for bounded or positive families in the Picard-Manin Banach space. The paper uses it together with the Hodge-index property to control spectral behavior.

nodes.label: Compactness subsequence extraction
nodes.summary: A method of passing to subsequences so bounded or tight objects converge to limiting objects, using results such as Bolzano-Weierstrass and Prokhorov.

nodes.label: Complemented Katz algorithm
nodes.summary: An enhancement of Katz's algorithm that tracks additional local and global Hodge data and determines Hodge numerical data for rigid local systems. The excerpt cites it as an algorithmic way to determine the associated Hermitian form's signature.

nodes.label: Complex analytic compactifiability of complete Calabi-Yau manifolds
nodes.summary: The property that a complete Calabi-Yau manifold admits a compactification in the complex analytic sense. The paper proves this property for ALG and ALH non-splitting gravitational instantons under fast curvature decay.

nodes.label: Complete Calabi-Yau manifold with Euclidean volume growth and quadratic curvature decay

nodes.label: Complete Calabi-Yau manifold
nodes.summary: A complete noncompact Calabi-Yau manifold, typically a complete Ricci-flat Kähler manifold in this context. The paper invokes Yau's compactification question for such manifolds.

is a complete Kähler metric, Ω is a holomorphic volume form

nodes.label: Complete hyperkähler 4-manifold
nodes.summary: A complete four-dimensional Riemannian manifold carrying a hyperkähler structure. It is the geometric ambient object used in the paper's definition of gravitational instanton.

evidence.quote: Any connected complete hyperkähler manifold (M^4,g)

nodes.label: Complex algebraic cycle
nodes.summary: A formal integer linear combination of irreducible complex algebraic subvarieties. The analytic blow-up cycle is a codimension-two algebraic cycle on projective space.

nodes.label: Complex analytic compactification

nodes.summary: A compactification of a noncompact complex manifold as a complex analytic space by adjoining boundary or divisor data. The paper proves such compactifications in ALG and ALH non-splitting cases.

nodes.label: Complex hyperbolic cusp closing by ramified covers
nodes.summary: For arithmetic ball quotients, choosing cone angles compatible with ramified covers yields orbifold Kähler-Einstein metrics whose pullbacks are smooth compact negatively curved Kähler-Einstein metrics converging to the complex hyperbolic cusp metric.

nodes.label: Complex hyperbolic lattice
nodes.summary: A lattice Γ in the complex hyperbolic isometry group PU(1,n). It acts on the associated Hermitian symmetric space X, producing the ball quotient S_Γ.

nodes.label: Complex linear surface group representation
nodes.summary: A group homomorphism from the surface group pi_1(Sigma_{g,n}) to GL_r(C). The paper studies such representations through their conjugacy classes under the mapping class group action.

evidence.quote: $\rho : \pi_ {1} (\Sigma_ {g, n}) \to \operatorname {G L} _ {r} (\mathbb {C})$

nodes.label: Complex orbifold
nodes.summary: A complex analytic space locally modeled on quotients of complex manifolds by finite group actions; it is the ambient setting for the analytic generality notions in the excerpt.

evidence.quote: a complex orbifold X

nodes.label: Complex polynomial
nodes.summary: A polynomial map f:C -> C of finite degree, studied through its zeros and the zeros of its derivative.

nodes.label: Complex projective line
nodes.summary: The one-dimensional complex projective space, serving as the base of the O(4) line bundle in the twistor-space construction.

nodes.label: Complex random variable
nodes.summary: A random variable taking values in the complex plane, used to encode empirical distributions of polynomial zeros and critical points.

nodes.label: Complex space
nodes.summary: A complex-analytic space, used as the ambient category for possibly singular analytic spaces in the paper.

nodes.label: Complex subvariety
nodes.summary: An analytic subspace V of the compact Kähler manifold, often of pure dimension p. The paper tests stability by integrating forms over all p-dimensional subvarieties.

nodes.label: Complex torus

nodes.label: Complexified Cartan decomposition

nodes.label: Conformal blocks for the special linear group
aliases: conformal blocks for the special linear group

nodes.label: Conformal hyperbolicity

nodes.label: Conic Lagrangian subvariety
nodes.summary: A C*-conic Lagrangian subvariety of a cotangent bundle. Relative holonomic modules are characterized using products of such subvarieties with parameter-space strata.

nodes.label: Conjugacy class of a matrix tuple
nodes.summary: The equivalence relation on tuples of monodromy matrices in which all matrices are conjugated by the same invertible matrix. Isomorphism classes of the local systems in the excerpt are represented by such simultaneous conjugacy classes.

nodes.label: Degenerating-background L-infinity estimate

nodes.label: Degree-two integral cohomology class

nodes.label: Deligne-Mumford compactification of curve moduli

nodes.label: Descartes equation

evidence.quote: write c = \chi(f)

nodes.label: Double degeneration
nodes.summary: A double degeneration is the paper's method of degenerating using cylinder data so as to reach a codimension-two boundary component when codimension-one degenerations do not preserve enough rank.

evidence.quote: we develop a notion of "double degenerations"
this gives a degeneration of the surface in a codimension 2 component of the boundary

nodes.label: Double ramification cycle
nodes.summary: The Chow class on the Deligne-Mumford compactification obtained by pushing forward the virtual fundamental class of the rubber stable-map compactification of the double ramification locus.

evidence.quote: The pushforward via the canonical morphism ... is the double ramification cycle

nodes.label: Double ramification cycle with target variety
nodes.summary: A target-variety version of the double ramification cycle for moduli of stable maps, together with a Pixton-type formula. The paper uses this target theory as an essential input and compares it with the Picard-stack formulation.

evidence.quote: Essential for our work is the formula for double ramification cycles for target varieties in [43]
Comparison to Pixton's formula with targets
evidence.section: 4.3. Comparison to Pixton's formula with targets

nodes.label: Double ramification locus
nodes.summary: The substack of smooth marked curves for which the line bundle associated to the weighted divisor of marked points is trivial: O_C(sum_i a_i p_i) is isomorphic to O_C.

evidence.quote: consider the substack defined by the following classical condition: O_C(sum_i a_i p_i) isomorphic O_C

nodes.label: Double ramification weight vector
nodes.summary: A tuple of integers attached to the marked points, classically required to have total sum zero. The weights determine the divisor whose associated line bundle is tested for triviality and the relative ramification data in the rubber compactification.

evidence.quote: Let A = (a_1, ..., a_n) be a vector of n integers satisfying sum_i a_i = 0.

nodes.label: Dual locally constant geometric sections
nodes.summary: Locally constant geometric sections dual to selected locally constant cycles. Their existence is the extra input used to extend Varchenko-style semicontinuity of b-exponents beyond the distinct-monodromy-eigenvalue case.

evidence.quote: existence of certain dual locally constant geometric sections

nodes.label: Dual torus fibration mirror construction
nodes.summary: The SYZ mirror construction that replaces each torus fiber over the affine base by its dual torus.

evidence.quote: fibrewise replacing the T^n fibres with the dual tori
Mirror symmetry is T-duality

nodes.label: Dualizing complex
nodes.summary: The analytic dualizing complex of a complex space. It is used to formulate duality criteria for extending coherent sheaf sections across singular subsets.

evidence.quote: If X is any complex space, we write omega_X^bullet ... for the dualizing complex

nodes.label: Dynamical degree of a rational self-map
nodes.summary: The k-th dynamical degree is the limit of deg_{k,omega}(f^n)^{1/n}. It is independent of the polarization and invariant under birational conjugacy.

evidence.quote: one can define the dynamical degrees
do not depend on the choice of polarization  $\omega$

nodes.label: Einstein Universe
nodes.summary: The boundary at infinity of pseudo-hyperbolic space, realized as the projective quadric in P(E) associated to q. It carries the positivity structure used to define positive and semi-positive loops.

evidence.quote: admits a boundary at infinity denoted  $\partial_{\infty}\mathbf{H}^{2,n}$  and called the Einstein Universe
which is the quadric in  $\mathbf{P}(E)$  associated to  $\mathbf{q}$

nodes.label: Elementary section of Milnor cohomology
nodes.summary: A holomorphic univalued section built from a locally constant eigencomponent section A, an exponent alpha, and the logarithm of the monodromy. It relates geometric sections to the saturated Brieskorn lattice quotient.

evidence.quote: defines the elementary section associated with a locally constant section A

nodes.label: Elliptic bootstrap from uniform Kähler equivalence
nodes.summary: Once the deformed Kähler metric is uniformly equivalent to the background metric, the coupled system becomes uniformly elliptic and standard regularity theory gives all higher derivatives of the potential.

evidence.quote: If \frac{1}{C}\omega_{0}\leq\omega_{\varphi}\leq C\omega_{0}

nodes.label: Elliptic estimates with exponential growth weights
nodes.summary: Elliptic PDE estimates formulated on weighted spaces with exponential growth or decay weights. The paper uses them in constructing holomorphic functions on ALH instantons.

evidence.quote: Elliptic estimates with exponential growth weights
Holomorphic functions on ALH instantons

nodes.label: Elliptic estimates with polynomial growth weights
nodes.summary: Elliptic PDE estimates formulated on weighted spaces where the weights encode polynomial growth or decay at infinity. The paper uses them in constructing holomorphic functions on ALF and ALG instantons.

evidence.quote: Elliptic estimates with polynomial growth weights
Holomorphic functions on ALF and ALG instantons

nodes.label: Elliptic holomorphic submersion
nodes.summary: A holomorphic submersion locally admitting a holomorphic vector bundle and a fiber-preserving spray that fixes the zero section and is fiberwise submersive at zero. This relative ellipticity implies convex ellipticity.

evidence.quote: A holomorphic submersion is elliptic

nodes.label: Elliptic homogeneous symmetric eigenvalue operator
nodes.summary: A symmetric degree-one homogeneous function f on the admissible cone whose partial derivatives with respect to each eigenvalue are positive, giving ellipticity for equations in the eigenvalues of h_\varphi.

evidence.quote: f(\lambda) is assumed to be invariant under permutations of the components of \lambda
f is elliptic in the sense that \frac{\partial f(\lambda)}{\partial \lambda_j} > 0 for any \lambda \in \Gamma
f (r \lambda) = r f (\lambda), \quad r > 0

nodes.label: Elliptic manifold
nodes.summary: A complex manifold admitting a holomorphic map from a holomorphic vector bundle to the manifold that fixes the zero section and is fiberwise submersive at the zero vector. Gromov's theorem gives that ellipticity implies the Oka property.

evidence.quote: A complex manifold Y is said to be elliptic

nodes.label: Embedded J-holomorphic curve
nodes.summary: A J-holomorphic curve whose parametrization gives an embedded image, relevant to local curve-counting contributions.

evidence.quote: finite sets of embedded curves

nodes.label: Entropy-to-C0 estimate for cscK
nodes.summary: The Section 5 argument showing that an entropy or coercive entropy bound for e^F controls Lq norms of e^F and hence the C0 norm of the Kähler potential. It uses Tian's alpha-invariant, an auxiliary Monge-Ampere potential, and the Alexandrov maximum principle.

evidence.quote: \int_{M}e^{qF}dvol_{g}\leq C_{5.2}
evidence.section: 5. Entropy bound of the volume ratio and C0 bound of Kähler potential

nodes.label: Escaping parameter xi
nodes.summary: The area ratio measuring the probability that a point in the first fundamental annulus never returns to the pre-renormalization range.

evidence.quote: escaping parameter ξ is the probability

nodes.label: Integer points of the essential skeleton
nodes.summary: The essential skeleton Sk(U) is the maximum locus of the norm of the log volume form in U^an; its integer points Sk(U,Z) are the integral birational points, equivalently the multiples of divisorial valuations where the volume form has a pole in the introductory definition. These points index the theta basis of the mirror algebra and boundary data for counts.

evidence.quote: called the essential skeleton of U
Let Sk(U, Z) := Sk(U) ∩ Ubir(Z)

nodes.label: Euclidean volume growth
nodes.summary: A lower volume growth condition saying geodesic balls have volume at least a fixed positive multiple of R^{2n}. It is one of the two standing hypotheses on the complete Calabi-Yau manifold.

evidence.quote: Vol(B_g(p, R)) ≥ κ R^{2n} for all R > 0

nodes.label: Even tautological subalgebra
aliases: subalgebra generated by even tautological classes
nodes.summary: The subalgebra of cohomology generated by even tautological classes. In higher genus and arbitrary rank, the paper proves P=W for this subalgebra.

evidence.quote: P = W holds for the subalgebra of cohomology generated by even tautological classes

nodes.label: Exceptional compact set for critical points
nodes.summary: After passing to a subsequence near the unit circle, one can isolate at most countably many possible macroscopic critical-point locations in a compact subset, outside of which the random critical point converges uniformly to 0.

evidence.quote: there exists a fixed compact subset ... with the property that converges uniformly to zero
evidence.section: 5. Getting a contradiction near the unit circle

nodes.label: Exceptional divisor
nodes.summary: The divisor introduced by the blowup at the origin, identified here with CP^{n-1}. Algebraic tangent cones are torsion-free sheaves on this divisor.

evidence.quote: exceptional divisor
D = CP^{n - 1}

nodes.label: Exceptional locus of a resolution
nodes.summary: The subset of a resolution where the map is not an isomorphism. In log resolutions this set is required to be a normal crossing divisor.

evidence.quote: If the exceptional locus of r is a normal crossing divisor E

nodes.label: Expected vanishing order of a line bundle
nodes.summary: An asymptotic invariant S_L(v) measuring the average order of vanishing of sections of multiples of L along a divisorial valuation v. It is paired with log discrepancy in the valuative stability threshold.

evidence.quote: Valuations and stability
L an ample Q-line bundle on X

nodes.label: Extended Apollonian packing type
nodes.summary: The refinement of Apollonian packing type that includes chi_2, and when relevant chi_4, as extra coordinates. The extended type determines which quadratic and quartic obstruction families occur.

evidence.quote: The (extended) type of A

nodes.label: Extension across subsets for coherent sheaves
nodes.summary: A coherent sheaf F has unique extension of sections across a subspace A when the natural map F -> j_* j^* F is an isomorphism. The paper uses duality criteria for this property to prove extension of differential forms.

evidence.quote: If these conditions are satisfied, we say that sections of F extend uniquely across A
evidence.section: 6. Coherent sheaves and Mixed Hodge modules

nodes.label: Exterior derivative on differential forms
nodes.summary: The differential operator sending p-forms to (p+1)-forms. The intrinsic criterion includes the top-degree forms d alpha wedge gamma.

evidence.quote: d alpha wedge gamma on Ureg

nodes.label: Extremal-disk neighborhood identification
nodes.summary: A non-holomorphic identification of a neighborhood of D in X with a disk bundle in the normal bundle L, produced using extremal holomorphic disks. It allows normal-bundle model estimates to be transferred to X near D.

evidence.quote: an intrinsic identification using the theory of extremal discs, which produces a (non-holomorphic) fibration in holomorphic discs
identify a neighbourhood of D in X with a disc bundle in L

nodes.label: Extremal Kähler metric
nodes.summary: A canonical Kähler metric generalizing cscK metrics, characterized classically by the holomorphicity of the gradient of scalar curvature. The excerpt uses the concept as the target metric in the definition of Calabi dream manifolds but does not define it.

evidence.quote: extremal Kähler metrics

nodes.label: F_Sigma space of b-divisors
nodes.summary: A space of numerical b-divisors with finite Sigma-type norm on which the paper defines an intersection pairing valued in codimension-two Weil b-classes.

evidence.quote: The space  $\mathcal{F}_{\Sigma}(\mathcal{X})$

nodes.label: Factorwise positive-dimensional Hodge locus
nodes.summary: The union of special subvarieties in the Hodge locus that are factorwise of positive period dimension.

evidence.quote: $\textnormal{HL}(S,{\mathbb{V}}^{\otimes})_{\textnormal{f-pos}}$ ... is the union of the special subvarieties ... factorwise of positive period dimension

nodes.label: Algebraicity of the factorwise positive Hodge locus in high level
nodes.summary: For a level at least three polarizable ZVHS, the factorwise positive-period-dimensional Hodge locus is a finite union of maximal atypical special subvarieties and therefore algebraic.

evidence.quote: If ${\mathbb{V}}$ is of level at least $3$ then $\textnormal{HL}(S,{\mathbb{V}}^{\otimes})_{\textnormal{f-pos}}$ is a finite union of maximal atypical special subvarieties (hence is algebraic)
evidence.section: 2.3 On the algebraicity of the Hodge locus

nodes.label: Factorwise positive period dimension
nodes.summary: A strengthened positive-period condition: after decomposing the connected Hodge variety into simple adjoint factors, the projection of the period image to each factor has positive dimension.

evidence.quote: If moreover the projection of $\Phi(Z^{\textnormal{an}})$ on each factor $\Gamma_i\backslash D_i$ ... has positive dimension, then $Z$ is said to be factorwise of positive period dimension

nodes.label: Family of compact holomorphically convex sets
nodes.summary: For a holomorphic submersion pi:Y to B, a subset S of Y such that pi restricted to S is proper and, locally over B, S is holomorphically convex in the corresponding inverse image of the base neighborhood.

evidence.quote: family of compact holomorphically convex sets

nodes.label: Family of curves over a contractible domain
aliases: C to Delta family of curves
nodes.summary: A family of curves over a contractible analytic domain Delta with a chosen central fiber C0, serving as the base setup for isomonodromic deformation.

evidence.quote: Let C0 be the central fiber of a family of curves C to Delta with Delta a contractible domain

nodes.label: Fano manifold
nodes.summary: A complex manifold whose anticanonical bundle −K_X is ample. The positive-curvature part of the paper assumes X is Fano.

evidence.quote: X is a Fano manifold (that is, −KX is ample)

nodes.label: Faster than quadratic curvature decay
nodes.summary: A curvature decay condition requiring the Riemann curvature norm to be bounded by a negative power strictly faster than r^{-2}. In the paper this is imposed with a small positive epsilon.

evidence.quote: curvature decaying as (1.1)
faster than quadratic curvature decay condition

nodes.label: Feigenbaum map
nodes.summary: An infinitely renormalizable quadratic-like map with bounded combinatorics and a priori bounds.

evidence.quote: bounded combinatorics and a priori bounds

nodes.label: Gromov-Hausdorff limit of Fermat Calabi-Yau metrics
nodes.summary: A subsequential metric limit of the Fermat Calabi-Yau metrics whose open dense regular part is locally isometric to a subset of the tropical simplex boundary carrying a smooth real Monge-Ampere metric.

evidence.quote: Any subsequential Gromov-Hausdorff limit of the CY metrics contains an open dense subset
locally isometric to R subset partial Delta_lambda^vee carrying a smooth real MA metric

nodes.label: Fermat family of Calabi-Yau hypersurfaces
nodes.summary: The one-parameter family of Calabi-Yau projective hypersurfaces X_s given by an equation of the form Z_0...Z_{n+1}+e^{-s} sum_i Z_i^{n+2}=0 for large s, approaching the large complex structure limit.

evidence.quote: We focus on the Fermat family of projective hypersurfaces of any dimension n

nodes.label: F^i-type locus for a class
nodes.summary: For a class lambda and integer i, V^i(lambda) is the intersection of its flat leaf with the Hodge filtration piece F^iV, and S^i(lambda) is its projection to the base.

evidence.quote: The locus of classes of Fi-type Vi(λ) for λ is the intersection of the flat leaf V(λ) with FiV ... The locus of Fi-type for λ is the projection Si(λ) := p(Vi(λ))

nodes.label: Fibered density property
nodes.summary: For a holomorphic submersion pi:Y to B, the Lie algebra generated by C-complete holomorphic vector fields tangent to fibers of pi is dense in the Lie algebra of all fiber-tangent holomorphic vector fields.

evidence.quote: enjoys the (fibered) density property

nodes.label: Filled Julia set
nodes.summary: For a quadratic-like map, the set of points whose full forward orbit remains in the domain U.

evidence.quote: set K(f) of points z

nodes.label: Filtered D-module
nodes.summary: A D-module equipped with a good filtration by coherent modules. It is one of the components underlying Hodge and mixed Hodge modules.

evidence.quote: the underlying filtered D_X-module (M, F_M) and the de Rham complex DR(M)

nodes.label: Finite-energy geodesic segment
nodes.summary: A d1-geodesic segment in E^1 obtained as a decreasing limit of C^{1,1} geodesics between smooth approximating potentials. These segments extend geodesic geometry from H to E^1.

evidence.quote: finite energy geodesic
d1-geodesic

nodes.label: Finite-energy Hermitian metric
aliases: finite energy positively curved Hermitian metric
nodes.summary: A positively curved possibly singular Hermitian metric of the form psi+u on a Q-line bundle, with u in the finite-energy pluripotential space E^1. It is the analytic ambient space for singular Kähler-Einstein metrics and energy functionals.

evidence.quote: finite energy Hermitian metric

nodes.label: Finite-energy potential
