nodes.summary: A plurisubharmonic potential with finite Monge-Ampère energy. Twisted Kähler-Einstein currents in the paper are required to have finite energy.

evidence.quote: of finite energy in the sense of [BBGZ13]
Finite energy potentials and psh geodesics
E^p = {ϕ ∈ PSH(M,ω0)
∫M |ϕ|pωnϕ < ∞

nodes.label: Finite-field point count of stable-curve moduli
nodes.summary: The number, in the stacky finite-field sense, of finite-field points of the Deligne-Mumford compactification of the moduli space of stable curves.

evidence.quote: We compute the number of $\mathbb{F}_q$ -points on $\overline{\mathcal{M}}_{4,n}$

nodes.label: Finite global monodromy
nodes.summary: The condition that the image of the monodromy representation of a local system is a finite group. It is one of the restrictions in the paper's boundedness and nonexistence statements.

evidence.quote: including those with finite global monodromy
with finite global monodromy

nodes.label: Finite group representation
nodes.summary: A linear action of a finite group on a vector space, used to decompose operators carrying finite symmetry groups.

evidence.quote: A digression on representation theory

nodes.label: Finite groupoid of stack-valued finite-field points
nodes.summary: For an algebraic stack over a finite field, the set of finite-field-valued points is treated as a finite groupoid, with isomorphism classes and automorphism groups of objects.

evidence.quote: $\mathfrak{X}(\mathbb{F}_q)$ is a finite groupoid

nodes.label: Finite-image surface group representation
nodes.summary: A surface group representation whose image subgroup in GL_r(C) is finite. The main result shows this property for low-rank MCG-finite representations.

evidence.quote: then  $\rho$  has finite image

nodes.label: Finite L2 curvature
nodes.summary: The condition that the curvature form of a connection has finite L2 norm on compact subsets. It is part of admissibility for HYM connections over singular sheaves.

evidence.quote: has finite  $L^2$  norm

nodes.label: Finite local monodromy
nodes.summary: The condition that each local monodromy transformation has finite order. The paper considers local monodromies whose orders divide a fixed integer n and rigid systems with finite local monodromies.

evidence.quote: local monodromies have orders which divide  n
with finite local monodromies

nodes.label: Finite-log-discrepancy valuation
nodes.summary: A real valuation v on X satisfying A_(X,D)(v)<infinity, with T-invariant and G-invariant subspaces used in the paper. Divisorial valuations are included among the relevant finite-log-discrepancy valuations.

evidence.quote: A_(X,D)(v)<+infinity

nodes.label: Finite mapping class group orbit
nodes.summary: The property that the orbit of a representation conjugacy class under the mapping class group action is a finite set.

evidence.quote: finite orbit under  $\mathrm{Mod}_{g,n}$

nodes.label: Finiteness of maximal special subvarieties in non-arithmetic ball quotients
aliases: finiteness of maximal totally geodesic subvarieties
nodes.summary: A non-arithmetic ball quotient S_Γ contains only finitely many maximal positive-dimensional totally geodesic, equivalently special, subvarieties. This is the main structural property proved by the paper.

evidence.quote: only finitely many maximal totally geodesic subvarieties

nodes.label: First Chern class
nodes.summary: The degree-two cohomology class associated with a complex manifold or line bundle. The cohomological compatibility condition for the twisted Kähler-Einstein equation is expressed using c1(X) and c1(L).

evidence.quote: is the first Chern class of some nontrivial holomorphic line bundle L on X
c_1(L) is primitive in H^2(X,Z)
let  $\psi_{i} = c_{1}(\mathcal{L}_{i})$  denote the first Chern class of this bundle

nodes.label: Flat bundle with never-semistable isomonodromic deformations
nodes.summary: A flat vector bundle on a smooth proper complex curve whose isomonodromic deformations to nearby curves fail to be semistable. This names the reusable property underlying the paper's counterexample, not the numbered result.

evidence.quote: construct a flat vector bundle on a smooth proper curve over C, whose isomonodromic deformations to a nearby curve are never semistable

nodes.label: Uniform Schauder estimate for flat cone metrics
nodes.summary: A Schauder estimate for the Laplacian of the flat cone metric on C*×C^{n−1} with cone angle 2πβ, with constants uniform as β tends to zero. It supplies the local analytic estimate needed for the gluing problem.

evidence.quote: establishing a Schauder estimate for the model cone metric on C* × Cn−1 with cone angle 2πβ which is uniform in β
Theorem 6.1 (Weak Schauder estimate)

nodes.label: Flat connection
nodes.summary: A connection with zero curvature, so that local horizontal sections form a local system and have monodromy.

evidence.quote: a vector bundle with flat connection

nodes.label: Flat leaf of a connection
nodes.summary: For a point lambda in the vector bundle of a flat connection, its flat leaf is the image in the bundle of the constant section over the universal cover determined by lambda.

evidence.quote: Given λ = π(s~, λ0) ∈ V we define V(λ) := π(S~ × {λ0}) ⊂ V the flat leave of λ for ∇.

nodes.label: Flat model cone metric along a divisor
nodes.summary: The local model for a cone singularity of angle 2πβ along a smooth divisor D, written in coordinates with D=(z1=0) as a product of a two-dimensional cone in the normal direction and a flat metric tangent to D.

evidence.quote: locally quasi-isometric to the model cone metric
on each coordinate chart (U,(zi)) where U ∩ D = (z1 = 0)

nodes.label: Flat T^3/Z2 orbifold limit
nodes.summary: A three-dimensional flat orbifold obtained as the quotient of a flat 3-torus by a Z2 action; it is one possible collapsed limit space of unit-diameter hyperkähler metrics on the K3 manifold.

evidence.quote: (dimension 3) a flat orbifold  $\mathbb{T}^3 /\mathbb{Z}_2$

nodes.label: Flat vector bundle with flat connection
nodes.summary: A vector bundle E on a curve equipped with a flat connection nabla; it is the differential-geometric object deformed in the paper's isomonodromy analysis.

evidence.quote: let (E0, nabla0) be a vector bundle with flat connection on C0
flat vector bundles under isomonodromic deformations

nodes.label: Fock-Goncharov skew-symmetric X-cluster variety
nodes.summary: A Fock-Goncharov cluster X-variety, possibly with frozen variables, whose skew-symmetric cluster data give a class of examples. Under the smooth affine Spec H0 condition in the paper, it yields a smooth affine log Calabi-Yau variety containing an open split algebraic torus.

evidence.quote: Fock-Goncharov skew-symmetric X-cluster variety
evidence.section: 1.4 Scattering diagram and comparison with GHKK

evidence.quote: U is a smooth affine log Calabi-Yau variety
evidence.section: 1.4 Scattering diagram and comparison with GHKK

nodes.label: Foliation in the tangent sheaf
nodes.summary: A subsheaf of the tangent sheaf closed under the Lie bracket. Foliations are the directions along which specialized locally trivial deformations are taken.

evidence.quote: foliation
evidence.section: 2 Locally trivial deformations along foliations and resolutions

nodes.label: Fontaine-Mazur conjecture
nodes.summary: A conjecture constraining arithmetic p-adic Galois representations, predicting that geometric representations arise from geometry or automorphic sources in appropriate settings. The paper gives applications of its methods to this conjectural framework.

evidence.quote: applications of our methods to the Putman-Wieland conjecture, the Fontaine-Mazur conjecture

nodes.label: Fourier coefficient extraction
nodes.summary: A method of extracting moment or integral constraints on the unit circle by comparing Fourier coefficients.

evidence.quote: From ... and Fourier uniqueness we conclude that the random variables have matching moments
To get such a contradiction we will instead take the second Fourier coefficient

nodes.label: Fractional ideal sheaf
nodes.summary: A coherent sheaf of fractional ideals inside the sheaf of rational functions. In the paper it is related to valuations and used to produce b-divisor classes for polynomial maps.

evidence.quote: Fractional ideal sheaves and valuations

nodes.label: Fredholm index of a J-holomorphic curve
nodes.summary: The Fredholm index of the linearized Cauchy-Riemann operator, identified with the virtual dimension of the moduli space containing the curve.

evidence.quote: virtual dimensions of the moduli spaces containing u and u o phi, also known as the indices

nodes.label: Free cylinder
nodes.summary: A free cylinder is a cylinder whose deformation can be varied independently from other cylinders in the ambient invariant subvariety. The paper uses free cylinders, especially nested ones, in its classification step.

evidence.quote: Classification using a nested free cylinder
Finding useful cylinders

nodes.label: Frobenius multilinear form
nodes.summary: For n at least 2, the R-multilinear form on the theta module sends theta_{P_1},...,theta_{P_n} to the generating series of eta(P_1,...,P_n,beta) over curve classes beta. These forms are the multilinear part of the Frobenius structure.

evidence.quote: called the Frobenius multilinear form

nodes.label: Frobenius structure on the mirror algebra
nodes.summary: A Frobenius-type structure in which the mirror algebra multiplication, trace, and multilinear forms satisfy <a_1,...,a_n>_n = Trace(a_1...a_n) for all n at least 2. This is the algebraic structure determined by the naive curve counts.

evidence.quote: Trace(a1a2 · · · an)
compatible multilinear form

nodes.label: Fuchsian locus
nodes.summary: The subset of the PSL2R or SL2R character variety consisting of conjugacy classes of faithful representations with discrete image. A magical sl2R-subgroup embeds this locus into X(G^R).

evidence.quote: Fuchsian representations

nodes.label: Fujiki constant
nodes.summary: The manifold-dependent constant appearing in the Fujiki relation for a hyper-Kähler manifold. The paper computes or constrains it for the fourfolds under consideration.

evidence.quote: our topological assumption forces the Betti numbers, the Fujiki constant, and the Huybrechts-Riemann-Roch polynomial

nodes.label: Fujiki relation
nodes.summary: The relation expressing top self-intersection numbers of degree-two classes on a hyper-Kähler manifold as a constant multiple of a power of the Beauville-Bogomolov form.

evidence.quote: assume that there exists a nonzero class l in H^2(X,Z) such that ∫_X l^{2n} = 0
equivalent to the existence of a nonzero class in H^2(X,Q) that is isotropic with respect to the Beauville-Bogomolov form q_X

nodes.label: Fully nonlinear comparison structural condition
nodes.summary: The structural hypothesis on the fully nonlinear operator, encoded by a constant \gamma, that lets the auxiliary Monge-Ampère comparison yield uniform estimates. The visible excerpt does not state the full formula, but it is the condition beyond ellipticity and homogeneity used for the fully nonlinear extension.

evidence.quote: It extends to more general fully non-linear equations satisfying a structural condition
c (n, p, \gamma)

nodes.label: Fully nonlinear Kähler eigenvalue equation
nodes.summary: The compact Kähler equation f(\lambda[h_\varphi])=e^F with normalized potential and eigenvalues constrained to \Gamma, where f is an elliptic homogeneous symmetric operator.

evidence.quote: f (\lambda [ h _ {\varphi} ]) = e ^ {F}, \quad \sup _ {X} \varphi = 0, \quad \lambda [ h _ {\varphi} ] \in \Gamma
\int_{X} e^{nF} \omega_{X}^{n} = V = \int_{X} \omega_{X}^{n}

nodes.label: Functorial pull-back of reflexive differentials
nodes.summary: A pull-back morphism f^* Omega_Y^[p] -> Omega_X^[p] for morphisms between normal complex spaces with rational singularities, agreeing with pull-back of Kahler differentials where defined and satisfying composition.

evidence.quote: there exists a pull-back morphism d_refl f : f^* Omega_Y^[p] -> Omega_X^[p]

nodes.label: Modulus of the fundamental annulus
nodes.summary: For a quadratic-like map f:U to V, the annulus between U and V is the fundamental annulus, and its conformal modulus measures the analytic quality used in a priori bounds.

evidence.quote: modulus of the fundamental annulus

nodes.label: Fundamental group of a punctured curve
nodes.summary: The fundamental group of C with a chosen point removed. Its representations into GL_r define the local systems used in the twisted character variety.

evidence.quote: \rho : \pi_1(C \setminus \{p\}) \rightarrow \operatorname{GL}_r

nodes.label: Fundamental group of a punctured Riemann sphere
aliases: fundamental group of P^1 minus S
nodes.summary: For P^1 minus s distinct points, the fundamental group has generators represented by loops around the punctures and one relation saying their product is the identity. This presentation gives the matrix-tuple description of local systems used in the paper.

evidence.quote: the fundamental group of an  s -punctured Riemann sphere ... as the free group on generators  gamma_1, ..., gamma_s ... modulo the relation  gamma_1 gamma_2 ... gamma_s = 1

nodes.label: Futaki invariant for a log Fano pair
nodes.summary: A character on holomorphic vector fields or one-parameter subgroup directions of the automorphism torus; in this paper CW_L(xi)=-Fut_(Z,Q)(xi). The valuative criterion requires this invariant to vanish on N_R.

evidence.quote: Fut ≡ 0
CW_L(xi)=-Fut

nodes.label: G-equivariant test configuration
nodes.summary: A test configuration equipped with a G-action commuting with the C*-action generated by the test-configuration vector field and restricting over the punctured line to the original G-action on the pair.

evidence.quote: G-equivariant test configuration

nodes.label: G-properness of Ding or Mabuchi energy
nodes.summary: The Ding or Mabuchi energy is G-proper if it is bounded below by a positive multiple of the reduced J_T functional minus a constant on K-invariant finite-energy metrics. This analytic coercivity is the bridge from stability to Kähler-Einstein existence.

evidence.quote: F(phi) >= gamma J_T(phi)-C
evidence.section: 2.2 Analytic criterion for the existence of Kähler-Einstein metrics

nodes.label: G-uniform Ding stability
nodes.summary: The Ding analogue of G-uniform K-stability: D^NA of every G-equivariant test configuration is bounded below by a positive multiple of the reduced non-Archimedean J norm. The paper proves it is equivalent to G-uniform K-stability in this setting.

evidence.quote: G-uniform Ding-stable
D^NA >= gamma J_T^NA

nodes.label: G-uniform K-stability
nodes.summary: A log Fano pair is G-uniformly K-stable if G is reductive and the CM weight of every G-equivariant test configuration is bounded below by a positive multiple of the reduced non-Archimedean J norm. This is the algebro-geometric condition paired with Kähler-Einstein existence in the paper.

evidence.quote: G-uniformly K-stable
CM >= gamma J_T^NA

nodes.label: Valuative criterion for G-uniform stability
aliases: valuative criterion for G-uniform Ding stability
nodes.summary: A criterion characterizing G-uniform K/Ding stability by reductivity, vanishing Futaki character on N_R, and a uniform delta>1 inequality: every G-invariant divisorial or finite-log-discrepancy valuation can be twisted by some xi in N_R so that A(v_xi) is at least delta times S(v_xi).

evidence.quote: valuative criterion
A(v_xi) >= delta_G S(v_xi)

nodes.label: Gamma-monodromy of a subvariety
nodes.summary: For a positive-dimensional subvariety W of S_Γ, the identity component of the Zariski closure in the K-form G of the image of π_1(W) under the natural K-local system.

evidence.quote: identity component of the Zariski closure

nodes.label: Gamma-special subvariety
nodes.summary: An irreducible algebraic subvariety W of S_Γ induced by a real sub-Shimura datum (H,X_H,Γ_H) of (G,X,Γ). In this paper such subvarieties give the group-theoretic model for totally geodesic subvarieties.

evidence.quote: W is Γ-special if it is induced by

nodes.label: Gauge transform
nodes.summary: A change of local frame or bundle automorphism used to compare connections. Tangent-cone convergence is taken after applying gauge transforms, and cone connections are considered up to gauge equivalence.

evidence.quote: applying gauge transforms
up to gauge equivalence

nodes.label: Gauss-Lucas convex hull property
nodes.summary: The zeros of f' lie in the convex hull of the zeros of f; in this paper it locates critical points inside the unit disk before applying the deleted disk condition.

evidence.quote: Part (i) is clear from the hypotheses on and the Gauss-Lucas theorem.

nodes.label: Gauss-Manin connection of a singularity
nodes.summary: The natural integrable connection on the cohomological Milnor fibration over the punctured disk. It controls period expansions and is identified with the connection on the Brieskorn lattice.

evidence.quote: H^n carries a natural integrable connection called the Gauss-Manin connection

nodes.label: Gaussian integer
nodes.summary: An element a+bi of the ring Z[i]. The quartic part of the paper works over Gaussian integers, using their norm, units, and unique factorization.

evidence.quote: The Gaussian integers Z[i]

nodes.label: Gaussian integer lattice associated to a circle
nodes.summary: A lattice Λ_C inside Z[i] associated to a circle C, with covolume and conductor equal to the nonzero curvature n. The chi_4 invariant is defined using elements of this lattice modulo n.

evidence.quote: C corresponds to a lattice ΛC ⊆ Z[i]

nodes.label: General linear group over the complex numbers
nodes.summary: The group GL_r(C) of invertible r by r complex matrices. It is the target group for the linear surface group representations in the paper.

evidence.quote: $\operatorname {G L} _ {r} (\mathbb {C})$

nodes.label: Generalized Cayley correspondence
nodes.summary: The isomorphism onto open-and-closed Higgs-bundle components defined by the Cayley map. It generalizes the Hermitian tube-type Cayley correspondence and recovers the Hitchin section in the split case.

evidence.quote: generalized Cayley correspondence
evidence.section: 7.1. Generalized Cayley correspondence and direct consequences

nodes.label: Generalized J-equation with density term
nodes.summary: The auxiliary equation tr_{ωϕ}χ + fχ^n/ωϕ^n = c, coupled with a cone inequality. It is the stronger PDE statement used inductively to prove the main J-equation result.

evidence.quote: trωϕχ + f χ^n/ωϕ^n = c

nodes.label: Generalized volume minimization for K-semistable valuations
nodes.summary: The principle that K-semistable valuations minimize normalized volume in the relevant Sasaki or Fano-cone setting. The proof uses it to bound the volume density of the metric tangent cone by that of the asymptotic cone.

evidence.quote: minimizing property of K-semistable valuations

nodes.label: Generic b-exponents of a plane branch
nodes.summary: The b-exponents occurring for generic fibers in a topologically trivial or mu-constant deformation of an irreducible plane curve singularity. This is the invariant predicted and computed by Yano's formula.

evidence.quote: for generic fibers of the deformation, the b-exponents

nodes.label: Generic Hodge datum of a variation
nodes.summary: The pair consisting of the generic Mumford-Tate group of the variation and the corresponding Mumford-Tate domain.

evidence.quote: The pair $({\mathbf{G}},D)$ is a connected Hodge datum ... called the generic Hodge datum of ${\mathbb{V}}$

nodes.label: Generic Mumford-Tate group
nodes.summary: The common Mumford-Tate group of Hodge-generic points of an irreducible subvariety Y for the restricted variation.

evidence.quote: Two Hodge-generic points in Yan for V have the same Mumford-Tate group, called the generic Mumford-Tate group MT(Y, V|Y) of Y for V.

nodes.label: Generic region of a degenerating Calabi-Yau
nodes.summary: A large-measure subset of the degenerating Calabi-Yau where the metric is modeled by the semiflat geometry and where the paper constructs a special Lagrangian torus fibration.

evidence.quote: In the generic region on X_s, which covers most of the measure on X_s
on the generic region U_s subset X_s

nodes.label: Generic-region SYZ fibration for the Fermat family
nodes.summary: For a subsequence of Fermat Calabi-Yau hypersurfaces with metrics in the scaled polarization class, the generic region admits a special Lagrangian T^n-fibration and has asymptotically full volume.

evidence.quote: there exists a special Lagrangian T^n-fibration on the generic region U_s subset X_s
Vol(U_s)/Vol(X_s) -> 1 as s -> infinity

nodes.label: Generic-region volume exhaustion
nodes.summary: The property that the generic region occupies asymptotically all of the total Calabi-Yau volume, expressed by Vol(U_s)/Vol(X_s) tending to 1.

evidence.quote: Vol(U_s)/Vol(X_s) -> 1 as s -> infinity
covers most of the measure on X_s

nodes.label: Generic regularity of unbranched multiple covers
nodes.summary: The property that, for generic choices of almost complex structure, unbranched multiple covers of closed holomorphic curves are regular.

evidence.quote: unbranched multiple covers of closed holomorphic curves are generically regular

nodes.label: Geodesic loop basis decomposition
nodes.summary: A method of expressing geodesic loops using a selected basis of loop generators. It is part of the paper's construction of a Riemannian fibration at infinity.

evidence.quote: Decomposing geodesic loops into basis

nodes.label: K-energy slope invariant of a geodesic ray
nodes.summary: For a unit-speed locally finite-energy geodesic ray ρ, the invariant is the limiting one-step increment of K-energy, lim_{k→∞}(K(ρ(k+1))−K(ρ(k))). Convexity of K-energy along finite-energy geodesics makes this limit well-defined.

evidence.quote: U([ρ]) = lim

nodes.label: Geodesic stability for cscK
nodes.summary: A Kähler class is geodesically stable if the K-energy slope invariant is positive for every locally finite-energy geodesic ray from any normalized finite-energy potential; semistability allows nonnegative slope. The paper proves its equivalence with cscK existence under discrete automorphism group.

evidence.quote: U invariant is always strictly positive
geodesic stable

nodes.label: Geometric Frobenius endomorphism
nodes.summary: The Frobenius endomorphism acting on etale cohomology in the trace formula for varieties or stacks over finite fields.

evidence.quote: where $F_{q}$ is the geometric Frobenius endomorphism

nodes.label: Geometric local system
nodes.summary: A local system arising from geometry, typically from the cohomology of a smooth proper family; over the complex numbers such systems are controlled by polarizable integral variations of Hodge structure.

evidence.quote: local system of geometric origin
geometric local systems

nodes.label: Geometric scattering diagram from analytic cylinders
aliases: D associated to U subset Y
nodes.summary: The scattering diagram D consists of pairs (x,f_x) for generic x in hyperplanes n^perp, where f_x is extracted from the wall-crossing transformations defined by infinitesimal analytic cylinder counts. It has finite polyhedral finite-order approximations and is later specialized to a compactification-independent diagram D_U.

evidence.quote: scattering diagram associated to U ⊂ Y
evidence.section: 1.4 Scattering diagram and comparison with GHKK

evidence.quote: counting infinitesimal analytic cylinders
evidence.section: 1.4 Scattering diagram and comparison with GHKK

nodes.label: Geometric section of Milnor cohomology
nodes.summary: A holomorphic section of the Milnor cohomology bundle obtained from a differential form, whose coefficients in period asymptotics define locally constant geometric sections. These sections feed the Varchenko-Brieskorn description of b-exponents.

evidence.quote: these sections will be called geometric sections

nodes.label: Geometric Zilber-Pink property for the positive atypical Hodge locus
nodes.summary: A geometric finiteness-and-alternative statement controlling irreducible components of the Zariski closure of the positive-period atypical Hodge locus by maximal atypical special subvarieties or product-type families with zero-period atypical fibers.

evidence.quote: the maximal atypical special subvarieties of positive period dimension arise in a finite number of families whose geometry we control

nodes.label: GHKK cluster mirror algebra
nodes.summary: The combinatorially defined mirror algebra can(X^vee) with theta function basis from Gross-Hacking-Keel-Kontsevich. In the paper's cluster case, its structure constants agree with the geometric non-archimedean counts and the algebra is identified with A_U, independently of the cluster structure.

evidence.quote: equal to our mirror algebra AU
evidence.section: 1.4 Scattering diagram and comparison with GHKK

evidence.quote: theta function basis, is independent of the cluster structure
evidence.section: 1.4 Scattering diagram and comparison with GHKK

nodes.label: Gibbons-Hawking ansatz
nodes.summary: A construction of 4-dimensional hyperkähler metrics with tri-holomorphic S1 symmetry from a positive harmonic function on a flat 3-manifold and a circle-bundle connection with curvature *dV.

evidence.quote: This is called the Gibbons-Hawking ansatz
evidence.section: 2. The Gibbons-Hawking ansatz and the model space

evidence.quote: 4-dimensional hyperkähler structures with an S1 symmetry
evidence.section: 2. The Gibbons-Hawking ansatz and the model space

nodes.label: Gibbons-Hawking neck region
aliases: doubly-periodic cousin of the Ooguri-Vafa metric
nodes.summary: An incomplete hyperkähler 4-manifold constructed from a harmonic function with monopole points on T2 x R. It is the transition region glued between two Tian-Yau ends.

evidence.quote: construct such a transition region
Our neck metric is instead constructed by applying the Gibbons-Hawking ansatz

nodes.label: GL^+(2,R) action on translation surfaces
nodes.summary: The orientation-preserving linear group GL^+(2,R) acts on translation surfaces by postcomposing translation charts with real linear maps. Orbit closures for this action are the invariant subvarieties studied in the paper.

evidence.quote: $GL^{+}(2,\mathbb{R})$ orbit closures of translation surfaces
$GL^{+}(2,\mathbb{R})$-invariant subvarieties

nodes.label: Global monodromy group of a local system
aliases: monodromy group of a local system
nodes.summary: The image of the fundamental-group representation associated to a local system. In this paper finite global monodromy is a key restriction on rigid local systems.

evidence.quote: the image of  rho  is called the (global) monodromy group of the local system
including those with finite global monodromy

nodes.label: Global Springer theory for Hitchin fibrations
nodes.summary: Yun's global analogue of Springer theory in the setting of Hitchin-type fibrations, using parabolic Hitchin geometry and Springer symmetries on direct-image complexes. The paper applies it to prove the required perversity statement.

evidence.quote: to global Springer theory in the sense of Yun
Global Springer theory

nodes.label: Good tangent sheaf splitting
nodes.summary: A splitting of the relative tangent sheaf into summands E^(i) and P such that the sum of the E^(i) is a foliation and each E^(i) is symplectic or dual to another summand.

evidence.quote: good
evidence.section: 2 Locally trivial deformations along foliations and resolutions

nodes.label: Grade number and purity for D-modules
nodes.summary: The grade number is the smallest Ext degree in which a module has nonzero Ext; a module is k-pure if every nonzero submodule has grade k. Purity is used in injectivity arguments for parameter localizations.

evidence.quote: grade number of N
The module N is pure, or k-pure

nodes.label: Graded pieces of the filtered de Rham complex
nodes.summary: The subquotient complexes obtained from the Hodge filtration on the de Rham complex. These graded pieces are used to express pushforwards of differential forms on a resolution.

evidence.quote: The p-th subquotient of this filtration is the complex of O_Y-modules gr_F^p DR(M)

nodes.label: Graded trace on cohomology
nodes.summary: The trace of an endomorphism on a graded cohomology theory, taken with the grading convention used in Lefschetz trace formulas.

evidence.quote: $\operatorname{Tr}$ denotes the graded trace

nodes.label: Gradient-volume ratio estimates for cscK
aliases: F lower bound and gradient estimate
nodes.summary: Pointwise estimates relating the potential, its gradient, and the logarithmic volume ratio: a C0 potential bound gives a lower bound for F and controls |grad phi|^2/e^F, while a gradient bound controls F from above.

evidence.quote: \frac{|\nabla\varphi|^{2}}{e^{F}}\leq C
evidence.section: 2. The volume ratio and C1 bound on Kähler potential

nodes.label: Grauert-Riemenschneider sheaf
nodes.summary: For a resolution r : Xtilde -> X, the sheaf omega_X^GR = r_* omega_Xtilde. Reflexivity of this sheaf defines weakly rational singularities in the paper.

evidence.quote: Here omega_X^GR = r_* omega_X̃ is sometimes called the Grauert-Riemenschneider sheaf

nodes.label: Gravitational instanton
nodes.summary: In this paper, a gravitational instanton is a complete hyperkähler four-manifold satisfying faster than quadratic curvature decay.

evidence.quote: we define a gravitational instanton as a complete, hyperkähler 4-manifold satisfying a decay condition

nodes.label: End structure of a gravitational instanton
nodes.summary: The differential, metric, and holomorphic structure of the noncompact end of a gravitational instanton. The paper studies these structures beyond just the tangent cone at infinity.

evidence.quote: What are the differential and metric structures of the infinity of these gravitational instantons?
the crucial point is to understand "the end" holomorphically

nodes.label: Greatest twisted Ricci lower bound
nodes.summary: The supremal Ricci lower-bound parameter compatible with the twisting current θ in the polarized class. The paper identifies this analytic invariant with a valuative stability threshold.

evidence.quote: the greatest (twisted) Ricci lower bound
The stability threshold and the greatest Ricci lower bound

nodes.label: Griffiths bundle
nodes.summary: The universal rational line bundle on the period quotient given by the tensor product of determinant Hodge bundles, with positivity along Griffiths-transverse directions.

evidence.quote: the Griffiths bundle)  $L \coloneqq \bigotimes_{i} \det F^{i}$  which exists universally on  $\Gamma \backslash \Omega$  as a  $\mathbb{Q}$ -bundle

nodes.label: Griffiths period-image quasi-projectivity conjecture
nodes.summary: The assertion that the closure of the image of a Griffiths-transverse period map to an arithmetic quotient of a period domain is a quasi-projective algebraic variety; the paper proves this through algebraic factorization and ampleness of the Griffiths bundle.

evidence.quote: the closure of the image of a period map  $\varphi : X^{\mathrm{an}} \to \Gamma \backslash \Omega$  as above was conjectured by Griffiths ... to be a quasi-projective algebraic variety

nodes.label: Griffiths transversality
nodes.summary: The condition that the derivative of a period map lies in the horizontal distribution determined by the Hodge filtration on the period-domain quotient.

evidence.quote: The period map satisfies Griffiths transversality: the derivative lands in a naturally defined distribution

nodes.label: Griffiths-transverse period map
aliases: period map on an algebraic space
nodes.summary: A complex analytic map from the analytification of a reduced separated finite-type complex algebraic space to an arithmetic quotient of a period domain, locally liftable to the period domain and satisfying Griffiths transversality on the regular locus.

evidence.quote: we define a period map  $\varphi : X^{\mathrm{an}} \to \Gamma \backslash \Omega$  to be a complex analytic map which locally lifts to  $\Omega$  and which satisfies Griffiths transversality

nodes.label: Gromov-Hausdorff compactification of hyperkähler K3 moduli
nodes.summary: The compactification of the unit-diameter hyperkähler K3 moduli space by adding Gromov-Hausdorff limit spaces, including both non-collapsed hyperkähler orbifold limits and collapsed limits.

evidence.quote: understanding the full Gromov-Hausdorff compactification  $\overline{\mathfrak{M}}$
obtained by adding the volume-non-collapsing Gromov-Hausdorff limits

nodes.label: Gromov-Hausdorff convergence
nodes.summary: A metric notion of convergence for compact metric spaces used to describe limits of the Calabi-Yau metric spaces in the degeneration.

evidence.quote: converge in the Gromov-Hausdorff sense
Any subsequential Gromov-Hausdorff limit of the CY metrics
endowed with the Gromov-Hausdorff topology
Gromov-Hausdorff limits of hyperkähler metrics

nodes.label: Gromov hyperbolic surface
nodes.summary: A metric hyperbolicity property of the intrinsic metric on a surface, used by the paper as a characterization of quasiperiodic maximal surfaces.

evidence.quote: characterised by a curvature condition, Gromov hyperbolicity or conformal hyperbolicity
6.4. Gromov hyperbolicity

nodes.label: Gromov product control
nodes.summary: Estimates on Gromov products used to construct a quasisymmetric boundary map extending the uniformization of a quasiperiodic surface.

evidence.quote: 7.1. Control of Gromov products
7.2. Construction of a quasisymmetric map

nodes.label: Gromov-Witten invariant
nodes.summary: A curve-counting invariant defined through moduli spaces of pseudoholomorphic curves, used here as an application of super-rigidity.

evidence.quote: Gromov-Witten invariants

nodes.label: Half-harmonic 1-form
nodes.summary: A 1-form satisfying the first-order equations d*phi = 0 and d+phi = 0; these forms appear in the kernel analysis for the linearized hyperkähler operator on Tian-Yau spaces.

evidence.quote: 1-forms φ satisfying d∗φ = 0, d+φ = 0

nodes.label: Half-line exterior differential mapping method
aliases: mapping properties of d on a half-line
nodes.summary: The local analytic method using mapping properties of the exterior derivative on a half-line to compute weighted L2 cohomology of QFB/QFC local models. It replaces the warped-product Kunneth approach unavailable for QFC metrics.

evidence.quote: mapping properties of the exterior differential on a half-line

nodes.label: Harder-Narasimhan filtration
nodes.summary: The canonical filtration of a torsion-free sheaf whose successive quotients are slope semistable with strictly decreasing slopes. The optimality function uses the first and last HN quotient slopes.

evidence.quote: Harder-Narasimhan filtration

nodes.label: Harmonic function with monopole points
aliases: doubly periodic harmonic function with poles
nodes.summary: A harmonic function on T2 x R, or equivalently a doubly periodic harmonic function on R2 x R, with finitely many pole singularities called monopole points; it defines the neck metric through the Gibbons-Hawking ansatz.

evidence.quote: harmonic function on the flat cylinder T2 ×R with finitely many poles
which we call the monopole points

nodes.label: Hasse-Weil zeta function
nodes.summary: A generating function encoding the numbers of finite-field points of a variety or stack over all finite extensions of a base finite field.

evidence.quote: Hasse-Weil zeta functions

nodes.label: Hasse-Weil zeta-function sieve
aliases: sieve based on Hasse-Weil zeta functions
nodes.summary: A point-counting method that uses Hasse-Weil zeta functions, together with sieve or stratification arguments, to compute finite-field point counts of moduli spaces.

evidence.quote: using a sieve based on Hasse-Weil zeta functions

nodes.label: Hausdorff dimension
nodes.summary: A fractal dimension for subsets of metric spaces, used to measure Julia sets and parameter sets in the quadratic family.

evidence.quote: positive Hausdorff dimension

nodes.label: 3-dimensional Heisenberg group
nodes.summary: The nilpotent Lie group of real upper triangular 3 by 3 matrices with ones on the diagonal, used to define Heisenberg nilmanifolds as compact quotients.

evidence.quote: the 3-dimensional Heisenberg group is H(1, R)

nodes.label: Heisenberg nilmanifold
nodes.summary: A compact quotient of the 3-dimensional Heisenberg group; equivalently in this paper, the total space of a nontrivial degree-b circle fibration over T2.

evidence.quote: a 3-dimensional Heisenberg nilmanifold
Nil3_b is the total space of a degree b circle fibration

nodes.label: Degree of a Heisenberg nilmanifold
nodes.summary: The integer degree of the circle fibration Nil3_b -> T2, well-defined up to sign unless a projection to an oriented T2 is fixed.

evidence.quote: degree is a well-defined integer
degree b circle fibration

nodes.label: Hermitian-Yang-Mills metric
nodes.summary: A Hermitian metric whose Chern connection satisfies the Hermitian-Yang-Mills equation with respect to a Kähler metric, equivalently the Kähler contraction of curvature is a scalar endomorphism.

evidence.quote: Hermitian-Yang-Mills (HYM) metric

nodes.label: Higgs bundle on a Riemann surface
nodes.summary: A holomorphic vector bundle on a Riemann surface together with a Higgs field. It is the Higgs-bundle side of the nonabelian Hodge correspondence used by the paper.

evidence.quote: Higgs bundles and local systems

nodes.label: High-rank invariant subvariety
nodes.summary: An invariant subvariety of genus g translation surfaces has high rank when its rank is at least g/2+1. This is the paper's main hypothesis for the classification result.

evidence.quote: We say that $\mathcal{M}$ has high rank if $\mathrm{rank}(\mathcal{M}) \geq \frac{g}{2} + 1$

nodes.label: Higher direct image sheaf
nodes.summary: The derived pushforward sheaf R^i r_* F associated to a holomorphic map and a sheaf F. Vanishing of such sheaves appears in rational singularities and local vanishing.

evidence.quote: R^i r_* O_X̃ = 0 for every i >= 1

nodes.label: Higher rank Teichmuller space
nodes.summary: An open-and-closed component or subset of a higher-rank character variety consisting entirely of discrete faithful representations, typically arising from closed Anosov or positive Anosov loci. The paper proposes Cayley loci as such spaces.

evidence.quote: higher rank Teichmüller spaces

nodes.label: Hilbert-Chow morphism for sheaf moduli
nodes.summary: The Hilbert-Chow-type morphism associated with the moduli space of certain torsion sheaves on an abelian surface. The paper studies the Hitchin fibration as a degeneration of this morphism.

evidence.quote: the Hilbert-Chow morphism associated with the moduli space of certain torsion sheaves

nodes.label: Hilbert scheme of two points on a K3 surface
aliases: Hilbert square of a K3 surface
nodes.summary: The Hilbert scheme parameterizing length-two zero-dimensional subschemes of a K3 surface; it is the model object for K3[2] deformation type.

evidence.quote: K3[2] deformation type

nodes.label: Hilbert scheme of points on C^2
aliases: Hilbert scheme of n points on C^2
nodes.summary: The Hilbert scheme of n points on the complex plane, here in the centered form Hilb_0^n(C^2). It is the moduli space carrying the Nakajima metric in the Vafa-Witten application.

evidence.quote: Hilbert scheme of n points on C^2

nodes.label: Hitchin base
nodes.summary: The affine vector space formed from holomorphic differentials on C that receives characteristic polynomials of Higgs fields. It is the target of the Hitchin fibration.

evidence.quote: \Lambda = \bigoplus_{i = 1}^{n} H^0(C,\Omega_C^{\otimes i})

nodes.label: Hitchin fibration
nodes.summary: The projective morphism h:M_Dol->Lambda with connected fibers that sends a Higgs bundle to the characteristic polynomial of its Higgs field. In this paper its perverse filtration is compared with the weight filtration on the character variety.

evidence.quote: The proper morphism (2), called the Hitchin fibration
which is referred to as the Hitchin system
The Hitchin map h is surjective and proper; it is also Lagrangian

nodes.label: Hitchin-Li-Mochizuki section
nodes.summary: A section associated with the universal Hitchin map in the paper's Teichmüller-space discussion, named from Hitchin and Li-Mochizuki constructions.

evidence.quote: 9.5. The Hitchin-Li-Mochizuki section
Complete solutions of Toda equations and cyclic Higgs bundles over non-compact surfaces

nodes.label: Hodge-bundle curvature obstruction to semistability
nodes.summary: The curvature behavior of Hodge bundles associated with polarizable variations of Hodge structure, used to explain why the underlying flat bundles are rarely parabolically semistable.

evidence.quote: flat vector bundles underlying polarizable variations are rarely (parabolically) semistable, due to well-known curvature properties of Hodge bundles

nodes.label: Hodge-bundle intersection formula for differential strata
aliases: intersection formula for Masur-Veech volumes and Siegel-Veech constants
nodes.summary: A method expressing Masur-Veech volumes and Siegel-Veech constants of strata of holomorphic or quadratic differentials as intersection numbers of classes on compactified projectivized Hodge bundles.

evidence.quote: write Masur-Veech volumes and Siegel-Veech constants for strata of holomorphic and quadratic differentials also through intersection numbers

nodes.label: Hodge class
nodes.summary: For a pure integral Hodge structure H, a Hodge class is an integral class whose complexification lies in F^0, equivalently a morphism of Hodge structures Z(0) -> H.

evidence.quote: a Hodge class in a pure Z-Hodge structure H = (HZ, F•) is a class in HZ whose image in HC lies in F0HC, or equivalently a morphism of Hodge structures Z(0) → H
a Hodge class of a pure $\mathbb{Z}$-Hodge structure $V = (V_{\mathbb{Z}}, F^{\bullet})$ is a class in $V_{\mathbb{Q}}$ whose image in $V_{\mathbb{C}}$ lies in the zeroth piece $F^0 V_{\mathbb{C}}$

nodes.label: Hodge codimension
nodes.summary: For a subvariety, the codimension of its period image inside the special closure determined by its generic Mumford-Tate group. It measures whether the intersection with a special subvariety is larger than expected.

evidence.quote: with weakly special and special closures ... We define the Hodge codimension of $Y$ for ${\mathbb{V}}$
evidence.section: 4.1 Two notions of typicality and atypicality

nodes.label: Hodge-de Rham operator
nodes.summary: The first-order differential operator d plus the formal adjoint of d acting on differential forms. Fredholm and parametrix properties of this operator control harmonic forms in QFB and QFC geometries.

evidence.quote: Hodge-deRham operator is Fredholm

nodes.label: Hodge filtration
nodes.summary: The increasing good filtration on the D-module underlying a Hodge or mixed Hodge module. Its graded pieces of the de Rham complex are used to identify sheaves of differential forms.

evidence.quote: an increasing good filtration F_M by coherent O_Y-modules, called the Hodge filtration

nodes.label: Hodge-generic point
nodes.summary: A point of an irreducible subvariety is Hodge-generic when the Mumford-Tate group of its fiber has maximal possible dimension along that subvariety.

evidence.quote: a point s of Yan is said to be Hodge-generic in Y for V if MT(Vs,Q) has maximal dimension when s ranges through Yan

nodes.label: Hodge generic subvariety of a ball quotient
nodes.summary: A subvariety W of S_Γ not contained in any strict special subvariety. The paper uses this notion for monodromy consequences and thin subgroup constructions.

evidence.quote: not contained in any strict special subvariety

nodes.label: Hodge index negativity for Picard-Manin classes
nodes.summary: A Hodge-index-type sign property for the intersection form on Sigma-controlled b-divisor classes. It supplies the quadratic-form control used in the spectral analysis.

evidence.quote: We prove a version of the Hodge index theorem
Hodge-index theorem on  $\mathcal{F}_{\Sigma}(\mathcal{X})$

nodes.label: Hodge-Lie algebra
nodes.summary: A reductive finite-dimensional Lie algebra over Q or R endowed with a weight-zero Hodge structure compatible with the Lie bracket and whose adjoint part is polarized by the Killing form.

evidence.quote: A $K$-Hodge-Lie algebra is a reductive (finite dimensional) Lie algebra $\mathfrak{g}$ over $K$ endowed with a $K$-Hodge structure of weight zero such that the Lie bracket ... is a morphism of $K$-Hodge structures

nodes.label: Hodge locus
nodes.summary: For a variation of Hodge structure, the Hodge locus is the set of base points whose fiber has more Hodge classes than the very general fiber.

evidence.quote: The Hodge locus HL(S, V) is the set of points s ∈ San for which the Hodge structure Vs admits more Hodge classes than the very general fiber

The Hodge locus $\mathrm{HL}(S, \mathbb{V}^{\otimes})$ is the subset of points $s \in S^{\mathrm{an}}$ for which the Hodge structure $\mathbb{V}_s$ admits more Hodge tensors than the very general fiber

nodes.label: Decomposition theorem for Hodge modules
nodes.summary: For projective morphisms, the direct image of a polarizable Hodge module decomposes into its cohomological Hodge-module summands, with corresponding filtered D-module decomposition. The paper uses it to relate differential forms on a resolution to the intersection complex.

evidence.quote: a version of the Decomposition Theorem for those filtered D-modules that underlie polarisable Hodge modules

nodes.label: Hodge morphism between connected Hodge varieties
nodes.summary: A complex analytic map between connected Hodge varieties induced by a morphism of the corresponding Hodge data.

evidence.quote: a Hodge morphism between connected Hodge varieties is the complex analytic map deduced from a morphism of the corresponding Hodge data
evidence.section: 3.1.1 Weakly special subvarieties of Hodge varieties

nodes.label: Hodge tensor
nodes.summary: A Hodge class appearing in tensor constructions generated by a Hodge structure and its dual; these tensors are the fixed tensors used to describe Mumford-Tate groups and tensor Hodge loci.

evidence.quote: let V⊗ be the countable direct sum of polarizable ZVHSs ... V⊗a ⊗ (V∨)⊗b
a Hodge tensor for $V$ is a Hodge class in $V^{\otimes} := \bigoplus_{a, b \in \mathbb{N}} V^{\otimes a} \otimes (V^{\vee})^{\otimes b}$

nodes.label: Hölder modulus of continuity
nodes.summary: A regularity property asserting that a map satisfies a uniform Hölder-type continuity bound. The paper proves this property for quasisymmetric maps to the Einstein Universe.

evidence.quote: they enjoy a uniform Holder modulus of continuity
3.5.Holder property

nodes.label: Holomorphic automorphism
nodes.summary: A biholomorphic self-map of a complex space or manifold. Fiber-preserving holomorphic automorphisms are the approximants produced by the relative Andersen-Lempert method in the proof.

evidence.quote: holomorphic automorphisms

nodes.label: Holomorphic cover of Riemann surfaces
nodes.summary: A holomorphic map between Riemann surfaces of finite degree, used to form multiple covers of curves by composition.

evidence.quote: cover of closed connected Riemann surfaces with degree d

nodes.label: Holomorphic form extension to a resolution
nodes.summary: The property that a holomorphic p-form on the regular locus extends to a holomorphic p-form on a resolution of singularities. The paper gives intrinsic criteria and consequences for this property.

evidence.quote: Which holomorphic p-forms on the regular locus Xreg extend to holomorphic p-forms on the complex manifold X̃?

nodes.label: Holomorphic function construction on gravitational instantons
nodes.summary: A method for producing holomorphic functions on gravitational instantons using weighted Hilbert spaces and elliptic estimates. These functions are used to study the end holomorphically and to compactify ALG and ALH non-splitting cases.

evidence.quote: The construction of holomorphic functions
the crucial point is to understand "the end" holomorphically

nodes.label: Holomorphic function germ
nodes.summary: An equivalence class near the origin of a holomorphic map f: (C^{n+1},0) -> (C,0). It is the ambient input for Bernstein-Sato, Milnor-fiber, and singularity constructions in the paper.

evidence.quote: Let f: (C^{n+1}, 0) -> (C, 0) be a germ of a holomorphic function

nodes.label: Holomorphic line bundle O(4) over CP^1
nodes.summary: The fourth tensor power of the hyperplane line bundle over CP^1. The paper considers its total space as the target of a twistor-space map.

evidence.quote: the total space of the O(4) bundle over CP^1

nodes.label: Holomorphic p-form on the regular locus
nodes.summary: A section of the sheaf of holomorphic p-forms over the smooth locus of a complex space. These are the forms whose extension is studied.

evidence.quote: A holomorphic p-form alpha in H^0(Xreg, Omega_X^p)

nodes.label: Holomorphic submersion
nodes.summary: A holomorphic map between complex spaces that is submersive in the holomorphic sense. The paper's relative Oka results, sprays, and fibered density property are all formulated for holomorphic submersions.

evidence.quote: pi:Y to B be a holomorphic submersion
evidence.section: 2. Dominating sprays and the Oka property

nodes.label: Oka property for holomorphic submersions
nodes.summary: A relative Oka property for a holomorphic submersion, expressed by approximation and lifting of maps while preserving the projection to the base. For the submersion to a point, it recovers the Oka property of the total space.

evidence.quote: enjoys the Oka property
evidence.section: 2. Dominating sprays and the Oka property

nodes.label: Holomorphic symplectic form
nodes.summary: A nondegenerate holomorphic two-form, interpreted in the singular setting as a reflexive two-form on a normal variety. It generates the reflexive-form algebra of an IHS variety.

evidence.quote: holomorphic symplectic form
carries a nowhere degenerate holomorphic 2-form (called a symplectic form) unique up to multiplication by a nonzero constant

nodes.label: Holomorphic vector bundle
nodes.summary: A vector bundle in the holomorphic category over a complex manifold or space. It is used in the definitions of elliptic manifolds and elliptic holomorphic submersions.

evidence.quote: holomorphic vector bundle E on Y

nodes.label: Holomorphic vector field
nodes.summary: A holomorphic section of the holomorphic tangent bundle of a complex manifold. The density property is formulated using the Lie algebra of such vector fields.

evidence.quote: holomorphic vector fields on Y

nodes.label: Holomorphic volume form
nodes.summary: A nowhere-vanishing holomorphic top-degree form on a Calabi-Yau manifold, used in the Calabi-Yau structure and in the definition of special Lagrangian geometry.

evidence.quote: (X_s, g_s, J_s, omega_s, Omega_s)

nodes.label: Holomorphic volume form with simple pole along the divisor
nodes.summary: A global holomorphic n-form on X\D with a simple pole along D, normalized by the residue form on D. It is used to normalize the Tian-Yau metric and compare it with the normal-bundle model.

evidence.quote: We have a global holomorphic n-form Ω on X with a simple pole along D, normalized by ϖ = ResDΩ

nodes.label: Holonomy control of geodesic loops
nodes.summary: Estimates or structural control on the holonomy associated with geodesic loops. The paper uses this as part of the asymptotic fibration construction.

evidence.quote: Control of holonomy of geodesic loops

nodes.label: Holonomy double-cover locus
nodes.summary: The holonomy double-cover locus consists of translation surfaces obtained as holonomy double covers of surfaces lying in a stratum of quadratic differentials. This locus is one of the two possible outcomes in the high-rank classification.

evidence.quote: the locus of all holonomy double covers of surfaces in a stratum of quadratic differentials

nodes.label: Horizontal distribution on a period quotient
nodes.summary: The natural distribution on an arithmetic quotient of a period domain that specifies the allowed tangent directions for variations of Hodge structure.

evidence.quote: the derivative lands in a naturally defined distribution on  $\Gamma \backslash \Omega$

nodes.label: Horofunction boundary method
nodes.summary: The use of horofunctions and their bounds to prove rigidity or characterize quasiperiodicity for maximal surfaces.

evidence.quote: 5.2. Horofunction Rigidity
6.2. Horofunction bounds

nodes.label: Huybrechts-Riemann-Roch polynomial
nodes.summary: For a hyper-Kähler manifold, the polynomial expressing the holomorphic Euler characteristic of a line bundle as a function of the Beauville-Bogomolov square of its first Chern class.

evidence.quote: the Huybrechts-Riemann-Roch polynomial of the hyper-Kähler fourfold
Computing Riemann-Roch polynomials

nodes.label: Hybrid conjugacy
nodes.summary: A quasiconformal conjugacy between quadratic-like maps that is conformal almost everywhere on the filled Julia set.

evidence.quote: hybrid conjugate to a unique quadratic map

nodes.label: HYM cone connection
nodes.summary: A Hermitian-Yang-Mills connection on C^n with radial cone structure, encoded by a polystable reflexive sheaf on CP^{n-1} through the projective sheaf cone construction.

evidence.quote: A_infty is a HYM cone connection
uniquely determined by the sheaf

nodes.label: Hyper-Kähler deformation type
nodes.summary: An equivalence class of hyper-Kähler manifolds under deformation through hyper-Kähler manifolds. The paper's classification identifies when a fourfold belongs to the K3[2] deformation type.

evidence.quote: Any hyper-Kähler manifold can be deformed into a hyper-Kähler manifold with a Lagrangian fibration.
K3[2] deformation type

nodes.label: Hyper-Kähler fourfold
nodes.summary: A hyper-Kähler manifold of complex dimension four. The paper classifies such manifolds under a topological numerical hypothesis.

evidence.quote: We prove that a hyper-Kähler fourfold satisfying a mild topological assumption is of K3[2] deformation type.

nodes.label: Hyper-Kähler manifold
nodes.summary: A simply connected smooth compact Kähler manifold carrying a nowhere degenerate holomorphic 2-form that is unique up to multiplication by a nonzero constant.

evidence.quote: A hyper-Kähler manifold is a simply connected smooth compact Kähler manifold which carries a nowhere degenerate holomorphic 2-form (called a symplectic form) unique up to multiplication by a nonzero constant.

nodes.label: Hyper-Kähler SYZ conjecture
nodes.summary: The assertion that any nontrivial nef line bundle on a hyper-Kähler manifold whose first Chern class has vanishing top self-intersection is semi-ample.

evidence.quote: Any nontrivial nef line bundle L on X such that ∫_X c_1(L)^{2n} = 0 is semi-ample.

nodes.label: Hyperbolic dimension
nodes.summary: For a rational map, the supremal Hausdorff dimension of its hyperbolic invariant subsets; the paper contrasts it with the Hausdorff dimension of the whole Julia set.

evidence.quote: hyperbolic dimension is different

nodes.label: Hyperbolic-Hausdorff dimension gap
nodes.summary: The phenomenon that the hyperbolic dimension of a rational map is strictly smaller than the Hausdorff dimension of its Julia set, realized here by positive-area Feigenbaum examples.

evidence.quote: gap between the Hausdorff dimensions

nodes.label: Hyperbolic pointed curve
nodes.summary: A pointed smooth proper connected curve (C,D) satisfying 2g-2+n>0, equivalently the cases g>=2, or g=1 with n>0, or g=0 with n>2.

evidence.quote: Call (C, D) hyperbolic if C is a smooth proper connected curve and either g >= 2 and n >= 0, g = 1 and n > 0, or g = 0 and n > 2

nodes.label: Period of a hyperkähler K3 metric
nodes.summary: The oriented maximal positive subspace of H^2(K;R) determined by the self-dual harmonic forms of a hyperkähler K3 metric.

evidence.quote: a hyperkähler metric  $g$  has a period
the element in the Grassmannian of oriented maximal positive subspaces

nodes.label: Hyperkähler metric
nodes.summary: A Riemannian metric with compatible quaternionic Kähler structures; in the paper Ricci-flat Kähler metrics on K3 are treated through their hyperkähler property.

evidence.quote: they are in fact hyperkähler
relying crucially on their hyperkähler property

nodes.label: Hyperkähler metric on a four-manifold
nodes.summary: A Riemannian metric on a smooth four-manifold whose holonomy is contained in SU(2) inside SO(4). Equivalently, after choosing an orientation, the bundle of self-dual 2-forms is trivialized by parallel sections.

evidence.quote: is hyperkähler if its holonomy group is contained in
the bundle  $\Lambda^{+}X$  of self-dual 2-forms is trivialized by parallel sections

nodes.label: Hyperkähler triple
aliases: triple of symplectic forms defining a hyperkähler metric
nodes.summary: A definite triple of closed 2-forms whose normalized coefficient matrix is the identity; it determines a hyperkähler metric and is the variable glued and perturbed in the construction.

evidence.quote: is called a hyperkähler triple if dω1 = dω2 = dω3 = 0
then gω is a hyperkähler metric
Such a triple is called a hyperkähler triple.
a hyperkähler triple uniquely determines a hyperkähler Riemannian metric

nodes.label: Hyperkähler triple perturbation method
nodes.summary: The analytic method that corrects an approximately hyperkähler triple by solving a gauge-fixed nonlinear elliptic system, producing an actual hyperkähler triple and hence a Ricci-flat Kähler metric.

evidence.quote: perturb to obtain a hyperkähler triple
find a triple of closed 2-forms θ

nodes.label: Identity component of the orthogonal group
nodes.summary: The identity component of the orthogonal group preserving the quadratic form q. In this setting it acts transitively by isometries on pseudo-hyperbolic space.

evidence.quote: Let  $\mathsf{G} \coloneqq \mathsf{SO}_0(\mathbf{q})$  be the identity component of  $\mathcal{O}(\mathbf{q})$
$\mathsf{G}$  acts transitively by isometries on  $\mathbf{H}^{2,n}$

nodes.label: Image of compactly supported cohomology in de Rham cohomology
nodes.summary: The image of the natural map from compactly supported de Rham cohomology to ordinary de Rham cohomology. The Sen and Vafa-Witten conjectures are reformulated as identifying this image with Hodge cohomology.

evidence.quote: Im[H_c^q -> H^q]

nodes.label: Infinite monodromy local system
nodes.summary: A local system whose monodromy representation has infinite image.

evidence.quote: geometric local systems of rank less than 2sqrt(g + 1) with infinite monodromy

nodes.label: Infinitely renormalizable map
nodes.summary: A map for which all successive renormalizations R^j f are defined for every nonnegative j.

evidence.quote: well defined for all j

nodes.label: Infinitesimal analytic cylinder count
nodes.summary: For a generic point x on a hyperplane n^perp and tangent data v,w, the count N(V_{x,v,w},alpha) counts analytic curves associated to an infinitesimal spine with two ends and a bend at x. These counts are the geometric input for wall-crossing and scattering functions.

evidence.quote: infinitesimal spine with two ends
evidence.section: 1.4 Scattering diagram and comparison with GHKK

evidence.quote: associated count of analytic curves N(Vx,v,w, α)
evidence.section: 1.4 Scattering diagram and comparison with GHKK

nodes.label: Inou-Shishikura class
nodes.summary: A compact holomorphic class of maps near parabolic fixed points, invariant under parabolic or almost parabolic renormalization and used to control perturbations of high-type Siegel maps.

evidence.quote: Inou-Shishikura class

nodes.label: Integer translate to a hypersurface component
aliases: translation by Z^r into codimension one
nodes.summary: A higher-codimension irreducible component of a Bernstein-Sato zero locus can be shifted by an integer vector so that it lies inside a codimension-one component. This is the paper's structural control over non-hypersurface components.

evidence.quote: codimension > 1 can be translated by an element of Z^r

nodes.label: Integral Apollonian circle packing
nodes.summary: An Apollonian circle packing generated by an integral Descartes quadruple; the Descartes equation then forces all curvatures in the packing to be integers.

evidence.quote: all curvatures in the packing are integral

nodes.label: Integral complex local system
nodes.summary: A complex local system whose monodromy data satisfy an integrality condition, such as traces or characteristic polynomials lying in algebraic integers. This is the target property in Simpson's integrality conjecture.

evidence.quote: Simpson's integrality conjecture

nodes.label: Integral trace property for complex hyperbolic lattices
nodes.summary: After passing to finite index, traces of Ad(γ) for γ in a complex hyperbolic lattice lie in the ring of integers of the trace field, equivalently the lattice lies in G(O_K). This integrality is used to construct the paper's integral VHS.

evidence.quote: Γ′ lies in G(O_K)

nodes.label: Integral VHS on a complex hyperbolic ball quotient
nodes.summary: The polarised integral variation of Hodge structure on S_Γ constructed from the complex Hodge structure associated with the lattice and its real embeddings. It realizes S_Γ inside a period domain and is the Hodge-theoretic backbone of the paper.

evidence.quote: natural structure of a polarised Z-VHS
evidence.section: 4.2. Weil restrictions after Deligne and Simpson

nodes.label: Intermediate K-semistable cone at infinity
nodes.summary: The possible intermediate normal affine cone W occurring between X and its asymptotic Calabi-Yau cone in the two-step degeneration. The main result proves that this intermediate cone is not genuinely distinct from the asymptotic cone.

evidence.quote: via a possible intermediate K-semistable cone W

nodes.label: Intersection cohomology
nodes.summary: A cohomology theory for stratified spaces depending on a perversity, such as lower or upper middle perversity. The paper identifies certain weighted QFC cohomology groups with intersection cohomology of the associated stratified space.

evidence.quote: intersection cohomology group of lower middle perversity

nodes.label: Intersection complex
nodes.summary: The perverse sheaf underlying the polarizable Hodge module determined by the constant variation of Hodge structure on the regular locus. It is intrinsic to the singular space and controls the extension sheaves in the proof.

evidence.quote: Its underlying perverse sheaf is the intersection complex of X
evidence.section: 8. Pure Hodge modules and differentials on the resolution

nodes.label: Intersection number of degree-two classes
nodes.summary: A numerical invariant obtained by integrating cup products of degree-two cohomology classes over the manifold, such as the top self-intersection of a first Chern class.

evidence.quote: satisfying certain numerical intersection conditions

nodes.label: Intersection pairing on Sigma b-divisors
nodes.summary: A bilinear intersection operation on Sigma-controlled b-divisors, with values in codimension-two Weil numerical b-classes. It is the intersection-theoretic input for the Hodge-index property.

evidence.quote: The pairing  $\mathcal{F}_{\Sigma}(\mathcal{X})\times \mathcal{F}_{\Sigma}(\mathcal{X})\to \mathrm{w - N}^2 (\mathcal{X})$

nodes.label: Intersection product of numerical cycles
nodes.summary: The multilinear pairing on numerical cycle classes used to produce numerical degrees such as (f^* omega^k . omega^{d-k}).

evidence.quote: as the intersection product  $\deg_{k,\omega}(f)$

nodes.label: Interval collapse of Ricci-flat K3 metrics
nodes.summary: A degeneration in which a family of Ricci-flat Kähler or hyperkähler metrics on a K3 surface converges in the Gromov-Hausdorff sense to a closed interval, with regular nilmanifold fibers and Tian-Yau or Taub-NUT bubbles.

evidence.quote: collapse to an interval
collapse to the standard metric on the closed interval [0, 1]
(dimension 1) a 1-dimensional unit interval

nodes.label: Intrinsic curvature of a surface
nodes.summary: The Gaussian curvature of the induced metric on a spacelike surface. The paper uses pointwise curvature conditions to characterize rigidity and quasiperiodicity of complete maximal surfaces.

evidence.quote: any complete maximal surface has non positive induced curvature
If the intrinsic curvature of  $\Sigma$  is zero at a point

nodes.label: Intrinsic wedge extension criterion for forms
nodes.summary: A p-form on the regular locus extends holomorphically precisely when alpha wedge beta and d alpha wedge gamma extend as n-forms for all local Kahler differentials beta and gamma. The logarithmic version replaces holomorphic n-form extension by log n-form extension.

evidence.quote: alpha wedge beta and d alpha wedge gamma on Ureg extend to holomorphic n-forms on r^{-1}(U)

nodes.label: Invariance properties of twisted double ramification cycles
nodes.summary: Structural invariance properties describing how twisted double ramification cycles behave under operations such as dualizing, adding unweighted markings, translating weights, twisting by pullback or vertical line bundles, and partial stabilization.

evidence.quote: Invariance properties
Dualizing; Unweighted markings; Weight translation; Twisting by pullback; Vertical twisting; Partial stabilization

nodes.label: Invariant subvariety of a translation-surface stratum
nodes.summary: An invariant subvariety is an irreducible closed GL^+(2,R)-invariant subvariety of a stratum of translation surfaces; by orbit-closure theorems these are exactly GL^+(2,R) orbit closures.

evidence.quote: all irreducible closed $GL^{+}(2,\mathbb{R})$-invariant subvarieties of strata of translation surfaces, or invariant subvarieties for short
are $GL^{+}(2,\mathbb{R})$ orbit closures

nodes.label: Irreducible Calabi-Yau variety
nodes.summary: A compact Kähler variety with rational singularities whose reflexive-form algebra on every quasi-étale cover is generated by a nowhere-vanishing reflexive form in top degree.

evidence.quote: irreducible Calabi-Yau

nodes.label: Irreducible holomorphic symplectic variety
nodes.summary: A compact Kähler variety with rational singularities whose reflexive-form algebra on every quasi-étale cover is generated by a holomorphic symplectic reflexive two-form.

evidence.quote: irreducible holomorphic symplectic

nodes.label: Irreducible local system
nodes.summary: A local system whose associated fundamental-group representation is irreducible. The paper studies the unitary rigid local systems satisfying this irreducibility condition.

evidence.quote: Such a local system is irreducible if the representation of  pi_1  is irreducible
irreducible complex rigid local systems on  P_C^1 - {p_1,...,p_s}

nodes.label: Irreducible local system on a punctured curve
nodes.summary: A GL_r-local system on C\{p}, equivalently an irreducible representation of pi_1(C\{p}) into GL_r. The twisted character variety parametrizes such objects with prescribed puncture monodromy.

evidence.quote: parametrizing isomorphism classes of irreducible local systems

nodes.label: Irreducible plane curve singularity
nodes.summary: A plane curve germ f: (C^2,0) -> (C,0) with one analytic branch. Yano's formula concerns generic b-exponents for this class of isolated plane curve singularities.

evidence.quote: defining an irreducible plane curve singularity

nodes.label: Isolated hypersurface singularity
nodes.summary: A hypersurface singularity defined by a holomorphic function germ whose singular behavior is isolated at the origin. The general machinery of Milnor fibers, monodromy, Gauss-Manin connection, and Brieskorn lattice is developed for this setting.

evidence.quote: f ... defines an isolated singularity

nodes.label: Isomonodromic deformation equation
nodes.summary: A differential equation governing deformations of differential equations or connections in which monodromy data remain fixed. Semisimple MCG-finite representations correspond to algebraic solutions of such equations.

evidence.quote: correspond to algebraic solutions to a certain isomonodromy differential equation

nodes.label: Isomonodromic deformation of a flat vector bundle
nodes.summary: A deformation of a flat vector bundle with connection along a family of curves such that the connection's monodromy representation remains constant.

evidence.quote: the isomonodromic deformation of (E0, nabla0) is the deformation (E, nabla) of (E0, nabla0) to C/Delta, such that the monodromy of the connection is constant

nodes.label: Isotropic Beauville-Bogomolov class
nodes.summary: A nonzero rational or integral degree-two cohomology class whose value under the Beauville-Bogomolov quadratic form is zero.

evidence.quote: a nonzero class in H^2(X,Q) that is isotropic with respect to the Beauville-Bogomolov form q_X

nodes.label: Iterated fibration structure
nodes.summary: A compatible collection of fibrations on the boundary hypersurfaces of a manifold with corners, organized by a partial order so that intersections and induced bases/fibers inherit compatible fibrations. It supplies the boundary data used to define manifolds with fibered corners.

evidence.quote: iterated fibration structure for M

nodes.label: Ivanov no-virtual-Z-quotient conjecture for mapping class groups
nodes.summary: The conjecture that mapping class groups do not virtually surject onto the infinite cyclic group Z. The excerpt relates this conjecture to MCG-finite representations and Putman-Wieland-type questions.

evidence.quote: Ivanov's conjecture that mapping class groups do not virtually surject onto  $\mathbb{Z}$

nodes.label: Jχ functional
nodes.summary: A functional on Kähler potentials defined using χ, ω0, ωϕ, and c0. Its Euler-Lagrange equation is the J-equation when χ is Kähler.

evidence.quote: The Jχ functional for any (1,1)-form χ is defined by
critical point of the Jχ functional is exactly the solution to the J-equation
Jχ(ϕ)
dJχ/dt

nodes.label: J-cone positivity condition
nodes.summary: The positivity inequality cω^p−pχ∧ω^{p−1}≥0, especially cω^{n−1}−(n−1)χ∧ω^{n−2}>0. It functions as the cone or subsolution condition in the continuity argument.

evidence.quote: cωp − pχ ∧ ωp−1 ≥ 0

nodes.label: J-equation
nodes.summary: For Kähler metrics ω0 and χ, the J-equation asks for ωϕ=ω0+i∂∂barϕ>0 satisfying tr_{ωϕ}χ=c0. Equivalently, χ∧ωϕ^{n−1}/(n−1)! = c0ωϕ^n/n!.

evidence.quote: trωϕ χ = c

nodes.label: Coercivity of the Jχ functional
nodes.summary: The lower growth condition Jχ(ϕ) ≥ εJω0(ϕ) − C for all Kähler potentials. It is one of the equivalent formulations of solvability of the J-equation.

nodes.label: J-holomorphic curve
nodes.summary: A map from a Riemann surface to an almost complex manifold satisfying the nonlinear Cauchy-Riemann equation.

evidence.quote: closed J-holomorphic curve

nodes.label: Jet space formalism
nodes.summary: A method for encoding local derivative data of maps or sections as points in jet spaces, used to formulate and prove generic conditions.

evidence.quote: Jet space formalism

nodes.label: Julia set
nodes.summary: The boundary of the filled Julia set of a quadratic-like map or polynomial.

evidence.quote: its boundary is the Julia set J(f)

nodes.label: K3[2] deformation type
nodes.summary: The deformation type of hyper-Kähler fourfolds represented by the Hilbert scheme of length-two subschemes of a K3 surface.

evidence.quote: We prove that a hyper-Kähler fourfold satisfying a mild topological assumption is of K3[2] deformation type.
same as those of K3[2] hyper-Kähler fourfolds

nodes.label: K3[2] numerical type
nodes.summary: A numerical condition on a hyper-Kähler fourfold matching the relevant topological and intersection-theoretic invariants of K3[2] fourfolds. The excerpt identifies it as the hypothesis in O'Grady's conjectural characterization of K3[2] deformation type.

evidence.quote: hyper-Kähler fourfolds of K3[2] numerical type are of K3[2] deformation type
forces the Betti numbers, the Fujiki constant, and the Huybrechts-Riemann-Roch polynomial of the hyper-Kähler fourfold to be the same as those of K3[2] hyper-Kähler fourfolds

nodes.label: K3 hyperkähler period domain
nodes.summary: The locally symmetric quotient Γ\O(3,19)/(O(3)×O(19)), where Γ is the automorphism group of the K3 cohomology lattice preserving the intersection form.

evidence.quote: $\mathcal {D} \equiv \Gamma \backslash O (3, 1 9) / (O (3) \times O (1 9))$
where  $\Gamma$  is the automorphism group of  $H^{2}(\mathcal{K};\mathbb{Z})$  preserving the intersection form

nodes.label: K3 hyperkähler period map
nodes.summary: The map from the unit-diameter hyperkähler K3 moduli space to the K3 period domain that assigns to a metric its self-dual harmonic 3-plane period.

evidence.quote: we have a well-defined period map
$\mathcal {P}: \mathfrak {M} \longrightarrow \mathcal {D}$

nodes.label: K3 intersection form
nodes.summary: The cup-product intersection form on H^2 of the K3 manifold; in this setting it has signature (3,19).

evidence.quote: intersection form on  $H^{2}(\mathcal{K};\mathbb{Z})$  has signature (3,19)

nodes.label: K3 manifold
nodes.summary: The oriented smooth four-manifold underlying a complex K3 surface.

evidence.quote: the K3 manifold  $\mathcal{K}$  is by definition the oriented smooth 4-manifold underlying a complex K3 surface

nodes.label: K3 surface
nodes.summary: A simply connected compact complex surface with vanishing first Chern class; in this paper it is the compact 4-manifold carrying the collapsing Ricci-flat Kähler or hyperkähler metrics.

evidence.quote: simply-connected compact complex surfaces with vanishing first Chern class
This important class of manifolds is a generalization in all even dimensions of K3 surfaces.

nodes.label: d1-properness of K-energy
aliases: properness of K-energy with respect to L1 geodesic distance
nodes.summary: K-energy is proper with respect to d1 if every normalized sequence with d1(0,ϕ_i) tending to infinity has K(ϕ_i) tending to infinity. The paper proves that this properness implies existence of cscK metrics, and treats the twisted analogue for Kβ.

evidence.quote: d1(0,ϕi)=∞ implies K(ϕi)=∞
evidence.section: 4. K-energy proper implies existence of cscK

evidence.quote: proper with respect to geodesic distance d1
evidence.section: 4. K-energy proper implies existence of cscK

nodes.label: K-form determined by a complex hyperbolic lattice
nodes.summary: The algebraic group over the adjoint trace field K whose real points recover G and in which Γ is contained up to conjugacy. It is the algebraic input for integral points and Weil restriction.

evidence.quote: Γ determines a K-form of G

nodes.label: K-semistability for Fano cones
nodes.summary: An algebro-geometric semistability condition for Fano cones. The intermediate cone W is treated as a K-semistable cone, while the asymptotic Calabi-Yau cone corresponds to the polystable endpoint.

evidence.quote: defines a K-semistable (resp. K-polystable) Fano cone

nodes.label: K-trivial variety
nodes.summary: A variety whose reflexive canonical sheaf is isomorphic to the structure sheaf. It is a stronger form of numerical K-triviality used after passing to suitable covers or reductions.

evidence.quote: K-trivial

nodes.label: Kähler cohomology class
nodes.summary: The de Rham cohomology class represented by a Kähler form. Uniform J-stability is formulated for the triple (M,[ω0],[χ]).

evidence.quote: (M, [ω0], [χ]) is uniformly J-stable

nodes.label: Kähler-Einstein cone metric along a divisor
nodes.summary: A Kähler-Einstein metric on X\D that has cone singularities of angle 2πβ along D and satisfies the current equation Ric(ωβ)=σ ωβ+(1−β)[D]. The paper studies these metrics as the cone angle β tends to zero.

evidence.quote: Kähler-Einstein metrics ωβ with a cone singularity of angle 2πβ along D
Ric ωβ = σωβ + (1 − β)[D]

nodes.label: Kähler-Einstein metric
nodes.summary: A Kähler metric whose Ricci form is proportional to the metric, with sign depending on the Einstein constant. In the conic setting the Ricci equation is interpreted as a current equation with a divisor contribution.

evidence.quote: Ric ωβ = σωβ + (1 − β)[D]

nodes.label: Kähler-Einstein metric on a log Fano pair
nodes.summary: A finite-energy Hermitian metric on -(K_X+D) whose Monge-Ampère measure equals the adapted measure e^{-phi}/|s_D|^2 in the pluripotential sense. Such a metric is bounded and smooth on the regular locus.

evidence.quote: Kähler-Einstein (Hermitian) metric
evidence.section: 2.2 Analytic criterion for the existence of Kähler-Einstein metrics
2.2 Analytic criterion for the existence of Kähler-Einstein metrics

nodes.label: Splitting of Kähler-Einstein metrics
nodes.summary: A decomposition of a singular Kähler-Einstein metric into semipositive closed (1,1)-forms or currents whose kernels are the complementary tangent summands. This controls limit leaves near the singular locus.

evidence.quote: omega_t=omega_t^1+omega_t^2

nodes.label: Logarithmic Kähler volume ratio
nodes.summary: The function F defined by the logarithm of the ratio of the deformed and background Kähler volume forms, equivalently by the determinant quotient in local coordinates. It is the auxiliary unknown in the coupled cscK system.

evidence.quote: \log{\omega_{\varphi}^{n}\over\omega^{n}}

nodes.label: Kähler metric
nodes.summary: A Hermitian metric on a complex manifold whose associated real (1,1)-form is closed. It supplies the HYM equation and the slope theory polarization.

evidence.quote: smooth Kähler metric
Kähler metrics ω0 and χ on M

nodes.label: Kähler polarization class
nodes.summary: A chosen Kähler cohomology class fixing the polarization in which the Calabi-Yau metric is taken; for the Fermat family the paper uses the scaled class s^{-1}[Delta].

evidence.quote: in the polarisation class s^{-1}[Delta]
[Delta] is a fixed Kahler class on CP^{n+1} restricted to X_s

nodes.label: Kähler potential
nodes.summary: A real function \varphi used to deform a background Kähler form by \omega_\varphi=\omega_X+i\partial\bar\partial\varphi, usually with a normalization such as \sup_X\varphi=0.

evidence.quote: If \varphi is a real smooth function on X, we let \omega_{\varphi} = \omega_{X} + i\partial \bar{\partial}\varphi
\sup _ {X} \varphi = 0

nodes.label: Kähler potential deformation
nodes.summary: The notation ωϕ=ω0+i∂∂barϕ>0 denotes a Kähler metric cohomologous to a reference metric ω0. Solutions are sought in this potential form, with ϕ unique up to constants.

nodes.label: Kähler test configuration
nodes.summary: A degeneration of a compact Kähler manifold equipped with a relatively Kähler class Ω, used to test stability. The paper imports the precise definition from Dervan-Ross.

evidence.quote: Kähler test configurations (X, Ω)

nodes.label: Middle convolution
nodes.summary: One of Katz's operations in the inductive construction of rigid irreducible local systems on a punctured projective line. In the excerpt it appears as background comparison to the paper's construction.

evidence.quote: Katz ... has given an inductive procedure of constructing all rigid irreducible local systems on  P^1 -S  via two operations, called middle convolution and tensoring

nodes.label: Klt pair with current
