nodes.summary: A pair consisting of a smooth projective manifold X and a klt twisting current θ. The log discrepancy Aθ(v) of divisorial valuations is measured with respect to this pair.

evidence.quote: with respect to the 'klt pair' (X, θ)

nodes.label: Kawamata log terminal singularity condition
nodes.summary: A mild singularity or integrability condition for pairs and singular currents. In the paper it is required for θ in the nonnegative twisted Kähler-Einstein setting and in the twisted Fano case.

evidence.quote: only admits a solution when θ is klt
with (X, Δ) klt
log terminal singularities
klt singularities

nodes.label: Knizhnik-Zamolodchikov equations
nodes.summary: Differential equations on conformal blocks whose solution local systems are used in the paper to realize unitary irreducible rigid local systems with finite local monodromies. The excerpt uses an alternate spelling, Knizhnik-Zamalodchikov.

evidence.quote: arise as solutions to the Knizhnik-Zamalodchikov equations on conformal blocks for the special linear group
Unitary irreducible rigid local systems and KZ equations

nodes.label: Kodaira-Parshin trick
nodes.summary: A construction method using families or covers of curves to produce geometric or monodromy examples; in the excerpt it is the source of the flat-bundle counterexample.

evidence.quote: The construction arises from the "Kodaira-Parshin trick"

nodes.label: Kolodziej-type L-infinity estimate
aliases: uniform estimate for the complex Monge-Ampère equation
nodes.summary: A uniform L^\infty bound for normalized solutions of the complex Monge-Ampère equation when the prescribed density is in L^q for q>1. The paper gives a PDE proof of this sharp estimate.

evidence.quote: sharp L^{\infty} estimates for the complex Monge-Ampère equation
Kolodziej [13], which established L^{\infty} estimates for the solution when the right hand side is in L^q, for q > 1

nodes.label: Kontsevich-Soibelman metric limit conjecture
nodes.summary: The metric-limit part of the SYZ conjecture asserting Gromov-Hausdorff convergence of scaled Calabi-Yau metrics to a singular affine manifold whose smooth locus carries a real Monge-Ampere metric.

evidence.quote: This part is also known as the Kontsevich-Soibelman conjecture
converge in the Gromov-Hausdorff sense to a singular affine manifold B

nodes.label: Kronecker symbol
nodes.summary: The quadratic-residue symbol used to define chi_2 and to test whether a shifted quadratic form can properly represent a candidate tangent curvature.

evidence.quote: Kronecker symbol

nodes.label: k-th degree of a rational self-map
nodes.summary: For a dominant rational self-map f of a d-dimensional normal projective variety and an ample class omega, the k-th degree is the intersection number (f^* omega^k . omega^{d-k}). Its iterates form the degree sequence measuring dynamical complexity.

evidence.quote: define its  $k$ -th degree ... as the intersection product

nodes.label: Hodge cohomology of L2 harmonic forms
nodes.summary: The space of square-integrable differential forms annihilated by d and its formal adjoint, used as the Hodge realization of reduced L2 cohomology. The conjectures in the paper are stated in terms of this space.

evidence.quote: Hodge cohomology, that is, their space of L2-harmonic forms

nodes.label: L-twisted real G-Higgs bundle
nodes.summary: For a real form G^R with complexified Cartan decomposition g=h plus m, an L-twisted G^R-Higgs bundle is an L-twisted (H,m)-Higgs pair (E_H,phi). The Higgs field phi is a holomorphic section of the associated m-bundle tensored with L.

evidence.quote: GR-Higgs bundle

nodes.label: Lagrangian fibration
nodes.summary: A fibration from a hyper-Kähler manifold whose fibers are Lagrangian with respect to the holomorphic symplectic form; in the setting recalled in the paper, smooth fibers are abelian varieties.

evidence.quote: f is a Lagrangian hence equidimensional fibration, and any smooth fiber X_b := f^{-1}(b) is an abelian variety
is Lagrangian with respect to the canonical holomorphic symplectic form

nodes.label: Lagrangian phase operator
nodes.summary: For eigenvalues λi of one Kähler form relative to another, the phase operator is the sum of arctan(λi). The dHYM equation fixes this phase to a constant.

evidence.quote: ∑ arctan λi = constant
where λi are the eigenvalues of ωϕ with respect to χ

nodes.label: Lagrangian submanifold
nodes.summary: A middle-dimensional submanifold of a symplectic or Kähler manifold on which the symplectic form restricts to zero; it is the base notion refined by special Lagrangian geometry.

evidence.quote: special Lagrangian T^n fibration
special Lagrangian singularities

nodes.label: Landing parameter eta
nodes.summary: The area ratio measuring the probability that an orbit starting in the domain of a Feigenbaum map eventually lands in the first pre-renormalization domain.

evidence.quote: landing parameter η is the probability

nodes.label: Langlands prediction for cohomology of smooth proper Deligne-Mumford stacks
nodes.summary: A conjectural expectation that simple motives in the cohomology of smooth proper Deligne-Mumford stacks over $\mathbb{Z}$ correspond, up to Tate twist, to conductor-one polarized algebraic cuspidal automorphic representations.

evidence.quote: conjectures from the Langlands program
should correspond to irreducible polarized algebraic cuspidal automorphic representations

nodes.label: Large complex structure limit
nodes.summary: A severe degeneration of complex structures in a Calabi-Yau family; in the Fermat family it is approached as the parameter s tends to infinity.

evidence.quote: degenerating to the large complex structure limit

nodes.label: Large genus asymptotic for principal strata area Siegel-Veech constants

evidence.quote: large genus limits for Masur-Veech volumes and area Siegel-Veech constants associated with principal strata

nodes.label: Large genus asymptotic for principal strata volumes
aliases: large genus limit of principal quadratic Masur-Veech volumes

nodes.label: Large genus asymptotic for psi-class correlators
aliases: large genus limit of psi-class intersection numbers
nodes.summary: The asymptotic regime in which g tends to infinity and psi-class correlators simplify to an explicit leading term involving double factorials, uniformly in the degrees under a logarithmic bound on n.

evidence.quote: the correlators (1.1) simplify considerably under this limit

nodes.label: Lattice subgroup
aliases: lattice in a locally compact group
nodes.summary: A discrete subgroup Γ of a locally compact group G such that the quotient Γ\G has finite invariant measure.

nodes.label: Leading eigen b-class of a rational map

nodes.label: Lebesgue measure
nodes.summary: The planar measure used in the paper; the authors use the word area for Lebesgue measure of subsets of the complex plane.

nodes.label: Lelong number
nodes.summary: A numerical invariant measuring the singularity order of a plurisubharmonic function or ray. The paper uses Lelong numbers in its study of psh rays and valuations.

nodes.summary: For a positive current, the set Y={x:ν(x)≥ε} where ν is the Lelong number. Siu's theorem makes this set a subvariety used in the inductive proof.

nodes.label: Level of a ZVHS

evidence.quote: The level of ${\mathbb{V}}$ is the level of the $\mathbb{Q}$-Hodge-Lie algebra $\mathfrak{g}^{\textnormal{ad}}_x$ for $x$ any Hodge generic point of $D$

nodes.label: Limit curve of a maximal surface

evidence.quote: has a limit curve  $\partial_{\infty}\Sigma$  in  $\partial_{\infty}\mathbf{H}^{2,n}$
every such semi-positive loop is the boundary of a unique complete maximal surface

nodes.label: Limit Künneth decomposition for local systems
nodes.summary: A decomposition of the local systems R^k pi_* Q induced by a product decomposition over a punctured disk. It is used to pass cohomological product data to the special fiber.

nodes.label: Limiting zero distribution profile

If ... then is uniformly distributed on the unit circle, and is almost surely zero.

nodes.label: Line bundle on a curve family
nodes.summary: A line bundle on the total curve of a family. In the Picard-stack formulation, it supplies the twisting datum for universal and twisted double ramification cycles.

evidence.quote: together with a line bundle L -> C

nodes.label: Linear Cauchy-Riemann operator
nodes.summary: A first-order elliptic operator arising from linearizing the pseudoholomorphic curve equation; its Fredholm properties control transversality.

nodes.label: Invertibility of the linearized conic Kähler-Einstein operator

Since Lβ = 1/2(∆gβ + 1)

nodes.label: Linearized hyperkähler triple operator

evidence.quote: The linearization of the elliptic system
D ≡ d∗ + d+

nodes.label: Liouville property for half-harmonic 1-forms
nodes.summary: A rigidity property for half-harmonic 1-forms on complete hyperkähler Tian-Yau spaces, used to rule out kernel elements in weighted spaces during the gluing analysis.

nodes.label: Liouville property for harmonic functions on Tian-Yau spaces

nodes.label: Local Bernstein-Sato polynomial

nodes.label: Local covering space from short geodesic loops
nodes.summary: A local covering construction associated with collections of short geodesic loops. It supports the paper's passage from collapsed local geometry to an asymptotic fibration.

nodes.label: Local cscK equation on a complex domain

nodes.label: Local monodromy

nodes.label: Magical sl2-triple

evidence.quote: minimal extension is the image of the natural morphism

nodes.label: Minimum norm of a test configuration
nodes.summary: A norm on Kähler test configurations that may replace J[ω0](X,Ω) in uniform stability inequalities. Its detailed definition is cited rather than developed in the excerpt.

evidence.quote: J[ω0](X, Ω) can be replaced by the minimum norm

nodes.label: Mirror algebra of an affine log Calabi-Yau variety
nodes.summary: The mirror algebra is the finitely generated commutative associative R-algebra structure on the theta module, with theta_0 as unit and multiplication constants given by non-archimedean disk counts. After setting curve classes to one, its compactification-independent variant is denoted A_U.

evidence.quote: unique finitely generated commutative associative R-algebra structure
we will refer to A... as mirror algebras

nodes.label: Mirror family as the spectrum of the mirror algebra
nodes.summary: The affine family X=Spec(A) over Spec(R) associated to the mirror algebra. Over Q it is a flat family whose fibers have the same dimension as U and whose generic fiber is log Calabi-Yau with log canonical singularities.

evidence.quote: X := Spec(A) −→ Spec(R)
generic fiber is log canonical and log Calabi-Yau

nodes.label: Misiurewicz map
nodes.summary: A polynomial or parameter for which the critical orbit is eventually periodic but not periodic.

evidence.quote: eventually periodic, but not periodic

nodes.label: Missing curvature
nodes.summary: A positive curvature c whose residue class modulo 24 appears in the packing but whose value c itself does not appear as a curvature.

evidence.quote: Call a positive curvature c missing
evidence.section: 1.2. The set of curvatures of an Apollonian circle packing

nodes.label: Mixed Hodge module
nodes.summary: A Saito mixed Hodge module is a Hodge-module-like object with an added weight filtration whose graded pieces are polarizable Hodge modules. The paper's proof of extension and logarithmic results relies on this theory.

evidence.quote: A graded-polarisable mixed Hodge module M on Y is an object of the same kind, but with an additional increasing filtration W_M

nodes.label: Modular locus for a ZVHS
nodes.summary: The union in S x S of all special correspondences for the variation; it is a subset of the Hodge locus of the product variation.

evidence.quote: The modular locus for $(S,{\mathbb{V}})$ is the union in $S\times S$ of the special correspondences.

nodes.label: Moduli space of holomorphic branched covers
nodes.summary: The parameter space of holomorphic branched covers of Riemann surfaces; in the motivating example its dimension is governed by the branch point count.

evidence.quote: space of holomorphic branched covers then has dimension

nodes.label: Moduli space of J-holomorphic curves
nodes.summary: A parameter space of J-holomorphic curves modulo the natural equivalences of the domain and target setup.

evidence.quote: moduli spaces of pseudoholomorphic curves

nodes.label: Moduli space of one-dimensional sheaves on an abelian surface
aliases: moduli space of torsion sheaves on an abelian surface
nodes.summary: A moduli space of certain torsion coherent sheaves with one-dimensional support on an abelian surface. It supplies the sheaf-theoretic geometry from which the Hilbert-Chow comparison is built.

evidence.quote: moduli space of certain torsion sheaves on an abelian surface

nodes.label: Moduli space of quadratic differentials
nodes.summary: The parameter space of pairs consisting of a complex curve and a quadratic differential, usually organized into strata according to the orders of zeros and poles.

evidence.quote: the moduli space of quadratic differentials

nodes.label: Moduli space of smooth marked curves
aliases: moduli space of smooth pointed curves
nodes.summary: The moduli space M_{g,n} parametrizes smooth complex genus-g curves with n ordered marked points.

evidence.quote: let  $\mathcal{M}_{g,n}$  denote the moduli space of smooth, complex, genus  $g$  curves with  $n$  marked points

nodes.label: Moduli stratification of multiple covers
nodes.summary: A stratification of moduli spaces of multiple covers by analytic and symmetry data, used to prove the paper's regularity and super-rigidity results.

evidence.quote: general stratification result for moduli spaces of multiple covers

nodes.label: Moment matching and matching balayage
nodes.summary: The limiting zero and critical-point variables have matching exterior moments, equivalently nearly matching balayages to larger disks, so they look the same from outside the unit disk.

evidence.quote: their balayages to any larger disk are very nearly equal
we conclude that the random variables have matching moments

nodes.label: Monge-Ampère eigenvalue operator
nodes.summary: The special eigenvalue operator f(\lambda)=(\prod_{k=1}^n \lambda_k)^{1/n}, whose associated eigenvalue equation is the complex Monge-Ampère equation.

evidence.quote: the Monge-Ampère operator f(\lambda) = (\prod_{k=1}^{n} \lambda_k)^{\frac{1}{n}}

nodes.label: Monge-Ampère energy
nodes.summary: An energy functional on plurisubharmonic potentials defined using Monge-Ampère measures. Finite-energy potentials are those for which this energy is finite.

evidence.quote: finite energy in the sense of [BBGZ13]

nodes.label: Entropy bound for Monge-Ampère density
aliases: entropy bound of the volume ratio
nodes.summary: A bound on the entropy integral ∫M e^F F dvol_g, where F is the logarithm of the Monge-Ampère density ω_ϕ^n/ω_0^n. This bound is used to control potentials and pass to limits along the continuity path.

evidence.quote: ∫M eF F dvolg
entropy bound
\int_{M}e^{F}Fdvol_{g}
evidence.section: 5. Entropy bound of the volume ratio and C0 bound of Kähler potential

nodes.label: Monge-Ampère p-energy
nodes.summary: An energy E_p(\varphi) used to normalize the weighted density (-\varphi)^p e^{nF} and to scale the Trudinger-type estimate. The excerpt shows its normalization role but omits the full standalone definition.

evidence.quote: in terms of the energy E_p
\frac {(- \varphi) ^ {p} e ^ {n F}}{E _ {p} (\varphi)} \omega_ {X} ^ {n}

nodes.label: Monic polynomial
nodes.summary: A polynomial whose leading coefficient is 1; the paper normalizes candidate polynomials to be monic by multiplying by a nonzero constant.

evidence.quote: we shall normalize f to be monic (by multiplying f by a constant)

nodes.label: Monodromic codimension
nodes.summary: For a subvariety, the codimension of its period image inside its weakly special closure determined by algebraic monodromy; equivalently it is compared by inequalities of the form dim Phi(S)-dim Phi(Z) < dim D_H-dim D_M.

evidence.quote: $\dim\Phi(S^{\textnormal{an}})-\dim\Phi(Z^{\textnormal{an}})<\dim D_H-\dim D_M$

nodes.label: Monodromy action
nodes.summary: The action on nearby or specialization complexes induced by analytically continuing around components of the divisor. In the multivariable setting there are r commuting monodromy actions, one for each function f_i.

evidence.quote: r simultaneous monodromy actions

nodes.label: Monodromy representation of a local system
nodes.summary: The representation of the fundamental group obtained by analytic continuation of flat sections of a local system or flat connection.

evidence.quote: such that the monodromy of the connection is constant
with infinite monodromy

nodes.label: Weil-restriction comparison of monodromy
nodes.summary: The Z-monodromy of W is the Weil restriction from K_W to Q of the Γ-monodromy of W. This is the key bridge between the real-geometric and integral-Hodge special structures.

evidence.quote: Z-monodromy of W is the Weil restriction

nodes.label: Monodromy zeta function
nodes.summary: A multivariable zeta invariant whose zeroes and poles are used to locate monodromy support components. The paper uses it in one proof of the structure of restricted support loci.

evidence.quote: zero or pole of the monodromy zeta function

nodes.label: Monoid ring of effective curve classes
nodes.summary: The coefficient ring R is the monoid ring over the effective curve class monoid NE(Y), with monomials z^beta recording curve classes. It is the base ring for the theta module and mirror family.

evidence.quote: R := Z[NE(Y)]

nodes.label: Morphism of complex spaces
nodes.summary: A holomorphic map between complex spaces. Functorial pull-back is defined for arbitrary morphisms between normal complex spaces with rational singularities.

evidence.quote: Let f : X -> Y be any morphism between normal complex spaces with rational singularities

nodes.label: Mostow-Vinberg arithmeticity criterion
nodes.summary: A criterion detecting arithmeticity of a lattice inside G(O_K) by compactness of the non-identity real forms of the associated algebraic group. The paper uses it to distinguish the arithmetic case from the non-arithmetic period-domain codimension case.

evidence.quote: arithmetic if and only if

nodes.label: mu-constant deformation
nodes.summary: A deformation f_y of an isolated singularity for which the Milnor number remains constant. In the plane-branch part of the paper, such deformations supply the equisingular families used for generic b-exponents.

evidence.quote: one-parameter mu-constant deformation

nodes.label: Multiple-cover index formula
nodes.summary: The relation expressing the index of a composite curve u o phi in terms of the degree of phi, the index of u, and branching corrections; in the branched case it includes the term -(n - 3)Z(d phi).

evidence.quote: standard index formula for closed holomorphic curves gives the relation

nodes.label: Multiplicative eigenvalue polytope
aliases: polytope controlling the multiplicative eigenvalue problem
nodes.summary: A polytope parameterizing the possible eigenvalue data in the multiplicative eigenvalue problem for SU(n). The paper studies its vertices and uses their strange duals to construct unitary irreducible rigid local systems.

evidence.quote: vertices in the polytopes controlling the multiplicative eigenvalue problem for the special unitary groups  SU(n)
an inductive mechanism for determining all vertices in the multiplicative eigenvalue problem for  SU(n)

nodes.label: Multiplicative eigenvalue problem for SU(n)
nodes.summary: The problem of determining which eigenvalues can occur for a product of unitary matrices, in the paper specifically for the special unitary group SU(n), given the eigenvalues of the factors. The controlling polytopes provide vertices from which the paper constructs rigid local systems.

evidence.quote: polytopes controlling the multiplicative eigenvalue problem for the special unitary groups  SU(n)  (i.e., determination of the possible eigenvalues of a product of unitary matrices given the eigenvalues of the matrices)

nodes.label: Multiplicativity of the perverse filtration
nodes.summary: The property that cup products respect the perverse filtration in the way needed for ring-level arguments. Together with Mellit's work, it is the remaining input to the full P=W conjecture according to the excerpt.

evidence.quote: reduces the full conjecture to the multiplicativity of the perverse filtration

nodes.label: Multiply covered J-holomorphic curve
nodes.summary: A J-holomorphic curve obtained by composing another J-holomorphic curve with a nontrivial holomorphic cover of its domain.

evidence.quote: curves that are multiply covered

nodes.label: Mumford-Tate domain
nodes.summary: The connected component of a real Mumford-Tate group conjugacy class of Hodge-structure morphisms, refining the classical Griffiths period domain.

evidence.quote: The space $D$ is a so-called Mumford-Tate domain, a refinement of the classical period domain for ${\mathbb{V}}$ defined by Griffiths

nodes.label: Mumford-Tate group
nodes.summary: The Mumford-Tate group of a rational Hodge structure is the Tannakian group of the tensor category generated by the structure and its dual, equivalently the subgroup of GL fixing its Hodge tensors.

evidence.quote: the Mumford-Tate group MT(H) ⊂ GL(H) of a Q-Hodge structure H is the Tannakian group ... Equivalently, the group MT(H) is the fixator in GL(H) of the Hodge tensors for H

nodes.label: Mumford-Tate group of a Hodge structure
nodes.summary: The smallest Q-algebraic subgroup of the relevant general linear group through which the Hodge-structure morphism factors. Away from a countable exceptional union, it is constant and called the generic Mumford-Tate group of the variation.

evidence.quote: smallest Q-algebraic subgroup
evidence.section: 4.3. Integral variations of Hodge structure and period maps

evidence.quote: it is the smallest $\mathbb{Q}$-algebraic subgroup of $\mathbf{GL}(V)$ whose base-change to $\mathbb{R}$ contains the image of $h$
evidence.section: 3.2 Generic Mumford-Tate group and algebraic monodromy group

nodes.label: Mumford-Tate subdomain
nodes.summary: An orbit M.x in the period domain, where M is the real group associated with a normal Q-algebraic subgroup of the Mumford-Tate group of x. Its arithmetic quotient is a Mumford-Tate subvariety.

evidence.quote: is an orbit M.x

nodes.label: BPF Banach space of numerical b-classes
nodes.summary: The Banach space of codimension-k numerical b-classes with finite BPF norm, obtained by completing Cartier-type b-classes. Rational maps act on these spaces by pullback and pushforward operators.

evidence.quote: The space  $\mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{BPF}}^k (\mathcal{X})$
Pullback and pushforward morphisms on  $\mathbf{N}_{\mathrm{BPF}}^{\bullet}(\mathcal{X})$

nodes.label: Naive rational curve count eta
nodes.summary: The number eta(P,beta) is the degree of a finite etale evaluation-stabilization map from the rational curve moduli space with prescribed boundary contacts. It is naive because it is a cardinality or degree, not a virtual fundamental class integral.

evidence.quote: η(P, β) to be the degree
counts here are naive counts

nodes.label: Naive count of curves with a fixed spine
nodes.summary: For a spine S and curve class gamma, N_i(S,gamma) is the length of the finite space of skeletal analytic curves of that class whose spine is S, with evaluation at an interior leg i. These counts interpolate the disk structure constants and support the gluing and deformation arguments.

evidence.quote: Ni(S, γ) := length(Fi(S, γ))
evidence.section: 1.3 Naive counts of spines in Sk(U)

nodes.label: Nakajima metric
nodes.summary: The hyperKahler metric on the centered Hilbert scheme Hilb_0^n(C^2), known in this setting to be QALE and hence covered by QFB/QAC methods. The paper computes its L2 Hodge cohomology consequences for the Vafa-Witten conjecture.

evidence.quote: Nakajima metric is in fact a QALE-metric

nodes.label: Nearby cycles complex
nodes.summary: A complex attached to a function that records the limiting topology and monodromy near its zero fiber. Sabbah's specialization complex is the multivariable analogue used in the paper.

evidence.quote: generalization of Deligne's nearby cycles complex

nodes.label: Nef isotropic line bundle
aliases: nef line bundle with isotropic first Chern class
nodes.summary: A nontrivial nef line bundle on a hyper-Kähler manifold whose first Chern class has vanishing top self-intersection, equivalently is isotropic for the Beauville-Bogomolov form.

evidence.quote: Any nontrivial nef line bundle L on X such that ∫_X c_1(L)^{2n} = 0
equivalent to the existence of a nonzero class in H^2(X,Q) that is isotropic with respect to the Beauville-Bogomolov form q_X

nodes.label: Nef line bundle
nodes.summary: A line bundle whose first Chern class lies in the nef cone, equivalently in the closure of the Kähler cone in the compact Kähler setting. The paper uses nefness as the positivity condition in the hyper-Kähler SYZ conjecture.

evidence.quote: L or its dual is nef.
Let L be a nef line bundle on X

nodes.label: Negative first Chern class
nodes.summary: The condition that the first Chern class of M is negative, allowing χ to be chosen in −c1(M). This connects coercivity of Jχ to coercivity of the K-energy.

evidence.quote: When c1(M) < 0, we can choose χ as a Kähler form in −c1(M)

nodes.label: Nested free cylinder
nodes.summary: A nested free cylinder is a free cylinder positioned inside a larger cylinder configuration so that its independent deformation can constrain the ambient invariant subvariety. It is singled out by the paper as a classification tool.

evidence.quote: Classification using a nested free cylinder

nodes.label: Nilpotent collapsing structure
nodes.summary: The nilpotent geometric structure organizing the regular collapsed regions, represented here by torus or Heisenberg nilmanifold fibers over an interval.

evidence.quote: NILPOTENT STRUCTURES AND COLLAPSING RICCI-FLAT METRICS
non-trivial nilpotent collapsing structure

nodes.label: Nilpotent element in a Lie algebra
nodes.summary: An element e of a complex Lie algebra whose adjoint endomorphism ad_e is nilpotent. In this paper, nonzero nilpotents are completed to sl2-triples and principal nilpotents give the split Hitchin case.

evidence.quote: called nilpotent

nodes.label: No semistability at infinity
aliases: elimination of the intermediate semistable cone
nodes.summary: The phenomenon that the intermediate K-semistable cone W in the two-step degeneration at infinity is isomorphic to the asymptotic Calabi-Yau cone C. Equivalently, the asymptotic degeneration collapses to one algebraic step.

evidence.quote: W is isomorphic to C as normal affine algebraic cones

nodes.label: Non-Archimedean Ding functional
nodes.summary: A non-Archimedean energy functional on test configurations or non-Archimedean metrics. In the twisted setting it involves the twisted log discrepancy Aθ(v).

evidence.quote: the non-Archimedean Ding functional, defined on test configurations and involving the log discrepancy Aθ(v)

nodes.label: Non-Archimedean Ding invariant
nodes.summary: The non-Archimedean Ding invariant, defined as -E^NA plus an L^NA term coming from a log canonical threshold. Replacing the CM weight by this invariant gives Ding stability.

evidence.quote: D^NA := -E^NA+L^NA

nodes.label: Non-Archimedean J functional
nodes.summary: The non-Archimedean J invariant of a test configuration or filtration, defined as Lambda^NA minus E^NA. It measures the size or nontriviality of a degeneration.

evidence.quote: J^NA = Lambda^NA-E^NA

nodes.label: Non-Archimedean metric
nodes.summary: A non-Archimedean analogue of a metric on L, often represented by test configurations. The paper studies its relation to geodesic rays of plurisubharmonic potentials.

evidence.quote: relationship between geodesic rays and non-Archimedean metrics

nodes.label: Non-Archimedean finite-energy potential
nodes.summary: A finite-energy non-Archimedean potential extending the test-configuration metric formalism. The paper compares these objects with finite-energy geodesic rays.

evidence.quote: Non-Archimedean potentials of finite energy and geodesic rays

nodes.label: Non-arithmetic Ax-Schanuel for ball quotients
nodes.summary: The Ax-Schanuel property for the uniformization map X to S_Γ of a non-arithmetic ball quotient: an atypically large component of an algebraic intersection with the graph of the uniformization projects into a strict special subvariety, unless zero-dimensional.

evidence.quote: non-arithmetic Ax-Schanuel conjecture
evidence.section: 5.4. Proof of the non-arithmetic Ax-Schanuel conjecture

nodes.label: Non-arithmetic ball quotient
nodes.summary: A ball quotient S_Γ associated with an irreducible non-arithmetic lattice Γ in PU(1,n). The paper defines this as the non-arithmetic case of its generalized real Shimura quotient formalism.

evidence.quote: non-arithmetic ball quotient

nodes.label: Non-arithmetic complex hyperbolic lattice
nodes.summary: A complex hyperbolic lattice in PU(1,n) that is not arithmetic. The finiteness theorem concerns ball quotients defined by such lattices for n>1.

evidence.quote: Γ⊂G is non-arithmetic

nodes.label: Non-autonomous basin with uniform bounds
nodes.summary: A basin associated to a sequence of holomorphic automorphisms satisfying uniform contraction bounds in local coordinates. Such basins are used to construct elliptic objects in the proof.

evidence.quote: non-autonomous basins with uniform bounds

nodes.label: Non-isotrivial geometric local system
aliases: non-isotrivial local system of geometric origin
nodes.summary: A geometric local system on a curve that is not isotrivial. The paper's rank lower bound is stated for this class on analytically very general pointed curves.

evidence.quote: minimum rank of a non-isotrivial local system of geometric origin

nodes.label: Non-isotrivial local system
nodes.summary: A local system that does not become constant after a finite base change; for semisimple local systems this excludes finite monodromy behavior.

evidence.quote: non-isotrivial local system of geometric origin
non-isotrivial polarizable integral variations of Hodge structure

nodes.label: Non-product generic Mumford-Tate group
nodes.summary: A generic Mumford-Tate group whose adjoint group is simple; this is the simplicity hypothesis under which the closure dichotomy is proved.

evidence.quote: Suppose that the adjoint group of the generic Mumford-Tate group MT(S, V) is simple (we will say that MT(S, V) is non-product).

nodes.label: Non-simple higher direct image locus
nodes.summary: The locus of rank-one local systems on U for which the relevant higher direct image fails to be a simple intermediate extension. The paper identifies this locus with restricted support loci and then translates it into D-module terms.

evidence.quote: locus of rank one local systems on U with non-simple higher direct image

nodes.label: Nonabelian Hodge correspondence for complex groups
nodes.summary: The correspondence relating semisimple complex local systems or character varieties to polystable Higgs bundles on a Riemann surface. The paper uses this Hodge-theoretic formalism in the proof strategy.

evidence.quote: The proofs rely on non-abelian Hodge theory

nodes.label: Nonabelian Hodge correspondence for real groups
nodes.summary: For each Riemann surface structure on Sigma, a homeomorphism between the moduli space of polystable G^R-Higgs bundles and the G^R-character variety. The paper uses it to transfer Cayley components to character varieties.

evidence.quote: nonabelian Hodge correspondence

nodes.label: Non-abelian Hodge correspondence for twisted moduli
nodes.summary: The diffeomorphism identifying the twisted Dolbeault and Betti moduli spaces, hence identifying their rational cohomology groups. P=W is formulated under this identification.

evidence.quote: Simpson constructed a diffeomorphism between the ... moduli spaces \mathcal{M}_{\mathrm{Dol}} and \mathcal{M}_B
Non-abelian Hodge theory [37, 38] gives a diffeomorphism between the two very different algebraic varieties M_Dol and M_B

nodes.label: Non-archimedean disk structure constants
nodes.summary: The multiplication constants chi are lengths of finite fibers of analytified evaluation maps satisfying the toric tail condition; heuristically they count non-archimedean analytic disks with prescribed boundary point, derivative, marked boundary contacts, and curve class. They are nonnegative integers used as the structure constants of the mirror algebra.

evidence.quote: structure constant χ(P1,...,Pn,Q,γ)
length of F

nodes.label: Nonelliptic Oka manifold
nodes.summary: A complex manifold that is Oka but not elliptic. The paper obtains such examples from complements of compact holomorphically convex sets with many accumulation points.

evidence.quote: nonelliptic Oka manifolds

nodes.label: Nonlinear Cauchy-Riemann equation
nodes.summary: The nonlinear equation defining pseudoholomorphic maps from a Riemann surface to an almost complex manifold.

evidence.quote: standard nonlinear Cauchy-Riemann equation

nodes.label: Nonlinear phase operator Fχ
nodes.summary: The fully nonlinear operator Fχ(f,ω)=sin(θ0−∑ arctan(1/λi))−f cos(θ0)∏√(λi^2+1), defined on the admissible cone Γ. It rewrites the perturbed dHYM equation for applying elliptic estimates.

evidence.quote: we say ω ∈ Γχ if the eigenvalues of ω with respect to χ is in Γ
define Fχ(f, ω) as F(f, λi)

nodes.label: Normal affine algebraic cone
nodes.summary: A normal affine algebraic variety carrying a cone structure with a distinguished vertex. The paper uses this algebraic category to compare the intermediate cone W with the metric asymptotic cone.

evidence.quote: A Calabi-Yau cone is naturally a normal affine algebraic cone.

nodes.label: Normal bundle of a divisor
nodes.summary: The holomorphic line bundle L=N_{D/X} modeling first-order neighborhoods of the divisor D in X. The paper uses its total space to construct local model metrics and to identify a neighborhood of D.

evidence.quote: We think of L as the normal bundle of D
The normal bundle L gives the infinitesimal neighbourhood of D in X.

nodes.label: Normal circle bundle of a divisor
aliases: unit circle bundle of the normal bundle
nodes.summary: The circle bundle in the normal line bundle of D. In the interval collapse it forms the typical fiber over interior points of the limiting interval.

evidence.quote: The fibers of the collapsing to an interval are the normal circle bundle of the divisor D.

nodes.label: Normal complex space
nodes.summary: A complex space with normal local analytic rings. Normality is part of the setup for rational singularities and reflexive sheaves.

evidence.quote: Let X be a normal complex space
normal complex spaces

nodes.label: Normal crossing divisor
nodes.summary: A divisor on a complex manifold that locally has coordinate hyperplane crossings; the strong version uses simple normal crossings. It controls allowed logarithmic poles on a log resolution.

evidence.quote: a divisor with (simple) normal crossings on X̃

nodes.label: Normal-crossing monomialization of a function tuple
aliases: monomial form on a log resolution
nodes.summary: On a log resolution, each pullback f_j becomes a unit times a monomial in normal-crossing coordinates. The resulting exponent vectors determine possible slopes and show positivity against a.

evidence.quote: u_j is a unit
L_k . a > 0

nodes.label: Normal projective variety
nodes.summary: An irreducible projective variety over a characteristic-zero field whose local rings are normal. It is the ambient space for the rational self-maps and numerical cycle groups considered in the paper.

evidence.quote: normal projective (irreducible) variety  $X$  of dimension  $d$

nodes.label: Normalized complex Monge-Ampère solvability
aliases: Yau solvability for normalized Monge-Ampère equations
nodes.summary: The existence of a normalized Kähler potential solving a complex Monge-Ampère equation with prescribed normalized volume form on a compact Kähler manifold.

evidence.quote: which is solvable due to Yau's theorem [24]

nodes.label: Normalized finite-energy potential space
nodes.summary: The subspace of finite-energy or smooth Kähler potentials satisfying the normalization I=0. It removes the additive-constant ambiguity and is the ambient space for geodesic stability.

evidence.section: 4. K-energy proper implies existence of cscK
6. Existence of cscK and geodesic stability

nodes.label: Normalized Sendov counterexample
nodes.summary: A monic degree-n polynomial f with zeros in the closed unit disk, a chosen real zero a in [0,1], and no zero of f' in the closed disk centered at a of radius 1.

evidence.quote: let f be a monic polynomial of degree n with all zeroes in Dbar(0,1), and let a in [0,1] be such that f(a)=0 ... f' has no zeroes in Dbar(a,1)

nodes.label: Normalized volume of a valuation
nodes.summary: An algebro-geometric invariant of valuations on singularities used to compare algebraic stability with metric volume density. The paper uses the normalized volume associated with the natural valuation at the vertex.

evidence.quote: normalized volume associated to the natural valuation

nodes.label: Nowhere dense closed analytic subset
nodes.summary: A closed analytic subset of a complex analytic space whose closure has empty interior; analytic generality is defined by removing such subsets.

evidence.quote: a nowhere dense closed analytic subset S subset X

nodes.label: Numerical cycle class
nodes.summary: A codimension-k algebraic cycle modulo numerical equivalence. These classes form the finite-dimensional spaces on each model whose intersections and birational transforms are used to build b-classes.

evidence.quote: Numerical cycles and their intersection

nodes.label: Numerical J-invariant
nodes.summary: An intersection-theoretic invariant J[χ](X,Ω) attached to a Kähler test configuration and a Kähler class [χ]. Uniform J-stability compares this invariant with J[ω0](X,Ω).

evidence.quote: numerical invariant J[χ](X, Ω)

nodes.label: Numerically K-trivial variety
nodes.summary: A normal complex variety with rational singularities for which some reflexive canonical power is a line bundle and the first Chern class of the canonical sheaf vanishes rationally.

evidence.quote: numerically K-trivial

nodes.label: O-minimal definable parameterization of special subvarieties
nodes.summary: A proof method that encodes candidate special subvarieties using definable sets in an o-minimal structure; countability plus definability then yields finiteness of the relevant parameter set.

evidence.quote: is definable in an o-minimal structure

nodes.label: o-minimal GAGA algebraization
nodes.summary: An algebraization principle identifying definable coherent analytic sheaves on analytifications of complex algebraic spaces with algebraic coherent sheaves.

evidence.quote: prove a GAGA-type theorem algebraizing definable coherent sheaves on complex algebraic spaces

nodes.label: o-minimal structure
nodes.summary: An expansion of the ordered real field whose definable subsets of the real line are finite unions of points and intervals; it supplies a class of tame subsets of real affine spaces with finiteness properties.

evidence.quote: an o-minimal structure specifies a class of "tame" subsets of  $\mathbb{R}^n$  with strong finiteness properties

nodes.label: Odd cohomology vanishing for stable-curve moduli
aliases: vanishing of odd cohomology below degree 11
nodes.summary: The property that the rational singular cohomology groups of $\overline{\mathcal{M}}_{g,n}$ vanish in specified odd degrees; in this paper the range is all odd k at most 9.

evidence.quote: vanishes for all odd $k \leq 9$
This vanishing of odd cohomology in degrees less than 11

nodes.label: Oka complement of a compact holomorphically convex set
aliases: Oka complement of a compact O(Y)-convex set
nodes.summary: In a Stein manifold with the density property, the complement of any compact holomorphically convex set is an Oka manifold. This is the paper's central reusable property.

evidence.quote: the complement Y setminus K is Oka

nodes.label: Oka complement of a polynomially convex set
nodes.summary: For a compact polynomially convex subset of C^n with n>1, its complement is Oka. The paper also proves broader closed-set versions under additional geometric hypotheses.

evidence.quote: complement C^n setminus K is Oka

nodes.label: Oka complement of a totally real affine subspace
nodes.summary: Except for the listed low-dimensional exceptional cases, the complement of R^k in C^n is Oka when n>1. This follows from the polynomial convexity applications of the relative complement theorem.

evidence.quote: complement C^n setminus R^k

nodes.label: Oka manifold
nodes.summary: A complex manifold with the convex approximation form of the Oka property: maps from neighborhoods of compact convex subsets of complex Euclidean space can be uniformly approximated there by entire maps into the manifold.

evidence.quote: is an Oka manifold if any holomorphic map

nodes.label: Operational Chow group of an algebraic stack
nodes.summary: A Chow-cohomology-style group of operations acting on Chow groups of maps to an algebraic stack. The universal double ramification cycle is constructed as an operational Chow class on the Picard stack.

evidence.quote: Operational Chow groups of algebraic stacks
evidence.section: 2.2. Operational Chow groups of algebraic stacks

evidence.quote: Constructing an operational Chow class

nodes.label: Optimal algebraic tangent cone
nodes.summary: An algebraic tangent cone whose HN slope spread Phi lies in the interval [0,1). This selects preferred algebraic tangent cones among generally nonunique choices.

evidence.quote: is optimal if
algebraic tangent cones at 0 are not necessarily unique

nodes.label: Outer mapping class action on the surface group
aliases: natural outer action of Mod_{g,n} on pi_1
nodes.summary: The natural action of the mapping class group on the surface group through outer automorphisms, induced by orientation-preserving homeomorphisms of the surface.

evidence.quote: There is a natural outer action of  $\mathrm{Mod}_{g,n}$  on  $\pi_1(\Sigma_{g,n},x)$

nodes.label: P=W conjecture for Higgs and character moduli
nodes.summary: The conjecture that, under non-abelian Hodge theory, the perverse filtration on H^*(M_Dol,Q) for the Hitchin fibration equals the even weight filtration steps W_{2k}=W_{2k+1} on H^*(M_B,Q). This paper proves and reduces cases of this conjecture.

evidence.quote: P_k H^*(\mathcal{M}_{\mathrm{Dol}},\mathbb{Q}) = W_{2k} H^*(\mathcal{M}_B,\mathbb{Q}) = W_{2k+1} H^*(\mathcal{M}_B,\mathbb{Q})
it predicts that the perverse filtration associated with the Hitchin system is matched with the (double-indexed) weight filtration associated with the Betti moduli space

nodes.label: Painleve VI equation
nodes.summary: The sixth Painleve equation, appearing as a special isomonodromy equation. In the paper's overview it is the classical case connected to rank-two representations of the four-punctured sphere.

evidence.quote: the Painlevé VI equation, originally introduced in 1902 by Painlevé

nodes.label: Parabolic Anosov representation
nodes.summary: A representation pi_1 Sigma to G^R with a unique continuous equivariant boundary map to G^R/P^R satisfying transversality and a dynamics-preserving condition. The paper uses these as the dynamical class underlying higher Teichmuller spaces.

evidence.quote: PR-Anosov

nodes.label: Parabolic approximand
nodes.summary: A quadratic map with a parabolic fixed point whose rational rotation number p_m/q_m approximates the high-type Siegel rotation number.

evidence.quote: parabolic fixed point with rotation number p_m/q_m

nodes.label: Parabolic Hitchin fibration
nodes.summary: A Hitchin-type fibration for Higgs bundles equipped with parabolic or marked-point data. It is the natural geometric setting underlying the parabolic support property and Yun-style global Springer theory used in the paper.

evidence.quote: Parabolic support theorem
using parabolic variants of the ingredients here

nodes.label: Parabolic renormalization
nodes.summary: Renormalization of a parabolic germ using Fatou coordinates, attracting and repelling cylinders, and transit or horn maps.

evidence.quote: parabolic renormalization

nodes.label: Parabolic semistability
nodes.summary: The parabolic analogue of vector-bundle semistability, using weighted data at marked points and parabolic slope. It is the stability condition relevant for logarithmic flat bundles in the excerpt.

evidence.quote: is (parabolically) semistable if the rank is small compared to the genus of the curve
rarely (parabolically) semistable

nodes.label: Parabolic support property for Hitchin fibrations
nodes.summary: A support statement controlling which supports can occur in the decomposition of the direct image for a parabolic Hitchin-type fibration. The paper proves this property as the geometric input for the strong perversity result.

evidence.quote: and prove a parabolic support theorem
Parabolic support theorem

nodes.label: Parabolic transit map
nodes.summary: The map carrying points from the attracting crescent to the repelling crescent through a perturbed parabolic petal; its limiting form controls the time spent in the parabolic gate.

evidence.quote: limiting transit map

nodes.label: Parameter annihilator ideal
nodes.summary: For a finitely generated relative D-module, its annihilator in the parameter ring defines an ideal B_N, with associated reduced support Z(B_N). For Bernstein-Sato quotient modules this parameter annihilator recovers Bernstein-Sato type ideals.

evidence.quote: reduced subvariety defined by the radical ideal of B_N
B_M ... equal to B_F

nodes.label: Period divergence in the K3 period domain
nodes.summary: The behavior of a sequence of hyperkähler K3 periods escaping every compact subset of the locally symmetric period domain D; in the paper this characterizes the collapsed part of the compactification.

evidence.quote: periods diverge to infinity in  $\mathcal{D}$

nodes.label: Period domain for polarized Hodge structures
nodes.summary: The homogeneous complex manifold parametrizing polarized Hodge structures on a fixed lattice with fixed Hodge numbers, realized as an open submanifold of a flag variety.

evidence.quote: space D of q_Z-polarised Hodge structures
evidence.section: 4.3. Integral variations of Hodge structure and period maps

evidence.quote: Let  $\Omega$  be the associated pure polarized period domain

nodes.label: Period integral along a vanishing cycle
nodes.summary: An integral of a holomorphic or relative differential form over a locally constant cycle in the Milnor fiber. Its asymptotic expansion produces geometric sections and candidate b-exponents.

evidence.quote: I(t) := integral over gamma(t) of eta

nodes.label: Period map for an integral VHS
nodes.summary: A holomorphic locally liftable Griffiths transverse map from the base variety to an arithmetic quotient of a period domain, equivalently the datum of a polarized integral VHS.

evidence.quote: period map associated to the Z-VHS

nodes.label: Algebraic factorization of a period-map image
aliases: algebraic image factorization of a period map
nodes.summary: A period map factors through a dominant algebraic map to a finite-type algebraic space whose analytification embeds as a closed analytic subspace of the period quotient.

evidence.quote: $\varphi$  factors ... as  $\varphi = \iota \circ f^{\mathrm{an}}$  where  $f: X \to Y$  is a dominant map of (reduced) finite-type algebraic spaces and  $\iota: Y^{\mathrm{an}} \to \Gamma \backslash \Omega$  is a closed immersion

nodes.label: Period map of a variation of Hodge structure
nodes.summary: The map from the base of a variation to its connected Hodge variety, encoding the variation and its tensor constructions.

evidence.quote: The ZVHS V is entirely described by its period map ΦS : S → Hod0(S, V) := Γ\D+
evidence.section: 3 Weakly special subvarieties and bi-algebraic geometry

evidence.quote: the $\mathbb{Z}$VHS ${\mathbb{V}}$ is entirely described by its holomorphic period map which sends a point $s\in S^{\textnormal{an}}$ to the class of $h_s$

nodes.label: Perverse filtration for the Hitchin system
aliases: perverse filtration of the Hitchin system
nodes.summary: The increasing filtration P_k H^*(M_Dol,Q) on cohomology induced by the proper Hitchin map. It is governed by the topology of the Hitchin system and supplies the P side of P=W.

evidence.quote: The perverse filtration is an increasing filtration
governed by the topology of the Hitchin system h

nodes.label: Perverse filtration associated with a proper morphism
nodes.summary: For a proper morphism \pi:X\to Y with X nonsingular, the perverse t-structure on D_c^b(Y) induces a finite increasing filtration P_k on H^*(X,Q). The paper studies this filtration for the Hitchin fibration.

evidence.quote: induces a finite and increasing filtration on the rational cohomology groups H^*(X,\mathbb{Q})

nodes.label: Perverse intermediate extension
nodes.summary: For an open embedding, the intermediate extension is the image of the natural map from lower-shriek to upper-star extension in the abelian category of perverse sheaves. It is used to detect when a rank-one local system has a non-simple higher direct image.

evidence.quote: images as perverse sheaves are the intermediate extensions

nodes.label: Perverse sheaf
nodes.summary: A constructible sheaf complex satisfying perverse support and cosupport conditions. It is the sheaf-theoretic component underlying Hodge modules and mixed Hodge modules.

evidence.quote: a perverse sheaf of Q-vector spaces rat M, called the underlying perverse sheaf
Perv(X)
the first two terms are perverse

nodes.label: Perverse t-structure on a constructible derived category
nodes.summary: The t-structure on the constructible derived category whose truncations define perverse sheaves and perverse cohomology. It is the mechanism used here to induce the perverse filtration.

evidence.quote: The perverse t-structure on the constructible derived category D_c^b(Y)

nodes.label: Perversity of a cohomology class
nodes.summary: A class \alpha has perversity k if it first appears in the kth step of the perverse filtration, i.e. lies in P_k but not in P_{k-1}, with the zero class allowed separately.

evidence.quote: We say that a class \alpha \in H^d(X,\mathbb{Q}) has perversity k if

nodes.label: Petri condition for Cauchy-Riemann operators
nodes.summary: A nondegeneracy condition used to obtain transversality for the relevant Cauchy-Riemann operators; the excerpt indicates that the paper proves it for generic J using jet methods.

evidence.quote: Petri's condition is satisfied for generic J

nodes.label: Pi-spray over a holomorphic map
nodes.summary: For a holomorphic submersion pi:Y to B and a holomorphic map f, a pi-spray is a holomorphic deformation s of f in auxiliary complex parameters such that pi composed with s remains pi composed with f. If the parameter domain is all of C^N, it is global.

evidence.quote: A (local) pi-spray over a holomorphic map
evidence.section: 2. Dominating sprays and the Oka property

nodes.label: Picard-Manin Banach space
nodes.summary: The Banach space of numerical b-divisor classes obtained from the Sigma construction, extending the Picard-Manin space from surface dynamics to higher dimensions.

evidence.quote: natural extension to higher dimensions of the Picard-Manin space
The Banach space  $\mathrm{N}_{\Sigma}^{1}(\mathcal{X})$

nodes.label: Picard stack of pointed curves with line bundles
aliases: Picard stack of curves with line bundles
nodes.summary: The Artin stack whose S-points are flat families of prestable n-pointed genus-g curves together with a line bundle on the universal curve. The paper extends double ramification theory from stable-curve moduli to this stack.

evidence.quote: An object of Pic_{g,n} over S is a flat family ... together with a line bundle
The Picard stack Pic_{g,n} is an algebraic (Artin) stack which is locally of finite type

nodes.label: Pixton's formula for double ramification cycles
nodes.summary: A tautological formula for double ramification cycles, originally conjectured by Pixton and proved for the classical cycle, reformulated and compared in this paper for the Picard-stack and twisted settings.

evidence.quote: A complete formula was conjectured by Pixton in 2014 and proven in [42]
Pixton's formula

nodes.label: Value semigroup of a plane branch
nodes.summary: The numerical semigroup Gamma = {v_f(g)} obtained from the curve valuation by intersection multiplicity with the branch. It is finitely generated by characteristic data and is a complete topological invariant of a plane branch.

evidence.quote: semigroup of values of this valuation

nodes.label: Plurisubharmonic function
nodes.summary: A function with nonnegative complex Hessian in the pluripotential sense; in the Kähler setting it supplies possibly nonsmooth potentials used in energy and integrability estimates.

evidence.quote: Let \varphi be a plurisubharmonic function such that \sup \varphi = -1

nodes.label: Plurisubharmonic potential
nodes.summary: A potential whose addition to a reference form produces a positive current. The paper studies finite-energy such potentials and geodesics among them.

evidence.quote: Finite energy potentials and psh geodesics

nodes.label: Pointed Gromov-Hausdorff convergence
nodes.summary: A convergence notion for pointed metric spaces used here to take limits of rescaled complete Calabi-Yau manifolds and of metric completions. It is the analytic convergence notion behind the asymptotic cone.

evidence.quote: converges in the pointed Gromov-Hausdorff sense

nodes.label: Pointed smooth proper curve
nodes.summary: A smooth proper connected complex curve together with marked points, equivalently a reduced effective divisor D of degree n when the points are unordered.

evidence.quote: Let C be a curve over C of genus g and D subset C a reduced effective divisor of degree n
We call an n-pointed curve (C, x1, ..., xn) hyperbolic

nodes.label: Poisson kernel for a disk
nodes.summary: The Poisson kernel gives the boundary density of harmonic measure for a disk and is used to express balayage to a circle.

evidence.quote: let denote the Poisson kernel ... normalized probability density of the harmonic measure

nodes.label: Polarised Calabi-Yau manifold
nodes.summary: A Calabi-Yau manifold equipped with a chosen Kähler polarization class; in the paper such manifolds appear with metric, complex structure, Kähler form, and holomorphic volume form data.

evidence.quote: n-dimensional polarised Calabi-Yau (CY) manifolds (X_s, g_s, J_s, omega_s, Omega_s)
Calabi-Yau metrics on X_s in the polarisation class s^{-1}[Delta]

nodes.label: Polarizable Hodge module
nodes.summary: A pure Hodge module consists of a regular holonomic D-module with Hodge filtration and an underlying perverse sheaf, subject to polarizability and support conditions. It is the pure object from which mixed Hodge modules are built.

evidence.quote: a polarisable Hodge module M of weight w on Y has three components

nodes.label: Polarizable variation of Z-Hodge structure
aliases: polarizable integral variation of Hodge structure
nodes.summary: A finite-rank integral local system on a smooth complex quasi-projective base together with the associated algebraic regular filtered flat connection and Hodge filtration, assumed polarizable in this paper.

evidence.quote: Let (VZ, V, F•, ∇) be a polarizable variation of Z-Hodge structure (ZVHS) of arbitrary weight on a smooth connected complex quasi-projective variety S.
supporting a pure polarized integral variation of Hodge structures
let $\mathbb{V} \coloneqq (\mathbb{V}_{\mathbb{Z}}, \mathcal{V}, F^{\bullet}, \nabla)$ be a polarizable variation of $\mathbb{Z}$-Hodge structures
non-isotrivial polarizable integral variations of Hodge structure
flat vector bundles underlying polarizable variations

nodes.label: Polarized projective manifold
nodes.summary: A projective manifold X equipped with an ample line bundle or Q-line bundle L. Ding stability in the paper is a property of this pair, with respect to the twisting current θ.

evidence.quote: If (X,L) is uniformly Ding-stable with respect to θ

nodes.label: Polynomial asymptotic rate to a Calabi-Yau cone
aliases: polynomial convergence rate to the asymptotic cone
nodes.summary: The existence of an end diffeomorphism under which the metric, complex structure, and holomorphic volume form converge to those of the asymptotic cone at rate O(r^{-delta-k}) in all derivatives. It is the paper's quantitative byproduct of no semistability at infinity.

evidence.quote: O(r^{-δ-k}), r → infinity

nodes.label: Polynomial automorphism of affine space
nodes.summary: An automorphism of affine d-space defined by polynomial coordinate functions. Via compactification it can be studied as a dominant rational self-map for dynamical-degree purposes.

evidence.quote: Polynomial maps of  $\mathbb{A}^d$
automorphism of the affine 3-space

nodes.label: Critical point of a polynomial
nodes.summary: A zero of the derivative f' of a polynomial f.

evidence.quote: zeroes of the derivative

nodes.label: Derivative of a polynomial
nodes.summary: The complex derivative f' of a polynomial f, whose zeros are the polynomial's critical points.

evidence.quote: then f' has at least one zero

nodes.label: Ring of polynomial-growth holomorphic functions
nodes.summary: The coordinate-type ring consisting of holomorphic functions with polynomial growth at infinity. It defines the normal affine variety X' and intrinsically characterizes the coordinate ring of the asymptotic cone.

evidence.quote: holomorphic functions on X with polynomial growth is finitely generated

nodes.label: Polynomial hull of a closed set
nodes.summary: For a closed subset of C^n, the paper defines its polynomial hull by exhausting the set by compact subsets and taking the union of their compact polynomial hulls.

evidence.quote: the polynomial hull hat S

nodes.label: Polynomial point count property for algebraic stacks
nodes.summary: An algebraic stack has the polynomial point count property when its finite-field point count is given by a polynomial in the field size q.

evidence.quote: polynomial point count property, i.e., the property that $\# \mathfrak{X}(\mathbb{F}_q)$ is a polynomial in $q$

nodes.label: Zero of a polynomial
nodes.summary: A complex number lambda such that f(lambda)=0 for a polynomial f.

evidence.quote: If lambda0 is one of these zeros

nodes.label: Polynomially convex set
nodes.summary: A subset of complex Euclidean space equal to its polynomial hull. Compact polynomially convex sets are the classical motivating case of the paper's Oka complement problem.

evidence.quote: compact polynomially convex set

nodes.label: Moduli space of polystable real G-Higgs bundles
nodes.summary: The gauge-orbit space of polystable L-twisted G^R-Higgs bundles over the compact Riemann surface. The K-twisted case is the target of the Cayley map.

evidence.quote: moduli space ML(GR)

nodes.label: Polystable sheaf
nodes.summary: A sheaf decomposing as a direct sum of stable sheaves of the same slope. The projective sheaf underlying an HYM cone connection is a direct sum of polystable sheaves.

evidence.quote: direct sum of polystable sheaves

nodes.label: Positive (1,1)-current
nodes.summary: A positive current of bidegree (1,1) on a complex manifold. Twisted Kähler-Einstein currents and quasi-positive twisting currents are built from this notion.

evidence.quote: a positive (1,1)-current ω in c1(L)

nodes.label: Positive closed (1,1)-current with bounded local potentials
nodes.summary: A closed positive (1,1)-current locally written as a smooth form plus dd^c of a bounded plurisubharmonic function. Bounded potentials allow restrictions to subvarieties and Bedford-Taylor Monge-Ampère products.

evidence.quote: bounded local potentials

nodes.label: Positive-dimensional Hodge locus
nodes.summary: The union of strict special subvarieties for V that are positive-dimensional for the variation, i.e. whose period image is not a point.

evidence.quote: We define the positive dimensional Hodge locus HL(S, V⊗)pos ⊂ HL(S, V⊗) as the union of the strict special subvarieties of S for V which are positive dimensional for V.
The Hodge locus of positive period dimension ... is the union of the special subvarieties of $S$ for ${\mathbb{V}}$ of positive period dimension

nodes.label: Positive-dimensional subvariety for a variation
nodes.summary: A closed irreducible subvariety Y is positive-dimensional for V when the restricted local system V|Y is not isotrivial; equivalently its algebraic monodromy is nontrivial or the period map does not contract it to a point.

evidence.quote: Y of S is said to be positive dimensional for V if the local system V|Y is not isotrivial. Equivalently ... its algebraic monodromy group HY for V ... is not equal to {1}

nodes.label: Closure dichotomy for the positive-dimensional Hodge locus
nodes.summary: When the generic Mumford-Tate group is non-product, the positive-dimensional tensor Hodge locus is either a finite union of strict special subvarieties or is Zariski-dense in the base.

evidence.quote: Then either HL(S, V⊗)pos is a finite union of strict special subvarieties of S; or it is Zariski-dense in S.

Then either $\textnormal{HL}(S,{\mathbb{V}}^{\otimes})_{\textnormal{f-pos}}$ is a finite union of maximal atypical special subvarieties of $S$ for ${\mathbb{V}}$, hence is algebraic; or the level of ${\mathbb{V}}$ is one or two
evidence.section: 2.4 On the typical Hodge locus in level one and two

nodes.label: Positive loop in the Einstein Universe
nodes.summary: A loop in the Einstein Universe compatible with its positivity structure; a basic example is the intersection of the Einstein quadric with a subspace of type (2,1). Positive loops are a stricter class than semi-positive loops.

evidence.quote: leading to the concept of positive loops and semi-positive loops
the intersection of the quadric with a space of type (2,1) is a positive loop

nodes.label: Positive map to the Einstein Universe
nodes.summary: A map into the Einstein Universe compatible with its positivity structure. In the paper, quasisymmetric maps to the Einstein Universe are positive maps.

evidence.quote: 2.5. Positive quadruples and positive maps
These maps are positive

nodes.label: Positive period dimension for a subvariety
nodes.summary: A subvariety has positive period dimension when its image under the period map has positive complex dimension.

evidence.quote: A subvariety $Z$ of $S$ is said of positive period dimension for ${\mathbb{V}}$ if $\Phi(Z^{\textnormal{an}})$ has positive dimension

nodes.label: Positive triple in a flag variety
nodes.summary: Using the distinguished semigroup U^R_{Theta,+}, a transverse triple (x_+,x_0,x_-) in G^R/P_Theta^R is positive when x_0 is obtained from x_- by an element of that semigroup. Boundary triples are positive according to the orientation of the boundary circle.

evidence.quote: positive triple

nodes.label: Positivity in the Einstein Universe
nodes.summary: A geometric order/positivity structure on the Einstein Universe; for example, a positive loop is obtained by intersecting the projective quadric with a subspace of type (2,1). It underlies positive triples, positive quadruples, positive maps, and semi-positive loops.

evidence.quote: This quadric admits a notion of positivity leading to the concept of positive loops and semi-positive loops
the intersection of the quadric with a space of type (2,1) is a positive loop

nodes.label: Postcritical set
nodes.summary: The closure of the forward orbit of the critical point of a map.

evidence.quote: closure of the orbit of its critical point

nodes.label: Power curvature family S_{d,u}
nodes.summary: The family S_{d,u} = {u n^d : n in Z} for positive integers u and d. Quadratic and quartic obstruction families are the cases d=2 and d=4.

evidence.quote: Let S_{d,u} := {u n^d : n in Z}

nodes.label: Prestable marked curve family
nodes.summary: A flat family of n-pointed genus-g curves in which nodal prestable fibers are allowed. Such families are the curve component of objects of the Picard stack in the paper.

evidence.quote: a flat family pi: C -> S of prestable n-pointed genus-g curves

nodes.label: Primary Gaussian integer
nodes.summary: An odd Gaussian integer congruent to 1 modulo 2+2i; equivalently its coordinates satisfy one of the specified congruence pairs modulo 4. Each odd Gaussian integer has a unique primary associate.

evidence.quote: call it primary if

nodes.label: Primitive integral Apollonian circle packing
nodes.summary: An integral Apollonian circle packing whose generating Descartes quadruple has gcd equal to 1. This is the standing class of packings studied in the paper.

evidence.quote: gcd(a, b, c, d) = 1

nodes.label: Primitive integral positive definite binary quadratic form
nodes.summary: A binary quadratic form f(x,y)=Ax^2+Bxy+Cy^2 with integral primitive coefficients and positive definiteness. Forms of discriminant -4a^2 are in bijection with primitive Descartes quadruples containing curvature a.

evidence.quote: primitive integral positive definite binary quadratic forms

nodes.label: Primitive renormalization combinatorics
nodes.summary: A renormalization type in which the little Julia sets are pairwise disjoint, in contrast to satellite combinatorics where they touch at a common point.

evidence.quote: pairwise disjoint

nodes.label: Principal stratum of quadratic differentials
nodes.summary: The generic stratum of quadratic differentials, where the singularities have the simplest allowed orders, typically simple zeros and, when marked poles are included, simple poles. The paper studies Masur-Veech volumes and area Siegel-Veech constants for these strata.

evidence.quote: principal strata in the moduli space of quadratic differentials

nodes.label: Product splitting of a locally trivial family
nodes.summary: A decomposition of a locally trivial family over a disk as a fiber product of two locally trivial subfamilies, extending a product decomposition over the punctured disk.

evidence.quote: X=Y times_Delta Z

nodes.label: Projective hypersurface
nodes.summary: A hypersurface in projective space cut out by one homogeneous equation; the Fermat family consists of projective hypersurfaces in CP^{n+1}.

evidence.quote: projective hypersurfaces of any dimension n

nodes.label: Projective manifold
nodes.summary: A smooth complex manifold that is algebraic and projective. It is the ambient space X throughout the main statements.

evidence.quote: Let X be a (smooth) projective manifold

nodes.label: Projective sheaf cone construction
nodes.summary: The construction of a reflexive sheaf on C^n as the pushforward under inclusion of the pullback of a sheaf on CP^{n-1} along the projection C^n minus the origin to projective space.

evidence.quote: underlying sheaf  E_infty  is of the form
natural projection map

nodes.label: Algebraicity of proper definable images
nodes.summary: The principle that a proper definable image of a complex algebraic space carries an algebraic structure, obtained by combining o-minimal GAGA with Artin algebraization.

evidence.quote: show that proper definable images of complex algebraic spaces are algebraic

nodes.label: Proper morphism of algebraic varieties
nodes.summary: A morphism of algebraic varieties satisfying the algebraic analogue of compactness. In the excerpt, a proper morphism with nonsingular source is the input for defining a perverse filtration on cohomology.

evidence.quote: Let \pi : X \to Y be a proper morphism with X a nonsingular algebraic variety.

nodes.label: Proper representation by a binary quadratic form
nodes.summary: A value represented by a binary quadratic form using coprime input variables x and y. Tangent curvatures are represented in this proper sense by shifted forms in the paper.

evidence.quote: properly represented (i.e. gcd(x, y) = 1)

nodes.label: Pseudo-hyperbolic space
nodes.summary: The quotient of the negative unit quadric {u in E | q(u) = -1} by ±Id, where q has signature (2,n+1). It carries the restricted pseudo-Riemannian metric of signature (2,n) and constant sectional curvature -1.

evidence.quote: Then the pseudo-hyperbolic space of signature  $(2,n)$  is
inherits by restriction of the quadratic form  $\mathbf{q}$  a pseudo-Riemannian metric of signature  $(2,n)$  and constant sectional curvature  $-1$

nodes.label: Plurisubharmonic geodesic ray
nodes.summary: A one-parameter geodesic ray in the space of plurisubharmonic Kähler potentials. The paper relates such rays to non-Archimedean metrics and studies their Lelong numbers.

evidence.quote: we study the relationship between geodesic rays and non-Archimedean metrics
Psh rays and Lelong numbers
geodesic ray

nodes.label: Psi-class correlator
nodes.summary: For nonnegative integers d_i, the correlator is the intersection number obtained by integrating the product of powers psi_i^{d_i} over the Deligne-Mumford compactification. It is nonzero only when the total degree |d| equals 3g+n-3.

evidence.quote: define the correlator  $\left\langle \prod_{i=1}^{n} \tau_{d_i} \right\rangle$  as the intersection of  $\psi$ -classes

nodes.label: Psi-class on the moduli of stable curves
nodes.summary: The psi-class psi_i is the first Chern class c_1(L_i) of the cotangent line bundle at the i-th marked point on the Deligne-Mumford moduli space of stable curves.

evidence.quote: let  $\psi_{i} = c_{1}(\mathcal{L}_{i})$  denote the first Chern class of this bundle; these are referred to as  $\psi$ -classes

nodes.label: Pullback and pushforward operators on b-cycles
aliases: action of rational maps on b-cycles
nodes.summary: The functorial operators induced by a dominant rational map on numerical b-cycle spaces, including BPF Banach spaces and the Picard-Manin b-divisor space.

evidence.quote: Action of rational maps on  $b$ -cycles
Pullback and pushforward morphisms

nodes.label: Punctured orientable surface
aliases: surface of genus g with n punctures
nodes.summary: An orientable topological surface of genus g with n punctures, denoted Sigma_{g,n}. It is the base surface whose fundamental group and mapping class group are studied.

evidence.quote: Let  $\Sigma_{g,n}$  be an orientable topological surface of genus  $g$  with  $n$  punctures

nodes.label: Pure-dimensional complex space
nodes.summary: A complex space all of whose irreducible components have the same complex dimension. The main extension criteria use a fixed pure dimension n.

evidence.quote: Let X be a reduced complex space of pure dimension n

nodes.label: Putman-Wieland conjecture
nodes.summary: A conjecture in low-dimensional topology concerning mapping-class-group finite-orbit behavior in representations arising from finite covers. The paper gives applications of MCG-finite representation methods to this conjectural circle.

evidence.quote: applications of our methods to the Putman-Wieland conjecture
connections to the Putman-Wieland conjecture

nodes.label: Q-Fano variety
nodes.summary: The special case of a log Fano pair with boundary D equal to zero. The paper's headline statement is formulated for arbitrary Q-Fano varieties.

evidence.quote: If D=0

nodes.label: QFB compactification
nodes.summary: The manifold with fibered corners M whose interior carries a QFB metric and which compactifies the corresponding complete Riemannian manifold. It records the boundary fibrations used in the QFB structure.

evidence.quote: manifold with corners M is the QFB-compactification

nodes.label: QFB compactification of SU(2) monopole moduli space
nodes.summary: The announced full compactification of the monopole moduli space for which the natural L2 metric is a QFB metric. The paper uses this as a conditional geometric input for the charge-3 Sen conjecture.

evidence.quote: full compactification of the monopole moduli space ... L2-metric is a QFB-metric

nodes.label: Weighted QFB conormal form complex
nodes.summary: A complex of weighted L2 forms whose derivatives under all QFB vector fields remain weighted L2. It is used as a sheaf-theoretic replacement for general weighted L2 forms in the computation of QFC cohomology.

evidence.quote: weighted QFB-conormal forms

nodes.label: Image method for Hodge cohomology of QFB metrics
nodes.summary: A method that compares maps from positively weighted cohomology to L2 harmonic forms and then to negatively weighted cohomology; decay gives surjectivity and injectivity follows from topological arguments. It is used to compute Hodge cohomology in the Nakajima, depth-2 QAC, and monopole applications.

evidence.quote: the surjectivity of our map follows almost immediately from the decay

nodes.label: Decay of L2 harmonic forms on QFB metrics
aliases: decay at infinity of L2 harmonic forms
nodes.summary: Under suitable exactness and fiberwise cohomology hypotheses, L2 harmonic forms for a QFB metric decay at infinity by a positive power of the total boundary defining function. This decay converts weighted cohomology computations into reduced L2 cohomology computations.

evidence.quote: L2-harmonic forms decay at infinity

nodes.label: Depth of a QFB metric
nodes.summary: The depth of a QFB metric is the depth of its underlying manifold with fibered corners, equivalently the largest codimension of a corner in the compactification. Depth is used to stratify computations such as the depth-2 QAC case.

evidence.quote: its depth ... the depth of the underlying manifold with fibered corners

nodes.label: QFB pseudodifferential parametrix method
nodes.summary: The companion-paper pseudodifferential calculus used to construct parametrices for QFB Hodge-de Rham and Hodge Laplace operators. In this paper it supplies the analytic decay input for harmonic forms.

evidence.quote: pseudodifferential calculus suitable to construct parametrices

nodes.label: Weighted L2 cohomology of QFB and QFC metrics
nodes.summary: The paper-specific weighted cohomology groups for QFC metrics with multiweight x^a, and for QFB metrics with the conformal degree shift relating QFB and QFC norms. These groups are the topological inputs for computing Hodge cohomology of QFB metrics.

evidence.quote: For a QFB-metric gQFB ... weighted L2-cohomology group

nodes.label: QFB tangent bundle
nodes.summary: The Lie algebroid vector bundle whose smooth sections identify with QFB vector fields via an anchor map over the interior. A QFB metric is defined from a smooth bundle metric on its dual.

evidence.quote: natural bundle QFBTM -> M, called the QFB-tangent bundle

nodes.label: Quasi-fibered boundary vector field
nodes.summary: A smooth vector field on a manifold with fibered corners that is tangent to each boundary fibration and sends the total boundary defining function v into v^2 times smooth functions. These vector fields form a Lie algebra inside the b-vector fields.

evidence.quote: The space VQFB(M) of quasi-fibered boundary vector fields

nodes.label: Identification of QFC weighted cohomology with intersection cohomology
aliases: QFC L2 cohomology and intersection cohomology identification
nodes.summary: Under degree, weight, and link conditions, the weighted L2 cohomology of a QFC metric is naturally isomorphic to the corresponding perversity intersection cohomology of the associated stratified space. In odd-link cases this also identifies L2 Hodge cohomology with middle perversity intersection cohomology.

evidence.quote: identified with some intersection cohomology groups

nodes.label: Local weighted cohomology model for QFC metrics
nodes.summary: The local computation of weighted QFC cohomology near a stratum by reducing to weighted cohomology of the fiber Zi below a degree threshold and obtaining vanishing above it. The model is a subset of a Cartesian product rather than a warped product.

evidence.quote: a good local model for QFB-metrics is not given by a warped product

nodes.label: Quadratic curvature decay
nodes.summary: A curvature bound of the form |Rm_g(q)| <= C d_g(p,q)^{-2} away from the base point. It is the second standing hypothesis used to invoke the Donaldson-Sun-Liu degeneration theory.

evidence.quote: |Rm_g(q)| ≤ Cd_g(p,q)^{-2} for all q ≠ p

nodes.label: Quadratic differential on a complex curve
nodes.summary: A possibly meromorphic section q of the square of the canonical bundle of a complex curve. Its zero and pole orders determine strata in the moduli space of quadratic differentials.

evidence.quote: principal strata volumes in the moduli space of quadratic differentials

nodes.label: Quadratic-like map
nodes.summary: A holomorphic double branched covering f:U to V between planar Jordan disks or quasidisks with U compactly contained in V and a single critical point.

evidence.quote: holomorphic double covering f: U \to V

nodes.label: Quadratic-like renormalization operator
nodes.summary: The operation taking the affine conjugacy class of the quadratic-like restriction of an iterate, producing the renormalization R(f).

evidence.quote: denoted by R(f)

nodes.label: Quadratic polynomial family
nodes.summary: The one-parameter family of quadratic polynomials, mainly P_c(z)=z^2+c and also the normalized Siegel form f_theta(z)=e^{2 pi i theta}z+z^2.

evidence.quote: P_c: z \mapsto z^{2} + c

nodes.label: Quadratic reciprocity law
nodes.summary: The reciprocity relation for Kronecker or Legendre symbols used to show propagation and constancy of the chi_2 invariant across tangent circles.

evidence.quote: Using quadratic reciprocity

nodes.label: Quadratic reciprocity obstruction
nodes.summary: A reciprocity obstruction for a family S_{2,u}, i.e. for curvatures of the form u n^2. Its occurrence is governed by the chi_2 invariant and quadratic reciprocity.

evidence.quote: if d = 2, we call it a quadratic obstruction

nodes.label: Quantum Schubert calculus
nodes.summary: The enumerative and quantum-cohomological Schubert calculus method used by the paper to construct rigid local systems. In the excerpt it is the source of the new family of local systems, but the excerpt does not spell out the full formalism.

evidence.quote: We give a construction which produces irreducible complex rigid local systems ... via quantum Schubert calculus and strange duality
we produce a new family of rigid irreducible local systems from enumerative geometry (quantum Schubert calculus)

nodes.label: Quartic reciprocity law
nodes.summary: The reciprocity law for quartic residue symbols in Gaussian integers. It is used to prove propagation and constancy of chi_4 and thereby to derive quartic obstruction families.

evidence.quote: quartic reciprocity for Z[i]

nodes.label: Quartic reciprocity obstruction
nodes.summary: A reciprocity obstruction for a family S_{4,u}, i.e. for curvatures of the form u n^4. In this paper it appears only for certain packing types and is controlled by the chi_4 invariant and quartic reciprocity in Gaussian integers.

evidence.quote: if d = 4, it is a quartic obstruction

nodes.label: Quartic residue symbol
