nodes.summary: A fourth-power residue character on coprime Gaussian integers with odd denominator, taking values among powers of i. It is the symbol used in the chi_4 invariant.

evidence.quote: The quartic residue symbol

nodes.label: Quasi-asymptotically conical metric
nodes.summary: A special QFB metric for which each maximal boundary hypersurface has base equal to the hypersurface and identity fibration. QAC metrics generalize QALE metrics analogously to how AC metrics generalize ALE metrics.

evidence.quote: quasi-asymptotically conical metric (QAC-metric)

nodes.label: Quasi-asymptotically locally Euclidean metric
nodes.summary: A metric that is ALE away from singular rays at infinity, as in Joyce's crepant resolutions of C^n/Gamma when the group action is not free away from the origin. In the paper it is a background subclass generalized by QAC metrics and includes the Nakajima metric examples.

evidence.quote: Quasi-Asymptotically Euclidean (QALE) introduced by Joyce

nodes.label: Quasi-étale cover
nodes.summary: A finite surjective morphism of normal complex spaces that is étale away from an analytic subset of codimension at least two. Such covers are the permitted finite covers in the decomposition theorem.

evidence.quote: quasi-étale cover

nodes.label: Quasi-fibered boundary metric
nodes.summary: A Riemannian metric on the interior of a manifold with fibered corners obtained from a smooth bundle metric on the QFB cotangent bundle via the anchor map. It is a complete infinite-volume metric with bounded geometry in the QFB setting.

evidence.quote: Quasi-Fibered Boundary (QFB) metrics

nodes.label: Quasi-fibered cusp metric
nodes.summary: A metric conformally related to a QFB metric by g_QFC = v^2 g_QFB, where v is the total boundary defining function. It is the cusp-type metric used to compute weighted L2 cohomology for QFB metrics.

evidence.quote: quasi-fibered cusp metrics (QFC-metrics), a class of metrics conformally related to QFB-metrics

nodes.label: Quasi-projective structure of a complete Calabi-Yau manifold
nodes.summary: The property that the complete Calabi-Yau manifold under the standing hypotheses carries a natural quasi-projective algebraic variety structure. It links the metric asymptotic cone to algebraic degenerations.

evidence.quote: X is naturally a quasi-projective variety

nodes.label: Quasi-unipotent local monodromy
nodes.summary: A local monodromy condition in which the monodromy matrices have roots of unity as eigenvalues. The paper uses it to invoke Katz's Hermitian-form result for irreducible rigid local systems.

evidence.quote: with quasi-unipotent local monodromies (i.e., the  A_i  have roots of unity as eigenvalues)

nodes.label: Quasicritical circle map
nodes.summary: A circle homeomorphism that is analytic away from one critical point but is allowed to be quasiregular of cubic type at that critical point.

evidence.quote: quasiregular over there

nodes.label: Quasiperiodic loop in the Einstein Universe
nodes.summary: A loop or map in the Einstein Universe satisfying the paper's quasiperiodicity condition. It is tied to quasiperiodic maximal surfaces via the loop-surface boundary correspondence.

evidence.quote: 2.7. Quasiperiodic loops and maps
4.5. Loops and surfaces
4.7.Periodic and quasiperiodic surfaces

nodes.label: Quasiperiodic maximal surface in pseudo-hyperbolic space
nodes.summary: A complete maximal surface in pseudo-hyperbolic space satisfying the paper's quasiperiodicity condition. The paper characterizes these surfaces by curvature bounds, horofunction bounds, conformal hyperbolicity, Gromov hyperbolicity, spatial-distance criteria, and lamination criteria.

evidence.quote: We study in this paper quasiperiodic maximal surfaces in pseudo-hyperbolic spaces
show that they are characterised by a curvature condition, Gromov hyperbolicity or conformal hyperbolicity
4.7.Periodic and quasiperiodic surfaces

nodes.label: Quasisymmetric map to the Einstein Universe
aliases: quasisymmetric maps from the projective real line to the Einstein Universe
quasisymmetric curve in the Einstein Universe
nodes.summary: A positive map from the projective real line to the Einstein Universe satisfying a quasisymmetry condition defined via the Einstein-Universe cross-ratio. Such maps have a uniform Hölder modulus of continuity and are linked, in equivariant cases, to Anosov representations.

evidence.quote: quasisymmetric maps from the projective real line to the Einstein Universe
These maps are positive and enjoy similar properties as their counterparts in the complex projective line
they enjoy a uniform Holder modulus of continuity

nodes.label: Radical of a reflexive form
nodes.summary: The subsheaf of tangent vectors whose contraction with a reflexive p-form vanishes. The common radical of all reflexive two-forms appears as the P summand in the good splitting.

evidence.quote: radical
evidence.section: 2 Locally trivial deformations along foliations and resolutions

nodes.label: Rank of an invariant subvariety
nodes.summary: The rank of an invariant subvariety M is one half the complex dimension of the image of its tangent space under the relative-to-absolute cohomology map: rank(M)=1/2 dim p(T_(X,omega)M). It is a positive integer at most the genus.

evidence.quote: $\mathrm {r a n k} (\mathcal {M}) = \frac {1}{2} \dim p (T _ {(X, \omega)} (\mathcal {M}))$
This is a positive integer that is at most the genus $g$

nodes.label: Rank of a linear representation
nodes.summary: The dimension r of the complex vector space on which a linear representation acts, equivalently the index r in GL_r(C). The main finite-image result imposes the inequality r < sqrt(g+1).

evidence.quote: $r < \sqrt{g + 1}$

nodes.label: Rank-one local system from a function tuple
nodes.summary: For lambda in (C*)^r, this is the rank-one local system on U obtained by pulling back the standard character local system on (C*)^r through F. Its monodromies encode the parameters used in the support loci.

evidence.quote: rank one C-local system on U obtained as the pullback

nodes.label: Rank-preserving degeneration
nodes.summary: A rank-preserving degeneration is a degeneration to the boundary of an invariant subvariety in which the rank remains sufficiently large for the inductive classification argument.

evidence.quote: Typical rank-preserving degenerations
it is not always possible to arrange for such codimension 1 degenerations $\mathcal{M}'$ to have high rank

nodes.label: Rational positive-slope hyperplane component
nodes.summary: A codimension-one component of Z(B_F^a) has an equation l_1 s_1+...+l_r s_r+b=0 with nonnegative rational slopes and positive rational intercept, and at least one positive slope in a coordinate where a_i is nonzero.

evidence.quote: codimension 1 is a hyperplane
l_i in Q_geq 0, b in Q_>0

nodes.label: Rational singular cohomology of stable-curve moduli
nodes.summary: The singular cohomology groups of the complex moduli spaces of stable curves, taken with rational coefficients.

evidence.quote: rational singular cohomology groups $H^{k}(\overline{\mathcal{M}}_{g,n})$
all singular cohomology is taken with rational coefficients

nodes.label: Rational singularities
nodes.summary: A normal complex space has rational singularities when, for a resolution, all higher direct images R^i r_* O_Xtilde vanish for i >= 1; equivalent formulations use Cohen-Macaulayness and the Grauert-Riemenschneider sheaf. Rational singularities imply weakly rational singularities.

evidence.quote: X is normal, and if r : X̃ -> X is any resolution of singularities, then R^i r_* O_X̃ = 0 for every i >= 1
rational singularities

nodes.label: Rational stable maps with prescribed boundary contacts
nodes.summary: A moduli space of pointed rational stable maps to Y of curve class beta, with marked points constrained to boundary strata and contact multiplicities prescribed by the skeleton points P_j, and with no other boundary intersections. Evaluation and stabilization maps from this space define the naive counts.

evidence.quote: moduli stack of n-pointed rational stable maps
f^{-1}(D) = Σ mjpj

nodes.label: Real form with Cartan involution
nodes.summary: A real form is the fixed locus of a conjugate-linear or anti-holomorphic involution, and a Cartan involution is a compatible holomorphic involution whose product with the real form is compact. This structure gives Cartan decompositions used throughout the paper.

evidence.quote: Cartan involution
evidence.section: 2.3. The canonical real form associated to a magical nilpotent

nodes.label: Real Monge-Ampère equation
nodes.summary: The real Monge-Ampere equation for a convex potential on an affine manifold, whose Hessian metric supplies the smooth limiting metric in the SYZ picture.

evidence.quote: compare the non-archimedean MA equation with the real MA equation
carrying a smooth real MA metric

nodes.label: Real Monge-Ampère metric
nodes.summary: A Hessian metric on the smooth locus of an affine manifold whose potential solves the real Monge-Ampere equation; it appears as the metric on the SYZ limit base.

evidence.quote: The limiting metric g_infty is a real Monge-Ampere metric on the smooth locus
carrying a smooth real MA metric

nodes.label: Real Shimura datum
nodes.summary: A triplet (G,X,Γ) consisting of a real reductive algebraic group, a discrete subgroup whose adjoint image is a lattice, and a G-orbit of morphisms from the Deligne torus satisfying the real Shimura-Deligne axioms.

evidence.quote: A real Shimura datum is a triplet

nodes.label: Real structure on the O(4) bundle
nodes.summary: An anti-holomorphic involutive structure on the O(4) bundle over CP^1. The paper notes that the twistor-space map commutes with this structure.

evidence.quote: There is a real structure on the O(4) bundle
the map z commutes with the real structure

nodes.label: Real sub-Shimura datum
nodes.summary: A triplet (H,X_H,Γ_H) inside a real Shimura datum where Γ_H is a lattice in H and X_H is an H-orbit satisfying the non-arithmetic real Shimura-Deligne axioms needed for subdomains.

evidence.quote: A real sub-Shimura datum is a triplet

nodes.label: Reciprocity obstruction for an Apollonian packing
nodes.summary: A power family S_{d,u} that contains infinitely many admissible values modulo 24 for a packing but contains no curvature actually appearing in that packing. Such an obstruction forces failure of the Apollonian local-global principle in the intersecting residue classes.

evidence.quote: forms a reciprocity obstruction

nodes.label: Reduced Archimedean J functional
nodes.summary: For a K-invariant finite-energy metric, J_T is the infimum of the usual J functional over pullbacks by the center torus T. It is the analytic norm used in G-properness.

evidence.quote: J_T(phi):=inf
evidence.section: 2.2 Analytic criterion for the existence of Kähler-Einstein metrics

nodes.label: Reduced Bernstein-Sato polynomial
nodes.summary: The quotient of the local Bernstein-Sato polynomial by the universal factor s+1 for an isolated singularity. Malgrange identifies it with a minimal polynomial on a Brieskorn-lattice quotient.

evidence.quote: reduced Bernstein-Sato polynomial is defined as b/(s + 1)

nodes.label: Reduced complex space
nodes.summary: A complex space whose structure sheaf has no nilpotent elements. The extension problem is posed for reduced complex spaces.

evidence.quote: Let X be a reduced complex space

nodes.label: Reduced L2 cohomology
nodes.summary: The quotient of closed L2 forms by the closure of exact L2 forms, with the analogous definition in the weighted setting. It is naturally identified with L2 harmonic forms when the relevant Hilbert complex has the Kodaira decomposition.

evidence.quote: reduced L2-cohomology group of degree q

nodes.label: Reduced non-Archimedean J norm
nodes.summary: The infimum of the ordinary non-Archimedean J functional over all N_R-twists of a T-equivariant test configuration or filtration. It is the norm used in G-uniform K-stability and G-uniform Ding stability.

evidence.quote: J_T^NA = inf

nodes.label: Reductive automorphism group data
nodes.summary: A connected reductive subgroup G of Aut(X,D)_0, its center torus T=C(G)_0, a maximal compact subgroup K containing the compact torus, and the cocharacter vector space N_R of T. These data define equivariance, reduced norms, and twisting directions.

evidence.quote: connected reductive subgroup

nodes.label: Reeb vector field on a Kahler cone
nodes.summary: The holomorphic Killing vector field xi = J(r partial_r) on a Kahler cone. In the paper it generates a compact torus action used in the affine embedding and equivariant comparison of W and C.

evidence.quote: there is a Reeb vector field ξ=J_C(r∂_r)

nodes.label: Reference Jω0 functional
nodes.summary: The special case of the J-functional with χ=ω0. It is used as the coercivity gauge and is comparable to Aubin's I- and J-functionals.

evidence.quote: Jω0 functional and Aubin’s I-functional

nodes.label: Reflexive power of the canonical sheaf
nodes.summary: The sheaf omega_X^[m] is the double dual of the m-fold tensor power of the canonical sheaf. It is used to define numerically K-trivial, K-torsion, and K-trivial varieties.

evidence.quote: omega_X^{[m]}

nodes.label: Reflexive coherent sheaf
nodes.summary: A coherent analytic sheaf naturally isomorphic to its double dual. In the paper's smooth complex setting, its singular locus is a complex analytic subset of high codimension.

evidence.quote: let E be a reflexive coherent sheaf
complex analytic subset of complex co-dimension at least 3
F is called reflexive if the natural morphism from F to its double dual F** is an isomorphism

nodes.label: Reflexive differential forms
nodes.summary: On a normal complex space, the sheaf Omega_X^[p] is the reflexive hull (Omega_X^p)** of the sheaf of Kahler p-differentials. These forms are the target of the functorial pull-back construction.

evidence.quote: Write Omega_X^[p] := (Omega_X^p)**

nodes.label: Sheaf of reflexive p-forms
nodes.summary: For a normal complex variety, Omega_X^[p] is the reflexive hull of the sheaf of p-forms, equivalently the pushforward of holomorphic p-forms from the regular locus.

evidence.quote: reflexive p-forms

nodes.label: Regular J-holomorphic curve
nodes.summary: A J-holomorphic curve whose linearized Cauchy-Riemann operator is surjective, so the nearby moduli space has the expected dimension.

evidence.quote: achieve transversality generically

nodes.label: Regular locus of a complex space
nodes.summary: The open subset of a complex space consisting of smooth points. Holomorphic forms in the extension problem are initially defined on this locus.

evidence.quote: holomorphic p-forms on the regular locus Xreg

nodes.label: Regular singular flat connection
nodes.summary: A flat connection on a punctured or marked curve whose singular behavior at the marked points is regular, equivalently logarithmic after suitable extension in the curve case.

evidence.quote: flat vector bundles with regular singularities

nodes.label: Regularized maximum of potentials
nodes.summary: A smoothing/gluing method for local potentials that approximates the maximum while preserving relevant positivity inequalities. It is used to patch potentials near singular sets in the proof.

evidence.quote: define ϕ4.25 = max~{...}
regularized maximum

nodes.label: Relative Andersen-Lempert approximation
nodes.summary: A method that approximates fiber-preserving isotopies of injective holomorphic maps, under fibered density and holomorphic convexity hypotheses, by fiber-preserving holomorphic automorphisms.

evidence.quote: relative Andersen-Lempert theorem

nodes.label: Relative characteristic variety
nodes.summary: For a good relative filtration on an A_R-module N, the relative characteristic variety is the support of gr N in T*X x Spec R. It is the geometric condition used to define relative holonomicity.

evidence.quote: relative characteristic variety Ch^rel(N), the support of gr N

nodes.label: Relative Chern-Simons integral
nodes.summary: A boundary integral comparing two connections that, under compatible trivializations extending over a region, equals the difference of trace curvature-square integrals over that region. It is used to compare limiting Yang-Mills energy contributions.

evidence.quote: relative Chern-Simons integral
Tr (F_G_j wedge F_G_j)

nodes.label: Relative cohomology of a translation surface
nodes.summary: For a translation surface (X, omega), relative cohomology H^1(X, Sigma, C) is taken relative to the zero set Sigma of omega. Tangent spaces to invariant subvarieties are subspaces of this relative cohomology.

evidence.quote: $H^{1}(X,\Sigma ,\mathbb{C})$, where $\Sigma$ is the set of zeros of $\omega$

nodes.label: Relative differential operator algebra
nodes.summary: For a regular commutative C-algebra R, A_R is the algebra D_X tensored with R. It is the ambient algebra for relative D-modules over parameter rings such as C[s].

evidence.quote: write A_R
A_R-module

nodes.label: Relative Douady space
nodes.summary: A complex space parameterizing compact analytic subspaces in fibers of a morphism. The proof uses proper components of relative Douady spaces to carry product leaves to the central fiber.

evidence.quote: Douady space

nodes.label: Relative filtration
nodes.summary: The order filtration on D_X extended R-linearly to A_R. It gives good filtrations of A_R-modules and their associated graded objects.

evidence.quote: order filtration from D_X extends R-linearly

nodes.label: Relative holonomic module
nodes.summary: A finitely generated A_R-module whose relative characteristic variety is a finite union of products Lambda_w x S_w, with Lambda_w conic Lagrangian in T*X and S_w irreducible closed in Spec R. This is the parameter-dependent holonomicity notion used throughout the paper.

evidence.quote: A finitely generated A_R-module N is relative holonomic
finite union of products Lambda_w x S_w

nodes.label: Relative Kähler endomorphism
nodes.summary: The endomorphism h_\varphi of the tangent bundle comparing \omega_\varphi to the background metric \omega_X; its eigenvalues are the arguments of the fully nonlinear operator.

evidence.quote: let h_{\varphi} be the corresponding endomorphism relative to the metric \omega_{X}
(h_{\varphi})^{j}_{k} = g^{j\bar{m}}(\omega_{\varphi})_{\bar{m} k}

nodes.label: Oka property of complements of holomorphically convex families
nodes.summary: For a holomorphic submersion with local Stein total spaces and fibered density property, removing a family of compact holomorphically convex sets yields a restricted submersion over the same base that has the Oka property.

evidence.quote: the restriction pi|_{Y setminus S}

nodes.label: Relative Picard space of curves
nodes.summary: The algebraic-space counterpart of the Picard stack that parametrizes line bundles in families of curves. It is part of the foundational setup for the Abel-Jacobi map and the image computations in the paper.

evidence.quote: The Picard stack and relative Picard space
evidence.section: 2.1. The Picard stack and relative Picard space

nodes.label: Relative tangent sheaf splitting
aliases: splitting of the relative tangent sheaf
nodes.summary: A decomposition of T_{X/S} into foliation summands extending the tangent splitting induced by a product decomposition on the punctured family. It bridges product decompositions of nearby fibers and the central fiber.

evidence.quote: relative tangent sheaf

nodes.label: Relative-to-absolute cohomology map
nodes.summary: The natural map p from H^1(X,Sigma,C) to H^1(X,C) forgets the relative part of a cohomology class. It is the projection used to define the rank of an invariant subvariety.

evidence.quote: There is a natural map $p:H^1 (X,\Sigma ,\mathbb{C})\to H^1 (X,\mathbb{C})$

nodes.label: Renormalizable quadratic-like map
nodes.summary: A quadratic-like map whose p-th iterate restricts to another quadratic-like map with connected little Julia sets arranged without crossing.

evidence.quote: renormalizable with period

nodes.label: Renormalization combinatorics
nodes.summary: The combinatorial data of a renormalization, encoded by the corresponding Mandelbrot copy, its center, or the Hubbard tree describing positions of little Julia sets.

evidence.quote: renormalization combinatorics

nodes.label: Renormalization fixed point
aliases: renormalization periodic point of period one
nodes.summary: A quadratic-like map invariant under renormalization, Rf=f up to the affine rescaling inherent in the renormalization operator.

evidence.quote: invariant under renormalization: Rf = f

nodes.label: Renormalization horseshoe
nodes.summary: A compact invariant set for the renormalization operator associated to a finite family of primitive Mandelbrot copies, with dynamics semiconjugate to a shift and parameter Cantor sets.

evidence.quote: associated renormalization horseshoe A

nodes.label: Renormalized limit measure
aliases: limit measure in measured GH convergence
nodes.summary: The measure retained on a collapsed Gromov-Hausdorff limit after normalizing the measures of the approximating spaces; in the interval case it can encode topological information of the collapsing family.

evidence.quote: extra structure of a renormalized limit measure
it encodes interesting topological information of the collapsing family

nodes.label: Representation-theoretic splitting of Cauchy-Riemann operators
nodes.summary: A method for decomposing symmetric Cauchy-Riemann operators into representation-indexed pieces, forming the analytic basis for the stratification of multiple-cover moduli spaces.

evidence.quote: representation-theoretic splitting of Cauchy-Riemann operators with symmetries

nodes.label: Residue class modulo 24
nodes.summary: A congruence class of integers modulo 24. For primitive integral Apollonian packings, appearing curvatures are restricted to six or eight such classes.

evidence.quote: residue classes modulo 24

nodes.label: Resolution numerical data of a singularity
nodes.summary: The combinatorial and numerical data from a resolution, including exceptional components, multiplicities N_i, strict transforms, and relative canonical coefficients k_i. The paper uses these data to construct candidate exponents and period expansions.

evidence.quote: total transform divisor and the relative canonical divisor are simple normal crossing divisors

nodes.label: Resolution of singularities of a complex space
nodes.summary: A proper modification from a complex manifold to a complex space, used to replace the singular space by a smooth model. The paper studies whether forms on the regular locus extend to such a resolution.

evidence.quote: r : X̃ -> X be a resolution of singularities
resolution of singularities

nodes.label: Restricted Sabbah support locus
nodes.summary: The support of the monodromy action on the restriction of psi_F C_X to f_i^{-1}(0), or more generally to the zero locus of f^a. The multi-index version is the union of the relevant one-component restricted loci.

evidence.quote: support of the monodromy action on the restriction
S_a(F) = union_i S_i(F)

nodes.label: Ricci current
nodes.summary: The Ricci curvature form or current associated to a Kähler form or singular positive current. It appears as the left-hand side of the twisted Kähler-Einstein equation.

evidence.quote: Ric(ω) = λω + θ

nodes.label: Ricci-flat Kähler metric
aliases: Calabi-Yau metric in the compact c1=0 setting
nodes.summary: A Kähler metric with vanishing Ricci curvature; Calabi-Yau metrics are the Ricci-flat Kähler metrics relevant to the paper.

evidence.quote: Ricci-flat Kahler metrics
Calabi-Yau metrics
Kähler metrics with vanishing Ricci curvature
The Ricci-flat Kähler metrics in this case have holonomy SU(2)

nodes.label: Ricci-flat metric
nodes.summary: A Riemannian metric whose Ricci curvature tensor vanishes identically.

evidence.quote: hyperkähler 4-manifolds have vanishing Ricci curvature
they form the simplest non-trivial class of Ricci-flat metrics

nodes.label: Riemann curvature tensor norm
nodes.summary: The pointwise norm of the Riemann curvature tensor of the Riemannian metric. It is the quantity controlled by the decay assumption defining the instantons in the paper.

evidence.quote: satisfying a decay condition

nodes.label: Riemann-Hilbert correspondence
nodes.summary: The correspondence between regular holonomic D-modules and constructible or perverse sheaves via the de Rham functor. The paper uses an explicit case to compare D-module extensions with topological support loci.

evidence.quote: explicit case of the Riemann-Hilbert correspondence
Taking the analytic de Rham complex

nodes.label: Riemann-Hurwitz formula
nodes.summary: The relation between Euler characteristics, covering degree, and branching for a holomorphic branched cover of Riemann surfaces.

evidence.quote: Riemann-Hurwitz formula

nodes.label: Riemann surface
nodes.summary: A one-dimensional complex manifold used as the domain of the holomorphic curves and covering maps in the paper.

evidence.quote: closed connected Riemann surfaces

nodes.label: Riemannian cone
nodes.summary: A metric space whose smooth part is diffeomorphic to R_{>0} times a compact link and whose metric has the form dr^2 + r^2 g_L. Calabi-Yau cones in the paper are Riemannian cones with compatible complex and Calabi-Yau structures.

evidence.quote: g_C = dr^2 + r^2 g_L is a Riemannian cone

nodes.label: Rigid local system
nodes.summary: A local system is rigid if its isomorphism class is determined by the conjugacy classes of its local monodromies. Equivalently, any matrix tuple with the same local conjugacy classes and product identity is simultaneously conjugate to the original tuple.

evidence.quote: It is called rigid if any other local system with the same conjugacy classes of local monodromies is isomorphic to it
if  B_1 B_2 ... B_s = I_l  and there exist  C_i ... such that  B_i = C_i A_i C_i^{-1}  then there exists a  C ... such that  B_i = C A_i C^{-1}

nodes.label: Ring of linear differential operators
nodes.summary: The ring of algebraic or holomorphic linear differential operators on the smooth space X. It is the operator algebra used in the functional equations defining Bernstein-Sato type ideals and their D-module avatars.

evidence.quote: D_X is the ring of linear differential operators on X

nodes.label: Root quadruple
nodes.summary: The Descartes quadruple containing the four smallest curvatures of a packing, used as a compact descriptor of the packing.

evidence.quote: called the root quadruple

nodes.label: S-invariant of a valuation
nodes.summary: An invariant of a valuation obtained by integrating volumes of the associated valuation filtration. For L=-(K_X+D), it is the valuation-side quantity compared with log discrepancy in the valuative criterion.

evidence.quote: S_L(v)=

nodes.label: Sabbah specialization complex
nodes.summary: The multivariable specialization complex associated to F=(f_1,...,f_r), replacing one nearby-cycle monodromy by r simultaneous monodromy actions. It is the topological object whose monodromy support is compared with Bernstein-Sato zero loci.

evidence.quote: Let psi_F C_X be Sabbah's specialization complex
monodromy action being replaced by r simultaneous monodromy actions

nodes.label: Sabbah support locus
nodes.summary: The subset of (C*)^r supporting the simultaneous monodromy action on the Sabbah specialization complex. For r=1 it is the set of monodromy eigenvalues on nearby cycles.

evidence.quote: support of this monodromy action on psi_F C_X
When r = 1, this is the set of eigenvalues

nodes.label: Safe trapping disk
nodes.summary: A quasidisk away from the postcritical set, centered near a repelling periodic point, that can be pulled back with bounded distortion along landing orbits and is used to estimate landing and escaping probabilities.

evidence.quote: safe trapping disk

nodes.label: Sard-Smale genericity method
nodes.summary: An infinite-dimensional transversality method used to show that a desired regularity condition holds for a residual or generic set of parameters.

evidence.quote: Sard-Smale theorem

nodes.label: Saturated Brieskorn lattice
nodes.summary: The t partial_t-saturation sum_{k>=0}(t partial_t)^k H''_{f,0} of the Brieskorn lattice. The quotient by t is the finite-dimensional vector space on which the b-exponent endomorphism acts.

evidence.quote: saturation of the Brieskorn lattice with respect to ... t partial_t

nodes.label: A priori estimates for scalar-curvature type equations
nodes.summary: The analytic estimate that solutions of the general coupled scalar-curvature type equations with controlled data and entropy have uniform potential and metric bounds, hence W^{4,p} bounds and compactness. This estimate supplies the closedness step in the continuity method.

evidence.quote: ω0 ≤ ωϕ ≤ Cω0
higher order derivatives can be estimated in terms of a $C^0$ bound

nodes.label: Scalar-curvature type coupled Monge-Ampère equation
nodes.summary: A coupled system det(g+ϕ_{i\bar j})=e^F det(g) and Δ_ϕF=−f+tr_ϕη, equivalent to a scalar-curvature expression R_ϕ=f+tr_ϕ(Ric−η). It abstracts the cscK and twisted cscK equations for a priori estimates.

evidence.quote: det(gi¯j + ϕi¯j)=eF det gi¯j
∆ϕF = −f + trϕη
re-written as a pair of coupled equations

nodes.label: Scalar-flat Kähler Liouville rigidity under Lp growth
nodes.summary: A rigidity statement for global plurisubharmonic potentials on C^n whose Levi form defines a scalar-flat Kähler metric and satisfies suitable Lp growth control: the logarithmic determinant is constant and the Levi Hessian is constant.

evidence.quote: This implies the Levi Hessian of u is constant

nodes.label: Scalar puncture monodromy condition
nodes.summary: The condition that the local monodromy around the chosen puncture p is the scalar matrix xi_r^chi I_r, with xi_r a primitive root of unity. This is the twisting in the Betti moduli space.

evidence.quote: \rho sends a loop around a chosen point p to \xi_r^\chi I_r

nodes.label: Second Fourier coefficient obstruction
nodes.summary: A contradiction method near the unit circle that extracts the second Fourier coefficient, combines it with second-order Taylor control, and uses square-negativity on the Sendov arc.

evidence.quote: following the 'second Fourier coefficient' strategy

evidence.quote: Applying Proposition ... and the Fubini-Tonelli theorem, we conclude
evidence.section: 5. Getting a contradiction near the unit circle

nodes.label: Self-dual harmonic forms
nodes.summary: The space of harmonic 2-forms that are self-dual with respect to a Riemannian metric; for a K3 hyperkähler metric this space gives the metric period.

evidence.quote: the space  $\mathbb{H}^{+}(g)$  of self-dual harmonic forms

nodes.label: Semi-ample line bundle
nodes.summary: A line bundle for which some positive tensor power is globally generated. In the hyper-Kähler SYZ conjecture, semi-ampleness of the nef isotropic line bundle produces a fibration.

evidence.quote: Any nontrivial nef line bundle L on X such that ∫_X c_1(L)^{2n} = 0 is semi-ample.

nodes.label: Semi-positive loop in the Einstein Universe
nodes.summary: A loop in the Einstein Universe satisfying a weak positivity condition. Complete maximal surfaces in pseudo-hyperbolic space have semi-positive limit curves, and conversely every such loop bounds a unique complete maximal surface.

every such semi-positive loop is the boundary of a unique complete maximal surface

nodes.label: Semiflat metric
nodes.summary: The nonsingular local SYZ model metric in which torus fibers are flat; in the generic region of the degeneration the Calabi-Yau metric is a small perturbation of this model.

evidence.quote: the metric g_s is a small perturbation of a semiflat metric
meaning that the T^n fibres are almost flat

nodes.label: Semigroup-constant deformation
nodes.summary: A deformation of the Teissier monomial curve that keeps the value semigroup fixed. The paper uses such deformations to construct the relevant mu-constant deformation of plane branches.

evidence.quote: miniversal semigroup constant deformation

nodes.label: Semipositive klt current
nodes.summary: A semipositive twisting current θ whose singularities satisfy the klt condition. This is the class of twisting currents used in the main twisted Fano statement.

evidence.quote: θ a semipositive klt current such that c1(X,θ) = c1(L)

nodes.label: Semisimple double centralizer subalgebra g(e)

nodes.label: Semisimple surface group representation
nodes.summary: A surface group representation that is semisimple as a complex linear representation. In the paper, semisimple MCG-finite representations are the objects corresponding to finite mapping-class orbits in the character variety.

evidence.quote: semisimple MCG-finite representations correspond to finite  $\mathrm{Mod}_{g,n}$ -orbits in the character variety

nodes.label: Semistability of isomonodromic deformations
nodes.summary: A semistability property for isomonodromic deformation families. The paper cites earlier work on this property as one of the proof inputs.

evidence.quote: our earlier work on semistability of isomonodromic deformations

nodes.label: Semistable reduction of a resolution
nodes.summary: A process replacing the resolution space by one where normal crossings are preserved while the total transform becomes reduced. It is used to analyze periods near exceptional divisors.

evidence.quote: This process is called semi-stable reduction

nodes.label: Semistable vector bundle on a curve
nodes.summary: A vector bundle on a smooth proper curve satisfying the usual slope inequalities for all nonzero proper subbundles. The paper studies when isomonodromic deformations have this property.

evidence.quote: isomonodromic deformations to a nearby curve are never semistable

nodes.label: Sen conjecture for SU(2) monopoles
nodes.summary: A prediction that the Hodge cohomology of the L2 metric on the universal cover of the reduced SU(2)-monopole moduli space is concentrated in middle degree q = 2k - 2 and decomposes by the natural Z_k action with one-dimensional weight spaces exactly for coprime weights. The paper proves the charge-3 case assuming the QFB compactification input.

evidence.quote: only non-trivial in middle degree

nodes.label: Square-negativity on the Sendov arc
nodes.summary: Nonzero points on the Sendov boundary arc have arguments forcing their squares into the left half-plane, giving negative real part.

the squares either vanish, or have argument in; in particular ... the real part is negative

nodes.label: Sendov boundary arc
nodes.summary: The part of the unit circle compatible with lying outside the unit disk centered at 1; it is the limiting boundary arc for large zeros in near-unit-circle counterexamples.

evidence.quote: large zeroes of stay close to the arc

nodes.label: Sendov lune
nodes.summary: The lens-shaped region where critical points of a normalized counterexample must lie: inside the unit disk but outside the forbidden disk centered at the distinguished zero a.

evidence.quote: surely lies in the disk, and surely lies in the lune
takes values in the thin lune

nodes.label: Sendov's conjecture
nodes.summary: If a complex polynomial of degree at least 2 has all zeros in the closed unit disk, then every zero lambda0 has a critical point within the closed disk centered at lambda0 of radius 1.

nodes.label: Sharp characteristic cycle
nodes.summary: The characteristic variety or cycle obtained from the associated graded module with respect to the sharp filtration. It records both cotangent and parameter directions for D_X[s]-modules.

evidence.quote: characteristic cycles ... with respect to the filtrations

nodes.label: Sharp filtration

nodes.label: Sheaf of Kahler differentials

nodes.label: Short geodesic loop

nodes.label: Siegel butterfly renormalization
nodes.summary: The complexified renormalization of Siegel maps obtained by transferring quasicritical circle-map butterfly renormalizations to the Siegel boundary by surgery.

nodes.label: Siegel disk

evidence.quote: A Siegel map f : (Ω, 0)

nodes.summary: The method ensuring high probability of returns to trapping disks, using hyperbolic expansion outside the postcritical set to make the escaping probability small.

evidence.quote: Siegel Return Machinery

nodes.label: sigma_e involution

evidence.quote: sigmae : g -> g

nodes.label: Sigma norm on b-divisors

The space  $\mathcal{F}_{\Sigma}(\mathcal{X})$

nodes.label: Quadratic form of signature (2,n+1)

nodes.label: Simple J-holomorphic curve

nodes.summary: The conjectural integrality property for cohomologically rigid complex local systems. The paper relies on recent work of Esnault-Groechenig and Klevdal-Patrikis on this conjecture.

nodes.label: Singular affine manifold

nodes.label: Structure sheaf

nodes.label: Reduced SU(2) monopole moduli space

nodes.label: Submultiplicativity of rational-map degree sequences

nodes.label: Subvariety integral criterion for J-stability
nodes.summary: The condition that for every p-dimensional subvariety V, the integral of (c0−(n−p)ε)ω0^p−pχ∧ω0^{p−1} over V is nonnegative. It gives a concrete numerical form of uniform slope J-stability.

evidence.quote: for all p-dimensional subvarieties V with p = 1, 2, ..., n

nodes.label: Subvariety phase function
nodes.summary: For a p-dimensional subvariety V, θV(t) is defined by θV(t)=arg∫V(χ+i tω0)^p, with the integral nonzero and limit pπ/2 as t→∞. It is the numerical phase data used in the dHYM stability condition.

evidence.quote: θV(t) = arg(∫V (χ + √−1tω0)^p)
limt→∞ θV(t) = pπ/2

nodes.label: Super-rigid J-holomorphic curve
nodes.summary: A strengthened isolation property for a simple J-holomorphic curve controlling the behavior of its multiple covers; the paper proves generic super-rigidity for simple index-zero curves in dimensions greater than four.

evidence.quote: simple index 0 curves in dimensions greater than four are generically super-rigid

nodes.label: Superattracting parameter
nodes.summary: A quadratic parameter for which the critical orbit is periodic and superattracting; such a parameter is the center of a corresponding Mandelbrot copy.

evidence.quote: superattracting parameter b_m

nodes.label: Supercritical dHYM phase
nodes.summary: The phase range θ̂∈(nπ/2−π/4,nπ/2), equivalently θ0=nπ/2−θ̂∈(0,π/4). The paper proves the uniform stability criterion in this phase interval.

nodes.label: Surface group
aliases: fundamental group of a punctured surface
nodes.summary: The fundamental group of the punctured orientable surface Sigma_{g,n} with a chosen basepoint x. It is the group represented into linear groups throughout the paper.

evidence.quote: let  $x\in \Sigma_{g,n}$  be a basepoint
$\pi_1(\Sigma_{g,n},x)$

nodes.label: Surface group character variety
nodes.summary: The orbit space of reductive representations pi_1 Sigma to G^R under conjugation by G^R. It is the representation-side moduli space compared with Higgs-bundle moduli.

evidence.quote: GR-character variety
finite  $\mathrm{Mod}_{g,n}$ -orbits in the character variety of  $\pi_1(\Sigma_{g,n})$

nodes.label: Székelyhidi fully nonlinear estimates
nodes.summary: A priori estimates for fully nonlinear elliptic equations on compact Hermitian manifolds. The paper invokes them to close the dHYM continuity paths after rewriting the equation with Fχ.

evidence.quote: It will be achieved by Székelyhidi’s estimates

nodes.label: Tangent cone at infinity
nodes.summary: A metric cone-like limit obtained by rescaling a noncompact metric space around infinity. The paper distinguishes this from the full differential and metric structure of the end.

evidence.quote: this is different from the tangent cone at infinity
Classification of tangent cone at infinity

nodes.label: Tangent sheaf
nodes.summary: The sheaf of derivations, equivalently the dual of the sheaf of differentials when defined as Hom(Omega^1,O). It controls locally trivial deformations and is split into foliations in the proof.

evidence.quote: T_X
evidence.section: 2 Locally trivial deformations along foliations and resolutions

nodes.label: Tangent space of an invariant subvariety
nodes.summary: At a translation surface (X, omega), the tangent space to an invariant subvariety M is a subspace of H^1(X,Sigma,C). The paper uses this tangent space to define rank and analyze degenerations.

evidence.quote: The tangent space $T_{(X,\omega)}(\mathcal{M})$ to $\mathcal{M}$ at a point $(X,\omega)$ is a subspace of the relative cohomology group

nodes.label: Taub-NUT metric
nodes.summary: A complete hyperkähler metric on R4 with cubic volume growth, expressible by the Gibbons-Hawking ansatz with potential sigma + 1/(2r), and appearing as an interior bubble.

evidence.quote: Vσ = σ + 1/(2r) then (M, g) is the Taub-NUT space
evidence.section: 2. The Gibbons-Hawking ansatz and the model space

evidence.quote: converge to Taub-NUT metrics

nodes.label: Tautological Chern classes on Higgs moduli
nodes.summary: Standard cohomology classes on the Dolbeault/Higgs moduli space obtained from Chern classes of the universal Higgs-bundle data. The paper studies their perverse degrees as the main cohomological input.

evidence.quote: strong perversity result on tautological classes
Strong perversity for Chern classes

nodes.label: Tautological cohomology generators for Higgs moduli
nodes.summary: Canonical cohomology classes generating tautological parts of the moduli-space cohomology. The paper studies their predicted positions in the perverse filtration.

evidence.quote: all tautological generators lie in the correct pieces of the perverse filtration

nodes.label: Taylor expansion method for zero location
nodes.summary: A second-order expansion method that approximates values of f and f' and locates zeros accurately enough to compare with the geometry of the Sendov lune.

evidence.quote: Taylor expanding out all relevant expressions to second order in the zeroes of
evidence.section: 5. Getting a contradiction near the unit circle

nodes.label: Teissier monomial curve
aliases: monomial curve associated with a semigroup
nodes.summary: The irreducible curve associated with a numerical semigroup Gamma, parametrized by u_i = t^{beta_i}. Teissier's deformation theory relates branches with semigroup Gamma to deformations of this monomial curve.

evidence.quote: introduce Teissier's monomial curve and its deformations

nodes.label: Tensor Hodge locus
nodes.summary: The tensor Hodge locus is the set of points whose fiber admits more Hodge tensors than the very general fiber; group-theoretically it is the locus of points that are not Hodge-generic in S.

evidence.quote: The Hodge locus HL(S, V⊗) is the subset of points s ∈ San for which the Hodge structure Vs admits more Hodge tensors than the very general fiber

nodes.label: Tensoring of local systems
nodes.summary: An operation on local systems used by Katz, together with middle convolution, to inductively construct rigid irreducible local systems. The excerpt mentions it only as part of this background algorithm.

evidence.quote: via two operations, called middle convolution and tensoring

nodes.label: Test configuration
nodes.summary: An algebro-geometric degeneration of a polarized variety used to test stability. The non-Archimedean Ding functional in the paper is defined on test configurations.

evidence.quote: defined on test configurations

nodes.label: Test configuration of a polarized pair
nodes.summary: A C*-equivariant degeneration over the affine line with a relatively semiample Q-Cartier divisor and an identification with the product family over the punctured line; for pairs, the divisor Q is closed up in the total space.

evidence.quote: A test configuration

nodes.label: Theta function consistency of a scattering diagram
nodes.summary: A consistency property requiring wall-crossing transformations across a wall to transport the corresponding positive, negative, and zero parts of local theta functions. The paper proves this for the geometric scattering diagram.

evidence.quote: D is theta function consistent

nodes.label: Theta module indexed by the essential skeleton
nodes.summary: Before multiplication is imposed, A is the free R-module with basis elements theta_P indexed by P in Sk(U,Z). This module is the underlying object on which the Frobenius multilinear forms and mirror algebra structure are defined.

evidence.quote: free R-module with basis Sk(U, Z)

nodes.label: Theta-positive Anosov representation
nodes.summary: A P_Theta^R-Anosov representation whose Anosov boundary curve sends positively ordered triples in the boundary of pi_1 Sigma to positive triples in G^R/P_Theta^R. The Cayley character variety loci contain open sets of such representations.

evidence.quote: Θ-positive Anosov

nodes.label: Theta-positive structure
nodes.summary: For a pair (G^R,P_Theta^R), a Theta-positive structure is given by invariant acute convex cones in the Levi representation spaces u_beta_j for simple roots beta_j in Theta. The paper relates the classified pairs to magical sl2-triples.

evidence.quote: Θ-positive structure

nodes.label: θ-twisted Kähler-Einstein current
nodes.summary: A positive (1,1)-current ω in c1(L), of finite energy, satisfying Ric(ω)=λω+θ. The parameter λ is fixed by the cohomological condition c1(X,θ)=λc1(L).

evidence.quote: A θ-twisted Kähler-Einstein current is a positive (1,1)-current ω in c1(L), of finite energy
Ric(ω) = λω + θ

nodes.label: Thin group
nodes.summary: A finitely generated subgroup of an arithmetic or algebraic group that is typically Zariski dense but of infinite index; the paper treats Apollonian packings as thin-group orbit examples.

evidence.quote: study of thin groups

nodes.label: 3-torus
nodes.summary: The product torus S1 x S1 x S1, appearing as the regular fiber when the circle bundle over T2 is trivial.

evidence.quote: regular fibers diffeomorphic to either 3-tori
is diffeomorphic to T3 otherwise

nodes.label: Tian alpha-invariant
nodes.summary: A uniform exponential integrability constant for normalized semipositive Kähler potentials: there are alpha and C such that \int_M e^{-alpha phi} dvol_g <= C. It is used to convert the auxiliary maximum principle estimate into Lq volume-ratio bounds.

evidence.quote: \int_{M}e^{-\alpha\phi}dvol_{g}\leq C_{5}
evidence.section: 5. Entropy bound of the volume ratio and C0 bound of Kähler potential

evidence.quote: where \alpha and C_{\alpha} are the constants coming from the \alpha-invariant estimate of (X, \omega_X)

nodes.label: Calabi asymptotics of Tian-Yau spaces
nodes.summary: The property that a complete Tian-Yau space is exponentially asymptotic, to arbitrary derivative order, to a Calabi model space at infinity.

evidence.quote: complete Tian-Yau space is exponentially asymptotic to a Calabi model space

nodes.label: Tian-Yau construction
aliases: Tian-Yau construction of complete Calabi-Yau metrics
nodes.summary: A method for producing complete Calabi-Yau metrics from compact Kahler-Einstein data by building an approximately Ricci-flat background metric and solving a noncompact complex Monge-Ampere equation. The paper adapts it nontraditionally to construct a Calabi-Yau metric on the intermediate cone W.

evidence.quote: Tian-Yau construction ... produces complete Calabi-Yau metrics

nodes.label: Tian-Yau metric
nodes.summary: The complete Ricci-flat Kähler metric on X\D associated with a Fano manifold and a smooth anticanonical divisor. In the paper it is the rescaled local smooth limit of the positive conic Kähler-Einstein metrics and is asymptotic to a Calabi normal-bundle model near D.

evidence.quote: the complete, Ricci flat Tian-Yau metric on X \ D
ωTY,L ... the Tian-Yau metric defined on {t < 0} ⊂ L
Tian-Yau and Taub-NUT metrics occurring as bubbles
We call such a hyperkähler metric a Tian-Yau metric

nodes.label: Rescaled small-angle convergence to the Tian-Yau metric
nodes.summary: For a Fano manifold with a smooth anticanonical divisor, the positive conic Kähler-Einstein metrics satisfy β^{-1-1/n}ωβ → ωTY locally smoothly on X\D as β tends to zero.

evidence.quote: β−1−1/n ωβ → ωTY in C∞loc(X \ D)
β−1−1/nωβ − ωTY = O(β1−ε−δ) on M

nodes.label: Local integrability of top-degree form pairings
nodes.summary: For a holomorphic top form on the regular locus, the associated (n,n)-form alpha wedge conjugate alpha is locally integrable across the singular set. This condition characterizes top-degree extension and analytically characterizes weakly rational singularities.

evidence.quote: alpha wedge overline alpha is locally integrable on X

nodes.label: Top-degree holomorphic form extension criterion
nodes.summary: A holomorphic n-form on the regular locus extends to a resolution exactly when its top-degree pairing is locally integrable. The paper uses this as a basic known case of the extension problem.

evidence.quote: a holomorphic n-form ... extends ... if and only if alpha wedge overline alpha is locally integrable

nodes.label: Topologically trivial deformation
nodes.summary: A deformation whose fibers preserve the topological type of the singularity. The paper studies generic behavior of b-exponents along such deformations of plane curve singularities.

evidence.quote: along topologically trivial deformations of a singularity

nodes.label: Toric model tropicalization
nodes.summary: A toric model is a birational map from Y to a toric variety Y_t preserving the open torus; composing its analytification with the toric retraction gives a tropicalization map to M_R. The paper uses this map to extract trees, walls, spines, and tropical curves from analytic stable maps.

evidence.quote: we call π a toric model
We tropicalize Y via the toric model π

nodes.label: Toric tail condition
nodes.summary: A boundary regularity condition for analytic maps: after retracting the analytic curve to the convex hull of its marked points, the punctured tail from the interior marked point to the added boundary point must map into the analytic torus. It turns infinite-dimensional disk counting into a finite count of closed rational curves.

evidence.quote: satisfy the toric tail condition
punctured tail T \ z maps into the torus

nodes.label: Toroidal compactification
nodes.summary: A compactification of a bounded symmetric domain quotient whose boundary is a reduced simple normal crossings divisor up to finite quotient singularities, with K_X+D pulled back from the minimal compactification.

evidence.quote: toroidal compactification X is a compactification with finite quotient singularities such that X \ X is a (reduced) divisor with simple normal crossings
evidence.section: 1. Closing the cusps of locally symmetric spaces

evidence.quote: KX + D = π*KXmin
evidence.section: 1. Closing the cusps of locally symmetric spaces

nodes.label: Toroidal compactification of a ball quotient
nodes.summary: A toroidal compactification of Γ\B^n whose boundary is a disjoint union of abelian varieties with negative normal bundle in the neat case. This is the geometric setting of the paper's negative-curvature small-angle analysis.

evidence.quote: D := X \ X° is a disjoint union of abelian varieties
D = ⊔ Dλ is a disjoint union of abelian varieties Dλ with negative normal bundle
evidence.section: 1. Closing the cusps of locally symmetric spaces

nodes.label: Torsion-free sheaf
nodes.summary: A coherent sheaf with no nonzero local sections annihilated by a nonzero divisor. Algebraic tangent cones in the paper are torsion-free sheaves on the exceptional divisor.

evidence.quote: torsion-free sheaf

nodes.label: Torsion-translated affine subtorus
nodes.summary: A translate of a complex affine algebraic subtorus of (C*)^r by a torsion point. The restricted support loci in the paper are finite unions of codimension-one subtori of this form defined over Q.

evidence.quote: finite union of torsion-translated complex affine algebraic subtori

nodes.label: Torus fibration
nodes.summary: A fibration whose regular fibers are n-dimensional real tori, used in SYZ to relate Calabi-Yau manifolds to an affine base.

evidence.quote: T^n fibration over the base B

nodes.label: Torus twist operation
nodes.summary: The cocharacter space N_R acts by twisting equivariant test configurations, filtrations, non-Archimedean metrics, and T-invariant valuations. For test configurations it changes the generator from eta to eta+xi; for filtrations it shifts weight spaces; for valuations it sends v to v_xi.

evidence.quote: eta+xi
F_xi^x R_m
v_xi

nodes.label: Total boundary defining function
nodes.summary: The product v of compatible boundary defining functions for all boundary hypersurfaces of a manifold with fibered corners. It determines the QFB vector fields and the conformal passage from QFB to QFC metrics.

evidence.quote: v = product_i x_i be a total boundary defining function

nodes.label: Totally geodesic hyperbolic plane
nodes.summary: A homogeneous complete maximal surface in pseudo-hyperbolic space isometric to the hyperbolic plane and totally geodesic in the ambient space.

evidence.quote: Among homogeneous complete maximal surfaces, we have the totally geodesic hyperbolic planes
For  $n = 0$ , we obtain the Minkowski model of the hyperbolic space

nodes.label: Totally geodesic subvariety of a ball quotient
nodes.summary: A positive-dimensional complex subvariety of S_Γ whose smooth locus is totally geodesic for the canonical Kahler metric. The paper identifies these with Γ-special and Z-special subvarieties.

evidence.quote: smooth locus is totally geodesic

nodes.label: Totally real affine subspace
nodes.summary: An affine real subspace of complex Euclidean space whose tangent spaces contain no nonzero complex line. Complements of such subspaces are a main application of the paper's relative theorem.

evidence.quote: totally real affine subspace

nodes.label: Trace field of a subvariety
nodes.summary: The field generated by traces of elements in Γ intersected with the Γ-monodromy group of W, under the adjoint representation. It controls the Weil restriction describing Z-monodromy.

evidence.quote: trace field of W

nodes.label: Trace map as theta-zero coefficient
nodes.summary: The trace map from the theta module to R extracts the coefficient of the distinguished basis vector theta_0. Compatibility with this trace characterizes the Frobenius algebra structure.

evidence.quote: taking coefficient of θ0

nodes.label: Translation surface
nodes.summary: A translation surface is represented in this paper by a pair (X, omega), where X is a Riemann surface and omega is a holomorphic one-form; the zeros of omega form the marked set used in relative cohomology.

evidence.quote: a point $(X,\omega)$
$\Sigma$ is the set of zeros of $\omega$

nodes.label: Formal properties of transverse spine counts
nodes.summary: For sufficiently general spines transverse to walls, the naive spine counts are deformation invariant, independent of the chosen interior evaluation leg, satisfy a product formula under gluing at finite vertices, and obey convexity/positivity/finiteness properties. These properties drive associativity and finiteness of the mirror algebra.

evidence.quote: deformation invariant among transverse spines
evidence.section: 1.3 Naive counts of spines in Sk(U)

evidence.quote: symmetry property... gluing formula... Convexity
evidence.section: 1.3 Naive counts of spines in Sk(U)

nodes.label: Trivial-determinant surface group representation
nodes.summary: A complex linear surface group representation whose determinant character is trivial. Algebraic Painleve VI solutions are related to MCG-finite representations with this property in the case g=0, n=4, r=2.

evidence.quote: MCG-finite representations with trivial determinant

nodes.label: Tropical geometry
nodes.summary: A piecewise-linear limit framework for algebraic varieties; in the paper it naturally produces the simplex boundary that models the regular part of the Gromov-Hausdorff limit.

evidence.quote: arising naturally from tropical geometry
Tropical and non-Archimedean limits of degenerating families

nodes.label: Tropical simplex boundary base
nodes.summary: The boundary of an (n+1)-dimensional simplex in R^{n+1}, denoted in the paper by the boundary of Delta_lambda^vee, containing an open dense subset that models the smooth real Monge-Ampere part of the Fermat metric limit.

the boundary of a certain (n+1)-dimensional simplex Delta_lambda^vee in R^{n+1} arising naturally from tropical geometry

nodes.label: Trudinger-type integrability estimate
nodes.summary: An exponential integrability bound for a normalized plurisubharmonic potential scaled by E_p(\varphi), with constants depending on n, p, the structural constant \gamma, and the alpha-invariant of the Kähler manifold.

evidence.quote: it also gives estimates of Trudinger type
\int_ {X} \exp \left\{c (n, p, \gamma) \alpha \left(\frac {- \varphi}{E _ {p} (\varphi) ^ {\frac {1}{n + p}}}\right) ^ {\frac {n + p}{n}} \right\} \omega_ {X} ^ {n} \leq 2 C _ {\alpha}

nodes.label: Twisted constant scalar curvature Kähler metric
nodes.summary: A Kähler metric whose scalar curvature is balanced by the trace of a twisting form, satisfying R_ϕ−R̄=tr_ϕβ−β̄ or the χ,t version used in the continuity path. It is the critical point equation for twisted K-energy.

evidence.quote: Rϕ − R = trϕβ − β
twisted cscK metric

nodes.label: Twisted cscK continuity path
nodes.summary: A one-parameter family of scalar-curvature equations interpolating between a solvable J-equation type endpoint and the cscK or twisted cscK equation. The paper proves openness and closedness under estimates to obtain existence.

evidence.quote: t(Rϕ − R) = (1−t)(trϕχ − χ)
continuity path

nodes.label: Stratum of twisted holomorphic or meromorphic differentials
nodes.summary: A moduli stratum of curves carrying holomorphic or meromorphic differentials with prescribed twisting and vanishing or pole data. Such strata are among the applications of the Picard-stack double ramification theory.

evidence.quote: Twisted holomorphic and meromorphic differentials
Closures

nodes.label: Twisted Ding functional
nodes.summary: A variational energy functional on finite-energy Kähler potentials whose critical points solve the twisted Kähler-Einstein equation. Its non-Archimedean analogue defines Ding stability.

evidence.quote: Ding-stability and twisted Kähler-Einstein currents
based on variational arguments

nodes.label: Twisted Dolbeault moduli space
nodes.summary: The twisted Simpson Dolbeault moduli space parametrizing stable Higgs bundles on C of rank r and Euler characteristic chi, with gcd(r,chi)=1. It is the source of the Hitchin fibration.

evidence.quote: The first moduli space \mathcal{M}_{\mathrm{Dol}} parametrizes stable Higgs bundles on C

nodes.label: Twisted double ramification cycle
nodes.summary: A double ramification cycle in which the weighted marked divisor is combined with an auxiliary line bundle on the curve, formulated naturally over the Picard stack. It generalizes the classical cycle on the moduli space of stable curves.

evidence.quote: extends the study of double ramification cycles from the moduli space of stable curves to the Picard stack of curves with line bundles

nodes.label: Twisted Fano polarization
nodes.summary: A polarized smooth projective manifold (X,L) equipped with a semipositive klt current θ such that c1(X,θ)=c1(L). This is the setting of the paper's main Ding-stability criterion.

evidence.quote: The main result of the present paper deals instead with the 'twisted Fano case'
θ a semipositive klt current such that c1(X,θ) = c1(L)

nodes.label: Twisted first Chern class
nodes.summary: The cohomology class c1(X,θ)=c1(X)-[θ]. In the twisted Kähler-Einstein setup it must equal λ c1(L), which determines λ.

nodes.label: Twisted GL_r character variety
nodes.summary: The affine Betti moduli space parametrizing irreducible GL_r local systems on the punctured curve with fixed scalar monodromy around the puncture. Its mixed Hodge weight filtration is the W side of P=W.

evidence.quote: The second moduli space is the (twisted) character variety \mathcal{M}_B.
The Betti moduli space M_B is the (twisted) character variety associated with GL_n(C) and degree d

nodes.label: Twisted K-energy functional
nodes.summary: A deformation of Mabuchi K-energy by adding or interpolating a J-functional term, written in the paper as Kβ=K+Jβ and Kχ,t=tK+(1−t)Jχ. Its critical points solve the corresponding twisted cscK equations.

evidence.quote: Kβ(ϕ)=K(ϕ)+Jβ(ϕ)
Kχ,t=tK+(1−t)Jχ

nodes.label: Twisted log discrepancy
nodes.summary: The log discrepancy Aθ(v) of a divisorial valuation v measured with respect to the klt pair (X,θ). It is a main input in the non-Archimedean Ding functional and valuative stability threshold.

evidence.quote: the log discrepancy Aθ(v) of divisorial valuations v on X with respect to the 'klt pair' (X, θ)

nodes.label: Twisted vector bundle
nodes.summary: A vector bundle associated to covering and representation data, used in the paper's splitting formalism for operators with symmetries.

evidence.quote: Twisted bundles and splittings of operators

nodes.label: Twistor-space map to the O(4) bundle
nodes.summary: A holomorphic map from the twistor space of an ALF-D_k instanton to the total space of the O(4) bundle over CP^1, compatible with the real structure.

the map z commutes with the real structure

nodes.label: Twistor space of a hyperkähler manifold
nodes.summary: The complex manifold encoding the sphere of complex structures associated with a hyperkähler manifold. It is used in the paper for ALF-D_k instantons.

Twistor space of ALF-D_k instantons

nodes.label: Two-class topological condition for K3[2] type

nodes.label: Typical Hodge locus

nodes.label: Unbranched cover of Riemann surfaces
nodes.summary: A holomorphic covering map between Riemann surfaces with no branch points.

nodes.label: Uniform complex Monge-Ampere estimates on crepant resolutions

nodes.label: Extension of uniformization to the limit curve

nodes.label: Unique continuation for harmonic and holomorphic functions
