nodes.label: Unlikely intersection for the period map
aliases: atypical intersection in the period domain
nodes.summary: A dimension-theoretically larger-than-expected intersection between the image or graph of the period map and a subperiod-domain condition. The paper recasts totally geodesic subvarieties as such intersections when Γ is non-arithmetic.

evidence.quote: W is an unlikely intersection
a complete elucidation of the distribution in $S$ of the special subvarieties in terms of typical/atypical intersections

nodes.label: Upper semicontinuity of b-exponents
nodes.summary: Under suitable hypotheses in a mu-constant deformation, the b-exponent associated with a locally constant cycle varies upper-semicontinuously with the deformation parameter. This is a main method for proving the generic formula.

evidence.quote: b-exponent ... depends upper-semicontinuously

nodes.label: Vafa-Witten conjecture for Hilbert schemes
nodes.summary: For the Nakajima metric on Hilb_0^n(C^2), the conjecture predicts that the natural inclusion from the image of compactly supported cohomology into Hodge cohomology is surjective, hence an isomorphism in the relevant degrees. The paper proves this for the Hilbert-scheme case under its QFB analysis.

evidence.quote: their conjecture predicts that the natural inclusion ... must be surjective

nodes.label: Filtration associated to a valuation
nodes.summary: The filtration of the anticanonical section ring attached to a valuation v, given by sections whose valuation is at least a real threshold. It is used to define S_L(v) and to compare valuations with test configurations.

evidence.quote: F_v^x R_m := {s; v(s)>=x}

nodes.label: Valuative criterion of integrability
nodes.summary: A method for checking integrability of singular weights or potentials by inequalities over valuations. The paper includes it as an appendix supporting the use of klt and log-discrepancy data.

evidence.quote: A valuative criterion of integrability

nodes.label: Valuative stability threshold
nodes.summary: A threshold defined valuatively, typically as an infimum of ratios Aθ(v)/S_L(v) over divisorial valuations. The paper uses it to express the greatest twisted Ricci lower bound.

evidence.quote: express the greatest (twisted) Ricci lower bound in terms of a purely algebro-geometric stability threshold
The stability threshold and the greatest Ricci lower bound

nodes.label: Vanishing cycle
nodes.summary: A locally constant homology section of the Milnor fibration whose represented cycle tends to zero as the base parameter tends to zero. Periods along such cycles have the asymptotic expansions used in the proof.

evidence.quote: gamma(t) be a vanishing cycle ... gamma(t) -> 0 as t -> 0

nodes.label: Vanishing cycles construction
nodes.summary: A sheaf-theoretic method using vanishing cycles to transfer cohomological and perversity information through degenerations or critical-locus descriptions. The paper applies earlier vanishing-cycle constructions to the strong perversity statement.

evidence.quote: we apply the vanishing cycles constructions in our earlier work
Perverse filtrations and vanishing cycles

nodes.label: Vertex of a multiplicative eigenvalue polytope
aliases: vertex in the multiplicative eigenvalue problem
nodes.summary: An extremal point of the polytope controlling multiplicative eigenvalue data. The paper's construction starts from the simplest such vertices and then applies strange duality.

evidence.quote: Roughly speaking, we show that the strange duals of the simplest vertices of these polytopes give all possible unitary irreducible rigid local systems
give an inductive mechanism for determining all vertices in the multiplicative eigenvalue problem for  SU(n)

nodes.label: Singular locus of the period image for a VHS
nodes.summary: The preimage under the period map of the singular locus of the analytic period image. A subset of the base is singular for the variation if it is contained in this preimage.

evidence.quote: The singular locus $S^{\textnormal{sing}}_{\mathbb{V}}\subset S^{\textnormal{an}}$ of $S$ for ${\mathbb{V}}$ is the preimage under $\Phi$ of the singular locus of the complex analytic variety $\Phi(S^{\textnormal{an}})$
evidence.section: 1.3 Special subvarieties as (a)typical intersection loci and conjectures

nodes.label: Virasoro constraints for psi-class correlators
nodes.summary: A family of recursive relations that uniquely determines the psi-class correlators. The paper analyzes these recursions combinatorially to obtain large genus asymptotics.

evidence.quote: a family of recursions, widely referred to as Virasoro constraints

nodes.label: Random-walk comparison for Virasoro coefficients
aliases: comparison with random walk jump probabilities
nodes.summary: A proof method comparing coefficients in the Virasoro recursive relations for psi-correlators with jump probabilities of an asymmetric simple random walk.

evidence.quote: a comparison between the coefficients in these relations with the jump probabilities of a certain asymmetric simple random walk

nodes.label: Virtual fundamental class
nodes.summary: The virtual cycle assigned to a moduli space with an obstruction theory, used here as the class pushed forward from the rubber moduli space.

evidence.quote: The rubber moduli space carries a natural virtual fundamental class

nodes.label: Volume-collapsing hyperkähler K3 limit space
nodes.summary: A Gromov-Hausdorff limit in the K3 hyperkähler moduli compactification lying outside the non-collapsing part; these limits arise from hyperkähler metrics whose volumes collapse and whose periods diverge in the period domain.

evidence.quote: volume collapsing Gromov-Hausdorff limits of hyperkähler metrics whose periods diverge to infinity
Any collapsed limit in  $\overline{\mathfrak{M}}\backslash \mathfrak{M}'$

nodes.label: Volume density of a metric cone
nodes.summary: The volume of the unit ball centered at the vertex of a metric cone. The proof compares the volume density at infinity with the density of the metric tangent cone at the singular point of W.

evidence.quote: κ(C)=Vol(B(O,1)) the volume density of C

nodes.label: Wall-crossing transformation from cylinder counts
nodes.summary: The wall-crossing transformation Psi_{x,n} sends a monomial z^v to a generating series over infinitesimal cylinder counts N(V_{x,v,w},alpha) z^alpha z^w. It extends to a field automorphism and determines a scattering function f_x.

evidence.quote: we define Ψx,n(zv)
evidence.section: 1.4 Scattering diagram and comparison with GHKK

evidence.quote: extends to an automorphism
evidence.section: 1.4 Scattering diagram and comparison with GHKK

nodes.label: Weak minimizer of K-energy
nodes.summary: A finite-energy potential ϕ* in E^1 attaining the infimum of K-energy, or of twisted K-energy, over the finite-energy space. The paper studies these minimizers before an Euler-Lagrange equation is a priori available.

evidence.quote: K(ϕ*) = inf
evidence.section: 5. regularity of weak minimizers of K-energy

evidence.quote: minimizer of K-energy over E1

nodes.label: Weakly atypical weakly special Hodge locus
nodes.summary: The union of positive-period-dimensional weakly special subvarieties that are weakly atypical for the variation.

evidence.quote: The weakly atypical weakly special Hodge locus ... is the union of the weakly atypical weakly special subvarieties of $S$ for ${\mathbb{V}}$ of positive period dimension
evidence.section: 4.1 Two notions of typicality and atypicality

nodes.label: Weakly atypical weakly special subvariety
nodes.summary: A weakly special subvariety that is singular for the variation or has excess intersection with the weakly special closure measured by monodromic codimension.

evidence.quote: $Y$ is said to be weakly atypical for ${\mathbb{V}}$ if either $Y$ is singular for ${\mathbb{V}}$ ... or if $\Phi(S^{\textnormal{an}})$ has an excess intersection ... with the weakly special subvariety
evidence.section: 4.1 Two notions of typicality and atypicality

nodes.label: Weakly rational singularities
nodes.summary: A normal complex space has weakly rational singularities when its Grauert-Riemenschneider sheaf is reflexive. The paper proves that all holomorphic forms extend over such spaces.

evidence.quote: we say that a normal space X has weakly rational singularities if the Grauert-Riemenschneider sheaf omega_X^GR is reflexive

nodes.label: Weakly special closure of F^i-type components
nodes.summary: For any class lambda and integer i, the Zariski closure of every component of S^i(lambda) is a weakly special subvariety for the variation.

evidence.quote: For any i ∈ Z and any λ ∈ VC, the Zariski-closure of any of the ... components of Si(λ) is a weakly special subvariety of S for V.
evidence.section: 1.4.1 On the Zariski-closure of the F^i-loci

nodes.label: Weakly special subvariety for a variation of Hodge structure
aliases: weakly special subvariety of S for V
nodes.summary: An irreducible component of the preimage under the period map of a weakly special subvariety of the connected Hodge variety. Equivalently, it is maximal among closed irreducible algebraic subvarieties with the same algebraic monodromy group.

evidence.quote: Any irreducible complex analytic component of ΦS−1(Y), where Y is a ... weakly special subvariety ... is called a ... weakly special subvariety of S for V.
weak Mumford–Tate domain

An irreducible algebraic subvariety $Y\subset S$ is called special, resp. weakly special for ${\mathbb{V}}$ or $\Phi$ if it is an irreducible component of the $\Phi$-preimage of its special closure (resp. its weakly special closure)

nodes.label: Weakly special subvariety of a Hodge variety
nodes.summary: For a Hodge morphism phi:T1 x T2 -> Y and a point t2 in T2, the image phi(T1 x {t2}) is a weakly special subvariety of the connected Hodge variety Y.

evidence.quote: Consider any Hodge morphism φ : T1×T2 → Y ... and any point t2 ∈ T2. Then the image φ(T1 × {t2}) is called a weakly special subvariety of Y.
evidence.section: 3.1.1 Weakly special subvarieties of Hodge varieties

nodes.label: Weakly symplectic split foliation
nodes.summary: The foliation E obtained from a good tangent sheaf splitting, whose summands are either symplectic or paired by a reflexive two-form. Deformations along such foliations are shown to be unobstructed.

evidence.quote: weakly symplectic split foliation
evidence.section: 2 Locally trivial deformations along foliations and resolutions

nodes.label: Wedge product of differential forms
nodes.summary: The alternating product operation on differential forms. It is used to test p-form extension by forming top-degree forms alpha wedge beta and d alpha wedge gamma.

evidence.quote: the holomorphic n-forms alpha wedge beta and d alpha wedge gamma

nodes.label: Weight filtration
nodes.summary: The increasing filtration on a mixed Hodge module whose graded pieces are polarizable Hodge modules of fixed weights. It is essential in the logarithmic part of the argument.

evidence.quote: an additional increasing filtration W_M, called the weight filtration

nodes.label: Weight filtration on the character-variety mixed Hodge structure
nodes.summary: The increasing weight filtration W on the mixed Hodge structure of H^*(M_B,Q). The P=W conjecture identifies even and odd adjacent weight steps with perverse steps for the Hitchin fibration.

evidence.quote: matches the weight filtration of the mixed Hodge structure on \mathcal{M}_B

nodes.label: Weight filtration on the twisted GL_n character variety
nodes.summary: The increasing weight filtration on the mixed Hodge structure of the affine twisted GL_n character variety. In P=W it is compared through the even steps W_{2k}=W_{2k+1}.

evidence.quote: mixed Hodge structure admits a nontrivial weight filtration
W_2k H^m(M_B,Q) = W_2k+1 H^m(M_B,Q)

nodes.label: Weighted asymptotic cone at infinity
nodes.summary: The normal affine algebraic variety obtained as the algebraic limit of weighted rescalings of X'. It is the intermediate cone in the Donaldson-Sun two-step degeneration and is described by leading terms or by the associated graded of the metric degree filtration.

evidence.quote: the weighted asymptotic cone W=lim Y_j is a normal affine algebraic variety

nodes.label: Weighted Dynkin diagram of an sl2-triple
nodes.summary: The Dynkin diagram whose node for a simple root alpha_i is labeled by alpha_i(h), with labels in {0,1,2}. It is the classification invariant used to list magical sl2-triples.

evidence.quote: weighted Dynkin diagram

nodes.label: Weighted Hilbert space with growth weights
nodes.summary: A Hilbert space of functions or sections equipped with polynomial or exponential growth weights. The paper uses such spaces in elliptic estimates used to construct holomorphic functions.

evidence.quote: Weighted Hilbert space

nodes.label: Weighted Hölder space for collapsing cone metrics
nodes.summary: A family of Hölder norms depending on β and on a weight wβ adapted to the collapsing geometry of the approximate cone metrics. These spaces encode the different scales in the circle, divisor, and Tian-Yau regions.

evidence.quote: These are weighted Hölder spaces.
we define a weight on X by wβ
the norm ... is a Cα norm with respect to the metric β−1wβ−1/n gβ

nodes.label: Weighted Hölder space for collapsing hyperkähler gluing
nodes.summary: Weighted Hölder norms on tensors over the glued manifold, with weights adapted to the different collapsing regions and rescaling behavior of the approximate metric.

evidence.quote: define weighted Hölder spaces
consistent with the different collapsing behaviors

nodes.label: Weighted L2 cohomology
nodes.summary: For a complete Riemannian manifold and positive weight w, this is the cohomology of closed differential forms whose w^{-1}-weighted norm is square integrable, modulo exact forms with weighted L2 primitives. The unweighted case is ordinary L2 cohomology.

evidence.quote: weighted L2-cohomology group of degree q is the quotient

nodes.label: Weighted Schauder estimate for collapsing hyperkähler gluing
nodes.summary: An elliptic estimate in the paper's weighted Hölder spaces, used to control solutions of the linearized problem uniformly across the collapsing regions.

evidence.quote: proving the weighted Schauder estimate
weight analysis package

nodes.label: Weighted W2,p estimate for cscK potentials
nodes.summary: For cscK solutions, the Laplacian n+Delta phi satisfies weighted Lp estimates of the form \int e^{-alpha(p)F}(n+Delta phi)^p <= C; if F is bounded this yields ordinary W2,p control of the potential.

evidence.quote: \int_{M}e^{-\alpha(p)F}(n+\Delta\varphi)^{p}\leq C
evidence.section: 3. The volume ratio and W2,p bound on Kähler potential

nodes.label: Weil numerical b-class
nodes.summary: A compatible family of numerical cycle classes on all birational models over X, with compatibility under pushforward. It generalizes Shokurov b-divisors to higher-codimension numerical cycles.

evidence.quote: Weil and Cartier  $b$  -classes
space of  $b$ -classes of higher codimension cycles

nodes.label: Weil restriction of an algebraic group
nodes.summary: The construction sending an algebraic group over a number field K to a Q-algebraic group whose real points decompose over the real embeddings of K. The paper denotes the Weil restriction of G from K to Q by widehat G.

evidence.quote: Weil restriction from K to Q

nodes.label: Yano generating function for b-exponents
nodes.summary: A fractional-power polynomial R built from the characteristic sequence via integers e_i, r_i, R_i, r'_i, and R'_i. Its exponents are Yano's proposed candidates for the generic b-exponents of a plane branch.

evidence.quote: Yano defines the following polynomial with fractional powers in t

nodes.label: Generic b-exponent formula for plane branches
nodes.summary: For generic curves in a suitable mu-constant deformation of an irreducible plane curve singularity, the generating series sum t^{alpha_i} of all mu b-exponents equals Yano's generating function R from the characteristic sequence.

evidence.quote: the b-exponents alpha_1,...,alpha_mu are given by the generating function R

nodes.label: Yau-Tian-Donaldson principle
nodes.summary: The principle relating existence of canonical Kähler metrics in a fixed Kähler class to an algebro-geometric stability condition. In this paper the relevant version is formulated for twisted Kähler-Einstein currents and Ding stability.

evidence.quote: relate the existence of a canonical metric in a given Kähler cohomology class to an algebroid-geometric condition of stability
Yau-Tian-Donaldson conjecture

nodes.label: Z-Ax-Schanuel for integral variations of Hodge structure
nodes.summary: The functional transcendence statement for the period map of the constructed integral VHS: atypically large intersections with the graph of the period map force containment in a strict special subvariety unless the projection is zero-dimensional.

evidence.quote: the Z-Ax-Schanuel
evidence.section: 5.4. Proof of the non-arithmetic Ax-Schanuel conjecture

nodes.label: Z-monodromy of a subvariety
nodes.summary: The algebraic monodromy of W with respect to the constructed integral VHS widehat V. It is a connected Q-subgroup of the Weil restriction widehat G.

evidence.quote: monodromy of W with respect to the Z-VHS

nodes.label: Z-special subvariety for the ball quotient VHS
nodes.summary: An irreducible component of the preimage under the period map ψ of the quotient of a Mumford-Tate subdomain for the constructed Z-VHS on S_Γ. The paper proves these coincide with Γ-special and totally geodesic subvarieties.

nodes.label: Zariski closure of the Apollonian group
nodes.summary: The larger algebraic group containing the Apollonian group, described here as an orthogonal group preserving the Descartes equation. The reciprocity obstructions do not occur at this Zariski-closed level.

evidence.quote: larger Zariski closure

nodes.label: Zariski tangent cone of a sheaf singularity
nodes.summary: The algebraic tangent cone at the origin of the singular locus of the underlying sheaf. It is included in the singular set of analytic tangent-cone convergence.
